revisionHistory.description: Initial Release
revisionHistory.author: SRA System

introduction.purpose: This Functional Requirements Specification (FRS) document provides the detailed description of functionalities required for the first version of the **cctns** (Crime and Criminal Tracking Network and Systems), a Mission Mode Project under the E-Governance initiative of the Government of India, Ministry of Home Affairs. The key guiding principle behind the functional design of CCTNS V1.0 is to focus on critical functionality that provides value to police personnel at the cutting edge, which in turn can improve outcomes in the areas of Investigation of Crime and Detection of Criminals.
introduction.documentConventions: This document follows the IEEE 830-1998 standard for Software Requirements Specifications. Requirements are organized by functional modules (Registration, Investigation, Prosecution, Search, Citizen Interface, Navigation, and Configuration) and non-functional quality attributes. Each module's detailed functional requirements are provided as annexures A1 through A7. Non-functional requirements are presented as numbered requirements within each quality attribute category. The functional architecture recommendations follow Service Oriented Architecture (SOA) principles with a 3-C (Core-Configuration-Customization) guiding framework.
introduction.intendedAudience: This document is intended for the development teams implementing the CCTNS solution for state-level deployment across India, the Ministry of Home Affairs project stakeholders overseeing the E-Governance Mission Mode Project, police department administrators and IT officers responsible for state-level customization and configuration, quality assurance engineers validating functional and non-functional compliance, and system architects designing the centralized deployment infrastructure.
introduction.productScope: The **cctns** system provides a comprehensive web-based platform for crime and criminal tracking across Indian police stations. It encompasses seven core functional modules: Registration (complaint intake and citizen-police interface), Investigation (post-registration case management and evidence tracking), Prosecution (court interfacing and judicial interaction recording), Search (basic and advanced case and criminal queries with customizable results), Citizen Interface (information exchange between citizens and police personnel), Navigation (role-based landing pages with alerts and task management), and Configuration (state-specific application parameter management). The system supports multilingual interfaces, offline operation capabilities, and centralized deployment with state-level customization. Non-functional requirements extensively cover usability (ISO 9241 compliance), accessibility (WCAG 1.0), security (SSL, VPN, XSS prevention, SQL injection prevention), performance (5-8 second simple search, 10-15 second advanced search), scalability across varying police station sizes, and comprehensive audit trail management.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO 9241 Ergonomics of human-system interaction — Parts 12, 14-17, 20, 110, 171, 303.
WCAG 1.0 Web Content Accessibility Guidelines.
ISO 14915-1 to ISO 14915-3 Software ergonomics for multimedia user interfaces.
www.usability.gov guidelines for user interface design.

overallDescription.productPerspective:
The **cctns** system is designed as a centralized, web-based application deployed at the state level within India's E-Governance framework. It replaces the existing paper-based and fragmented crime record management processes across police stations nationwide. The system is architected around SOA principles with a functional architecture composed of four major components: Core Services, Support Layer, Security and Access Control, and a Customization Layer.

The 3-C architecture (Core-Configuration-Customization) enables a standard core application to be deployed across states with state-specific configurations and customizations plugged in as extensions. The core services and support layer are deployed as standard components with configuration changes, while the customization layer overrides and extends core services based on specific state requirements. This architecture supports India's diverse administrative landscape where each state may have unique legal procedures, language requirements, and operational workflows.

The system operates within a 3-tier datacenter architecture with n-tier application design separating presentation logic from business logic and data-access logic. It interfaces with the broader criminal justice ecosystem including courts (through the Prosecution module), citizen services (through the Citizen Interface), and potentially external databases for criminal records and case cross-referencing.
productFunctions: Registration Module: Acts as the primary interface between police and citizens for complaint registration. Citizens register complaints, and based on evidence, facts, and investigation, police determine the appropriate course of action. This module eases the approach, interaction, and information exchange between police personnel and complainants.

Investigation Module: Facilitates the crime investigation process following complaint registration. Introduces operational efficiencies by automating most tasks that occur after initial entries are made during Registration, streamlining evidence management, suspect tracking, and case documentation workflows.

Prosecution Module: Aids the interfacing between police and courts during case prosecution. Provides a platform for recording entries of court interactions, managing the designated constable's coordination with the judicial system, and tracking prosecution milestones and outcomes.

Search Module: Provides police personnel with basic and advanced search capabilities across cases, persons, crime types, modus operandi, and property. Supports customizable results views by criminal/accused or by case. Enables different types of reporting queries including monthly reporting and RTI (Right to Information) related queries.

Citizen Interface Module: Acts as a conduit for information exchange between citizens and police units. Citizens use it to obtain information or acknowledgements from police, while police respond with minimal turnaround time. Reduces paperwork and improves overall productivity for both citizens and police personnel.

Navigation Module: Provides role-based landing pages with information such as assigned cases, alerts, and pending tasks. Helps police personnel plan better and execute with greater efficiency through personalized dashboard views and task prioritization.

Configuration Module: Maintains application configuration according to state-specific requirements and keeps data elements and rules up to date. Manages act and sections, state-specific data, castes, tribes, property information, and other configurable data through create, update, and delete operations.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: Investigating Officers are the primary operational users at the cutting edge of police work. They initiate and manage crime investigations, handle evidence documentation, interact with suspects and witnesses, and coordinate with the prosecution. They require efficient case management tools, quick search capabilities for modus operandi matching, and seamless transition between Registration and Investigation modules. The system is fundamentally designed to deliver value to this user class.

Records room staff manage the documentary aspects of police station operations. They handle case file organization, maintain registers, and support the administrative workflow of crime recording. They require bulk data entry capabilities, efficient filing and retrieval systems, and report generation tools for periodic statutory requirements.

Station-level administrators configure the system for their station's specific needs, manage user access within the station, and oversee the operational use of all modules. They require administrative dashboards showing station-level metrics, pending tasks, and system alerts.

Citizens interact with the system primarily through the Citizen Interface module to register complaints, track complaint status, obtain acknowledgements, and access information from police. They require an intuitive, accessible interface that works across browsers with minimal client-side requirements. The system must support users with special needs and comply with accessibility standards.

Help desk users manage the support module, viewing reports on submitted defects and enhancement requests categorized by category, status, and age. They require reporting tools and the ability to track and update the status of support tickets.

Super users manage security attributes including access rights, security levels, privileges, and password management for groups and individual users. They configure the system at the state level, manage user profiles, allocate users to groups, and oversee the audit trail. They are the only users authorized to modify security configurations.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment:
The **cctns** system is deployed as a centralized web application within a **3-tier datacenter architecture**. The application follows an **n-tier architecture** with clear separation of presentation logic, business logic, and data-access logic. The system is designed for browser-based access with minimal client-side requirements, supporting multiple browsers without requiring specialized client software.

The system must operate effectively across India's diverse infrastructure conditions, including police stations connected on low-bandwidth networks. It supports offline mode for critical functionality, ensuring operational data is not lost during equipment or communication network failures. The deployment is centralized at the state level with configuration and customization capabilities for state-specific extensions.

The technical stack follows open standards with SOA-based modular design, supporting HTTPS communication with SSL encryption, VPN connections, AJAX-based user experience enhancements, hierarchical caching, and asynchronous HTTP socket capabilities for scalability. Static content (documents, images) is hosted on the web server for performance optimization.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The system must be implemented using Service Oriented Architecture (SOA) with a modular design following the 3-C (Core-Configuration-Customization) framework.
The system must be developed on Open Standards and designed for centralized deployment and maintenance at the state level.
The system must support multilingual interface to accommodate India's diverse linguistic requirements across states.
The system must work in offline mode with critical functionality to ensure operational continuity during network outages.
The system must be designed for satisfactory performance even on low-bandwidth connections at police stations.
The system must use HTTPS as the communication protocol with SSL encryption for all data in transit.
The system must support selective encryption of stored data for protecting sensitive case information.
The system must prevent cross-site scripting (XSS), SQL injection, and other common web security vulnerabilities through input validation, parameterized queries, and data sanitization.
The system must not allow hard delete of any records; only soft deletion by tagging rows for deletion is permitted.
The system must be designed for access through browser-based clients with minimal client device requirements.
The system must support multiple types of communication services for remote access.
The system must be extensible to provide access through PDAs and mobile data terminals.
The system must adopt standardized formats and common metadata elements.
The system must be built on a common User Access and Authentication Service to ensure Single Sign-On for end users.
Search results must be fetched from the database in configurable batches of 10 or 20 records maximum, with paginated display.
Database indexes must be applied on key columns used for searching to ensure performance requirements are met.
userDocumentation: The system shall provide detailed context-sensitive help material for all possible actions and scenarios on all user interfaces in the application, accessible in both offline and online modes.
User guides shall be provided for each user role covering module-specific operations, search techniques, and administrative functions.
The system shall provide a site map showing the application structure in an overview form for navigation assistance.
assumptionsAndDependencies: The system depends on state-level datacenter infrastructure for centralized deployment, including adequate server capacity, network connectivity, and disaster recovery capabilities.
Police stations have at minimum low-bandwidth network connectivity to the state-level deployment, with the system designed to operate satisfactorily under such constraints.
The existing paper-based processes and data can be migrated or entered into the system to establish the initial case and criminal database.
States will provide their specific requirements for act and sections, local data elements, and customization needs through the Configuration module.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces:
The **cctns** system provides a comprehensive web-based user interface designed for maximum usability and accessibility compliance. The interface follows **ISO 9241** ergonomic standards across multiple parts (12, 14-17, 20, 110, 171, 303) and adheres to **WCAG 1.0** content accessibility guidelines.

Key interface characteristics include: role-based landing pages with personalized dashboards showing assigned cases, alerts, and pending tasks; customizable user interfaces where users can modify displayed columns, layout, appearance, menu contents, function keys, colors, fonts, and font sizes saved in user profiles; persistent data entry defaults including user-definable values, previous-item values, and context-derived values (date, file reference, user identifier); simultaneous display of multiple entities (cases, suspects); and input device independence allowing activation via keyboard and various input devices.

The interface design follows 47 detailed usability principles covering: text equivalents for non-text media, self-descriptive navigation with clear position indicators, alternative access paths for different navigation strategies, minimized navigation effort, avoidance of unnecessary windows and splash screens, minimized vertical scrolling with no horizontal scrolling, descriptive link labels avoiding generic terms, printer-friendly document versions, appropriate use of white space and color (never as the only means of coding information), consistent page layout with consistently placed titles and navigation components, compliance with human perception principles, and individualization and user adaptation capabilities including user-specified profiles.

Error handling follows strict guidelines: all error messages must be meaningful with explanatory text and suggested corrective actions, displayed in the user's interface language. The interface must support users with special needs through compatibility with specialist software and appropriate interface guidelines.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The system is designed for browser-based access and does not impose direct hardware interface requirements beyond standard computing devices with web browser capability. The system must be extensible to support access through PDAs and mobile data terminals. The 3-tier datacenter architecture requires standard server hardware for web servers, application servers, and database servers, with database servers hosting static content separately for performance optimization.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces:
The system interfaces with court information systems through the Prosecution module for recording judicial interactions and tracking case prosecution status. The Configuration module interfaces with state-specific legal databases for act and section definitions. The Support module interfaces with email and SMS notification services for sending alerts to users when actions are taken on support tickets. The system uses a common User Access and Authentication Service enabling Single Sign-On across the application. The system is designed for SOA-based integration supporting standardized data formats and common metadata elements.

The system leverages AJAX-based technology for enhanced user experience, asynchronous HTTP socket capabilities for scalability, and hierarchical caching for frequently accessed search results. Database operations utilize parameterized queries and indexed key columns for search performance optimization.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The system communicates via **HTTPS** protocol using HTTP over encrypted SSL connections. It supports **secure VPN connections** for remote access to the application. Two-way digital signatures ensure secure data transmission. The system supports multiple types of communication services for remote access from diverse police locations across India. All data transmission is encrypted to protect sensitive criminal and case information during transit across potentially insecure network segments connecting remote police stations to state-level datacenters.

systemFeatures.name: Complaint Registration and Case Intake
systemFeatures.description: This feature implements the Registration module, serving as the primary interface between citizens and police for complaint registration. It captures initial complaint information, assigns case identifiers, and initiates the case lifecycle within the CCTNS system. The module is designed to streamline the traditionally paper-heavy process of First Information Report (FIR) registration and allied complaint documentation at police stations across India. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Citizen files a complaint at the police station. Response: The system presents the Registration form, captures complaint details (complainant information, incident details, evidence description), assigns a unique case identifier, and generates an acknowledgement for the citizen.

Stimulus: Police officer reviews registered complaint and determines FIR eligibility. Response: The system updates the case status, links the complaint to the appropriate act and sections from the configured legal database, and forwards the case to the Investigation module.

Stimulus: Citizen requests status update on a previously registered complaint through Citizen Interface. Response: The system retrieves the current case status and displays it through the Citizen Interface module with relevant details.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide a web-based registration form for capturing citizen complaints with all required data fields including complainant information, incident details, location, and evidence description.
The system shall assign a unique case identifier to each registered complaint following the state-specific numbering scheme configured in the Configuration module.
The system shall link registered complaints to the appropriate act and sections from the state-specific legal database.
The system shall generate printed and electronic acknowledgements for citizens upon successful complaint registration.
The system shall support data entry with persistent defaults including user-definable values, previous-item values, and context-derived values to minimize repetitive input.
The system shall automatically create an audit trail entry for each registration action including the user identity, timestamp, and action details.
The system shall support offline registration capability for critical functionality when network connectivity is unavailable, with automatic synchronization upon connectivity restoration.

systemFeatures.name: Crime Investigation Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature implements the Investigation module, facilitating the complete crime investigation process from initial case assignment through evidence management to case closure. It automates most tasks that follow the initial Registration entries, introducing operational efficiencies for Investigating Officers working at the cutting edge of police operations. The module is the primary value delivery mechanism for the IO user class. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Case is forwarded from Registration to Investigation and assigned to an IO. Response: The system updates the IO's navigation dashboard with the new case assignment, displays pending investigation tasks, and makes all registration data available in the investigation context.

Stimulus: IO records investigation findings including witness statements and evidence details. Response: The system captures the investigation entries, updates the case status, maintains the investigation timeline, and creates audit trail records.

Stimulus: IO performs a search for similar modus operandi across existing cases. Response: The system executes the search across the criminal database and returns matching cases organized by relevance for cross-referencing.

Stimulus: Investigation is completed and case is ready for prosecution. Response: The system compiles the investigation findings, generates the chargesheet documentation, and forwards the case to the Prosecution module.
functionalRequirements: The system shall support case assignment to Investigating Officers with automatic notification through the Navigation module dashboard.
The system shall provide comprehensive investigation data entry forms for recording witness statements, evidence details, suspect information, and investigation progress.
The system shall maintain a chronological investigation timeline for each case with all entries timestamped and attributed to the entering officer.
The system shall support linking related cases across police stations for cross-referencing investigation data.
The system shall automate the generation of investigation-related statutory documents and reports.
The system shall support offline investigation data entry with synchronization upon network restoration.
The system shall create an unalterable audit trail for all investigation actions including data creation, reading, updates, and deletions.

systemFeatures.name: Court Prosecution Interface
systemFeatures.description: This feature implements the Prosecution module, providing the interface between police and the judicial system during case prosecution. A designated constable from each police station continuously interfaces with courts, and this module digitizes and streamlines that critical interaction, ensuring timely tracking of court dates, hearing outcomes, and prosecution milestones. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Case is forwarded from Investigation to Prosecution after chargesheet filing. Response: The system creates a prosecution record, links it to the investigation data, and initiates the court interaction tracking workflow.

Stimulus: Designated constable records the outcome of a court hearing. Response: The system captures the hearing date, outcome, next hearing date, and any court directives, updating the case prosecution timeline.
Stimulus: Court disposes of the case with a verdict. Response: The system records the final disposition, updates the case status to closed, and archives the complete prosecution record.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide a platform for recording all entries of court interactions including hearing dates, outcomes, judge directives, and next hearing schedules.
The system shall track prosecution milestones from chargesheet filing through final court disposition.
The system shall generate alerts for upcoming court dates through the Navigation module dashboards.
The system shall maintain links between prosecution records and their originating investigation and registration data.
The system shall support generating prosecution-related statutory reports and court submission documents.
The system shall create unalterable audit trail entries for all prosecution-related actions.

systemFeatures.name: Advanced Search and Reporting Engine
systemFeatures.description: This feature implements the Search module, providing police personnel with powerful basic and advanced search capabilities across the entire CCTNS database. The search engine must deliver results within strict performance thresholds (5-8 seconds for simple search, 10-15 seconds for advanced search) regardless of database size. It supports customizable result views and multiple query types for operational and statutory reporting. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Police officer executes a basic search by person name. Response: The system returns matching records within 5-8 seconds in a paginated result list with configurable batch sizes of 10 or 20 records.

Stimulus: Police officer executes an advanced search with multiple criteria (crime type, modus operandi, location, date range). Response: The system returns matching results within 10-15 seconds, with results viewable by criminal/accused or by case.

Stimulus: Police officer requests a monthly crime statistics report. Response: The system generates the configured report pulling data from the case database and presenting it in the required statutory format.

Stimulus: Police officer attempts to search for a case they do not have access rights to. Response: The system excludes the restricted case from search results without indicating its existence (configurable security level).
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide basic search functionality returning results within 5-8 seconds regardless of database size or number of cases.
The system shall provide advanced search with multiple criteria returning results within 10-15 seconds regardless of database size.
The system shall support search by person, crime type, modus operandi, property, case number, and other configurable criteria.
The system shall provide customizable result views allowing users to organize results by criminal/accused or by case.
The system shall support different types of reporting queries including monthly statistical reports and RTI-related queries.
The system shall fetch search results from the database in configurable batches of 10 or 20 records maximum with paginated display.
The system shall fetch only display-required fields initially, retrieving additional details only when the user selects a specific record for detailed viewing.
The system shall never include in search results any record which the requesting user does not have the right to access.
The system shall leverage hierarchical caching for frequently used search queries and database indexes on key search columns.
The system shall retrieve and display cases accessed within the previous 2 months within 5-8 seconds, and older cases within 20 seconds.

systemFeatures.name: Citizen Interface and Communication Portal
systemFeatures.description: This feature implements the Citizen Interface module, acting as the digital conduit between citizens and police units for information exchange, complaint tracking, and acknowledgement retrieval. It reduces paperwork, improves response turnaround time, and enhances transparency in police-citizen interactions. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Citizen accesses the portal to check the status of their registered complaint. Response: The system authenticates the citizen, retrieves the case status, and displays relevant information without exposing restricted investigation details.
Stimulus: Citizen submits a request for information. Response: The system records the request, routes it to the appropriate police unit, and provides the citizen with a tracking reference.
Stimulus: Police unit responds to a citizen request. Response: The system delivers the response to the citizen and updates the interaction record with minimal turnaround time.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide a citizen-facing interface for complaint status tracking using the case reference number provided at registration.
The system shall enable citizens to receive acknowledgements and information from police through the digital interface.
The system shall support public access to a designated subset of data and functionality appropriate for citizen interaction.
The system shall maintain interaction records between citizens and police for audit and service quality tracking.
The system shall comply with all accessibility requirements including WCAG 1.0 and ISO 9241-20 for ICT accessibility.
The system shall support text equivalents for all non-text media objects for accessibility compliance.

systemFeatures.name: Role-Based Navigation and Dashboard
systemFeatures.description: This feature implements the Navigation module, providing personalized landing pages for each user role. Dashboards display assigned cases, active alerts, pending tasks, and priority items, helping police personnel plan their activities and execute with greater efficiency. It serves as the central hub from which users access all other system modules. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Investigating Officer logs into the system. Response: The Navigation module displays the IO's personalized dashboard showing newly assigned cases, pending investigation tasks, upcoming court dates, and system alerts.
Stimulus: Administrator logs in. Response: The system displays an administrative dashboard with station-level metrics, pending approvals, user management notifications, and system health indicators.
Stimulus: New case is assigned to an IO. Response: The Navigation module updates the IO's dashboard in real-time with the new assignment alert.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide role-based landing pages customized for each user class (IO, Records Staff, Administrator, Help Desk).
The system shall display assigned cases, alerts, and pending tasks on user dashboards.
The system shall support individualization and user adaptation, allowing users to customize dashboard content and navigation.
The system shall maintain user profiles storing customization preferences including display columns, layout, appearance, menu contents, and function key assignments.
The system shall make the current user profile evident and allow users to view, modify, and delete their profiles on demand.
The system shall provide consistent navigation components placed in consistent locations across all pages.
The system shall include a link to the home page or landmark page on every page and provide a site map showing the application structure.

systemFeatures.name: Comprehensive Audit Trail System
systemFeatures.description: This feature implements the Audit module, maintaining an unalterable record of all actions taken by users or system triggers on critical entities within the CCTNS system. The audit trail is legally significant as it supports data admissibility and assists in data recovery. It captures all CRUD operations on cases, suspects, and property, along with user identity, timestamps, and administrative parameter changes. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Any user performs a create, read, update, or delete action on a critical entity (case, suspect, property). Response: The system automatically captures the action type, user identity, date and time, and affected entity details in the unalterable audit trail.

Stimulus: User attempts to access a case they do not have rights to access. Response: The system blocks the access, captures the violation in the audit trail, and enforces the configured response level (display metadata, indicate existence only, or completely hide).

Stimulus: External auditor requests the audit trail for a specific case. Response: The system exports the complete audit trail for the specified case without affecting the stored audit trail, providing all related data in a format accessible to personnel unfamiliar with the system.
functionalRequirements: The system shall maintain an unalterable audit trail that cannot be modified or deleted by any user, capturing all CRUD actions on critical entities.
The system shall track events automatically without manual intervention once audit trail functionality is activated.
The system shall maintain the audit trail for at least the life of the case to which it refers.
The system shall make audit trail data available for inspection on request, allowing specific events to be identified by authorized external personnel with little or no system familiarity.
The system shall support exporting audit trails for specified cases without affecting the stored audit trail.
The system shall capture and store access control violations including unauthorized access attempts.
The system shall provide audit reports organized by case, by user, and in chronological sequence.
The system shall support audit reports organized by workstation and network address where technically appropriate.
The system shall log all activities involving cases and related documents or data to ensure legal admissibility and assist in data recovery.

systemFeatures.name: Access Control and Security Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature implements the Access module and the comprehensive security framework of the CCTNS system. It enforces role-based access control, group-based permissions, and configurable security levels for sensitive case data. The system supports multiple authentication tiers, encryption, and protection against common web vulnerabilities. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Super user creates a new user profile and assigns group membership. Response: The system creates the user profile, assigns the specified groups, and sets initial access rights based on group permissions.

Stimulus: User searches for cases. Response: The system filters results based on user access rights, completely excluding cases the user cannot access (at the most stringent security level, not even indicating their existence or including them in result counts).
Stimulus: Administrator attempts to change security attributes for a user group. Response: The system verifies the requesting user has super-user privileges before allowing the modification.
functionalRequirements: The system shall support limiting access to cases to specified users or user groups.
The system shall provide role-based control for all functionality within the system.
The system shall allow users to be members of more than one group simultaneously.
The system shall restrict user profile creation and group allocation to admin-users only.
The system shall allow users to stipulate which other users or groups can access their cases.
The system shall restrict changes to security attributes (access rights, security levels, privileges, password management) to super-users only.

The system shall provide configurable responses when unauthorized users attempt to access restricted cases: display title and metadata only, indicate case existence without details, or completely hide the case including from search result counts.
The system shall support multi-tier authentication where required.
The system shall support SSL encrypted connections and secure VPN connections.
The system shall use HTTPS with SSL encryption for all communication.
The system shall support selective encryption of stored data.
The system shall ensure secure data transmission using SSL and 2-way digital signatures.
The system shall prevent cross-site scripting, validate and encode incoming data, prevent SQL injection using parameterized queries, and sanitize all user inputs.
The system shall validate data at both client and server sides.
The system shall enforce soft deletion only — no hard delete operations are permitted on any records.

systemFeatures.name: Application Configuration and State Customization
systemFeatures.description: This feature implements the Configuration module, enabling the CCTNS application to be customized according to each state's specific requirements. It manages the master data elements and business rules that differ across India's diverse state jurisdictions, including act and sections, state-specific data elements, castes, tribes, and property classification systems. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: State administrator adds a new act and section to the legal database. Response: The system creates the new act/section entry and makes it available in all Registration and Investigation forms for the state.

Stimulus: State administrator updates a property classification category. Response: The system updates the configuration, and all subsequent forms and search criteria reflect the updated classification.
Stimulus: Administrator modifies state-specific data rules. Response: The system validates the changes against core system constraints and applies the updates to the state's customization layer.
functionalRequirements: The system shall support create, update, and delete operations on configurable data elements including act and sections, state-specific data, castes, tribes, and property information.
The system shall maintain the customization layer separately from core services, enabling state-specific extensions without modifying the core application.
The system shall validate all configuration changes against core system integrity constraints.
The system shall apply configuration changes to all relevant modules (Registration, Investigation, Prosecution, Search) upon update.
The system shall maintain an audit trail of all configuration modifications.

systemFeatures.name: Help and Support System
systemFeatures.description: This feature implements the Help and Support modules, providing context-sensitive assistance and defect/enhancement tracking capabilities for all system users. The Help module ensures users can access relevant guidance for any action or scenario, while the Support module provides a formal mechanism for reporting issues and tracking their resolution. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User triggers context-sensitive help on any user interface screen. Response: The system displays detailed help material specific to the current screen actions and scenarios, available in both online and offline modes.
Stimulus: User logs a defect through the Support interface. Response: The system records the defect, assigns a tracking identifier, and enables the user to monitor resolution progress.
Stimulus: Help desk user reviews submitted defects. Response: The system displays reports on submitted defects and enhancement requests categorized by category, status, and age.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide detailed context-sensitive help material for all possible actions and scenarios on all user interfaces.
The system shall make help accessible in both offline and online modes.
The system shall provide a support interface for users to log defects or enhancement requests and track their resolution.
The system shall send alerts via email or SMS when actions are taken on submitted support tickets, if the user opts in.
The system shall enable help desk users to view reports on support tickets organized by category, status, and age.
The system shall provide support access both within the application and externally through a browser interface.

performanceRequirements: The system shall perform simple searches within 5-8 seconds and advanced searches with multiple criteria within 10-15 seconds, regardless of storage capacity or number of cases in the system. In this context, performing a search means returning a result list; it does not include retrieving the full records themselves.

The system shall retrieve and display cases accessed within the previous 2 months within 5-8 seconds, enabling rapid retrieval of frequently-used cases where frequency of use correlates with recent use.
The system shall retrieve and display cases not accessed within the previous 2 months within 20 seconds, accommodating cases stored on slower media than more active records.
The system shall provide adequate response times for commonly performed functions under both standard and peak conditions.

The system shall leverage caching (hierarchical cache for frequent search results), AJAX-based client-side rendering, asynchronous HTTP socket capabilities, and database indexing on key search columns to meet performance requirements.
safetyRequirements: The system shall ensure that operational data is not lost in case of any failure of equipment or communication network, supporting offline mode for critical functionality with automatic synchronization upon connectivity restoration.
In the event of any software or hardware failure, it must be possible to restore the system with inline synchronization within the configured maximum restoration time.
securityRequirements: The system shall provide role-based access control with configurable security levels that can completely hide restricted cases from unauthorized users including their existence in search result counts.
The system shall support multi-tier authentication, SSL encrypted connections, secure VPN connections, and HTTPS communication with SSL encryption.
The system shall support selective encryption of stored data and secure data transmission using SSL and 2-way digital signatures.

The system shall prevent cross-site scripting (XSS), SQL injection, and other web security vulnerabilities through input validation, input encoding, parameterized queries, and user input sanitization.
The system shall validate data at both client and server sides, enforce soft deletion only (no hard delete permitted), and maintain comprehensive unalterable audit trails.
The system shall restrict security attribute modifications (access rights, security levels, privileges, password management) to super-users only.
The system shall log all access control violations including unauthorized access attempts in the audit trail.
softwareQualityAttributes: Usability: The system user interfaces shall comply with ISO 9241 standards across multiple parts and follow www.usability.gov guidelines. The interface must employ a single consistent set of user interface rules, support customization of displayed columns, layout, colors, fonts, and navigation components, and be suitable for users with special needs through compliance with WCAG 1.0 and ISO 9241-20 accessibility standards.

Availability: The system must be available during defined operational hours with planned downtime not exceeding the configured maximum per rolling three-month period. Unplanned downtime must not exceed the configured threshold, and system restoration must be possible within the defined maximum restoration time.

Scalability: The system must be scalable and must not have any features that would preclude use in small or large police stations with varying numbers of cases handled. The architecture must support deployment across India's diverse police station landscape.

Maintainability: The SOA-based modular design with the 3-C framework ensures that the system can be maintained through configuration and customization without modifying core services. The n-tier architecture separating presentation, business, and data-access logic supports independent maintenance of each layer.

Portability: The system must run on multiple browsers with minimal client device requirements. It must be designed for centralized deployment across state-level datacenters while supporting diverse infrastructure conditions including low-bandwidth connections.

Accessibility: The system must comply with WCAG 1.0 for content accessibility, ISO 9241-20 for ICT accessibility, and ISO 9241-171 for software accessibility. All non-text media objects must have text equivalents. Text must be resizable by users, and user interfaces must be designed for input device independence.
businessRules: All case data modifications must be tracked through unalterable audit trails maintaining legal admissibility of electronic records.
No hard deletion of records is permitted; all deletions must be soft deletions through row tagging.
The system must follow the 3-C (Core-Configuration-Customization) architectural principle, ensuring that state-specific changes do not modify the core application.

otherRequirements: The system shall support multilingual interfaces to accommodate India's diverse linguistic requirements across states.
The system shall adopt standardized data formats and common metadata elements for interoperability.
The system shall provide public access to a designated subset of data and functionality for citizen transparency.

glossary.definition: Crime and Criminal Tracking Network and Systems, the national E-Governance Mission Mode Project for digitizing police operations across India.
Mission Mode Project, a category of projects within India's E-Governance framework with defined scope and timelines.
Functional Requirements Specification, the document type describing detailed system functionality.
Investigating Officer, the police officer assigned to investigate a registered criminal case.
First Information Report, the formal document recording the initial report of a cognizable offense.
Right to Information, an Indian law providing citizens the right to request information from public authorities.
Service Oriented Architecture, the architectural pattern used for the CCTNS system design.
Core-Configuration-Customization, the framework enabling standardized core deployment with state-specific extensions.
Secure Sockets Layer, the encryption protocol used for securing data in transit.
Virtual Private Network, used for secure remote access to the CCTNS system.
Web Content Accessibility Guidelines, international accessibility standards for web content.
Asynchronous JavaScript and XML, technology used for enhanced client-side user experience.
Cross-Site Scripting, a web security vulnerability that the system must prevent.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This flowchart illustrates the complete case lifecycle through the CCTNS system from citizen complaint registration through investigation to prosecution and court disposition. Decision nodes determine case routing based on investigation outcomes and prosecution decisions.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    CitizenComplaint("Citizen Files Complaint") --> RegistrationForm["Registration Module: Capture Details"]
    RegistrationForm --> AssignCaseID["Assign Unique Case Identifier"]
    AssignCaseID --> LinkActSections["Link to Act and Sections"]
    LinkActSections --> GenerateAck["Generate Citizen Acknowledgement"]
    GenerateAck --> ReviewComplaint{"FIR Eligible?"}
    ReviewComplaint -->|No| NCReport["Record as Non-Cognizable Report"]
    ReviewComplaint -->|Yes| CreateFIR["Create First Information Report"]
    CreateFIR --> AssignIO["Assign to Investigating Officer"]
    AssignIO --> UpdateDashboard["Update IO Navigation Dashboard"]
    UpdateDashboard --> Investigation["Investigation Module: Record Findings"]
    Investigation --> CollectEvidence["Document Evidence and Witnesses"]
    CollectEvidence --> SearchMO["Search Modus Operandi Database"]
    SearchMO --> InvestigationComplete{"Investigation Complete?"}
    InvestigationComplete -->|No| Investigation
    InvestigationComplete -->|Yes| FileChargesheet{"File Chargesheet?"}
    FileChargesheet -->|No| ClosureReport["File Closure Report"]
    FileChargesheet -->|Yes| ProsecutionModule["Prosecution Module: Track Court Hearings"]
    ProsecutionModule --> RecordHearing["Record Court Hearing Outcome"]
    RecordHearing --> CaseDisposed{"Case Disposed?"}
    CaseDisposed -->|No| NextHearing["Schedule Next Hearing Alert"]
    NextHearing --> ProsecutionModule
    CaseDisposed -->|Yes| ArchiveCase("Archive Complete Case Record")
    NCReport --> ArchiveCase
    ClosureReport --> ArchiveCase
flowchartDiagram.caption: CCTNS Case Lifecycle from Complaint to Disposition

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This sequence diagram shows the interaction between a Citizen, the CCTNS web application, the IO, the Court System interface, and the Audit Trail during a complete case lifecycle from registration through prosecution.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Citizen as Citizen
    participant CCTNS as CCTNS WebApp
    participant DB as Case Database
    participant IO as Investigating Officer
    participant Court as Prosecution Module
    participant Audit as Audit Trail

    Citizen->>+CCTNS: Submit Complaint via Citizen Interface
    CCTNS->>DB: Create Case Record with Unique ID
    CCTNS->>Audit: Log Registration Action
    DB-->>CCTNS: Case ID Generated
    CCTNS-->>-Citizen: Display Acknowledgement with Case ID

    CCTNS->>IO: Notify New Case Assignment via Dashboard
    IO->>+CCTNS: Access Case via Navigation Module
    CCTNS->>DB: Retrieve Case Registration Data
    DB-->>CCTNS: Return Case Details
    CCTNS-->>-IO: Display Investigation Interface

    IO->>+CCTNS: Record Investigation Findings
    CCTNS->>DB: Update Case with Evidence and Statements
    CCTNS->>Audit: Log All Investigation Actions
    DB-->>CCTNS: Case Updated
    IO->>CCTNS: Execute MO Search
    CCTNS->>DB: Search Criminal Database
    DB-->>CCTNS: Return Matching Cases
    CCTNS-->>-IO: Display Search Results

    IO->>+CCTNS: File Chargesheet and Forward to Prosecution
    CCTNS->>DB: Update Case Status to Under Prosecution
    CCTNS->>Audit: Log Prosecution Forwarding
    CCTNS->>Court: Create Prosecution Record
    Court-->>CCTNS: Prosecution Tracking Initiated
    CCTNS-->>-IO: Confirm Prosecution Forwarding

    Citizen->>+CCTNS: Check Case Status via Citizen Interface
    CCTNS->>DB: Retrieve Case Status
    DB-->>CCTNS: Return Current Status
    CCTNS-->>-Citizen: Display Status Update
sequenceDiagram.caption: CCTNS Case Registration to Prosecution Sequence

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This ER diagram models the core data entities of the CCTNS system including cases, persons (complainants, suspects, witnesses), evidence, court hearings, users, and the audit trail. Relationships show cardinality between entities.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    CASE ||--|{ PERSON_INVOLVEMENT : "involves"
    PERSON ||--o{ PERSON_INVOLVEMENT : "participates_in"
    CASE ||--o{ EVIDENCE : "contains"
    CASE ||--o{ COURT_HEARING : "prosecuted_through"
    CASE ||--o{ AUDIT_ENTRY : "tracked_by"
    USER ||--o{ AUDIT_ENTRY : "performed_by"
    USER }|--|{ USER_GROUP : "member_of"
    USER_GROUP ||--o{ ACCESS_PERMISSION : "granted"
    CASE }|--|| POLICE_STATION : "registered_at"
    CASE }|--|| ACT_SECTION : "classified_under"

    CASE {
        int case_id PK
        string case_number UK
        string case_type
        string status
        string description
        datetime registration_date
        int investigating_officer_id FK
        int police_station_id FK
        int act_section_id FK
        datetime last_accessed
    }
    PERSON {
        int person_id PK
        string full_name
        string father_name
        string address
        string identification_marks
        string photo_path
        string category
    }
    PERSON_INVOLVEMENT {
        int involvement_id PK
        int case_id FK
        int person_id FK
        string role
        datetime involvement_date
    }
    EVIDENCE {
        int evidence_id PK
        int case_id FK
        string evidence_type
        string description
        string storage_location
        datetime collected_date
    }
    COURT_HEARING {
        int hearing_id PK
        int case_id FK
        datetime hearing_date
        string court_name
        string outcome
        datetime next_hearing_date
        string judge_directives
    }
    USER {
        int user_id PK
        string username UK
        string password_hash
        string full_name
        string role
        int police_station_id FK
    }
    USER_GROUP {
        int group_id PK
        string group_name UK
        string security_level
    }
    ACCESS_PERMISSION {
        int permission_id PK
        int group_id FK
        string resource_type
        string access_level
    }
    AUDIT_ENTRY {
        int audit_id PK
        int case_id FK
        int user_id FK
        string action_type
        string entity_type
        string old_value
        string new_value
        datetime timestamp
        string workstation
        string network_address
    }
    POLICE_STATION {
        int station_id PK
        string station_name UK
        string district
        string state
    }
    ACT_SECTION {
        int act_section_id PK
        string act_name
        string section_number
        string description
        string state_code FK
    }
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: CCTNS Crime and Criminal Tracking Entity Relationships

Maximum unplanned downtime duration and incident count thresholds per rolling three-month period are TBD.
Maximum system restoration time following software or hardware failure is TBD.

Integration specifications with existing state-level criminal justice databases and inter-state data sharing protocols are TBD.
