introduction.purpose: This Software Requirements Specification (SRS) document defines all functional, performance, and quality requirements for **gamma j** (GAMMA-J Web Store). The GAMMA-J Web Store is designed to allow new online store owners a quick and easy means to set up and perform sales and other core business over the internet. This document outlines all of the functions, capabilities, and requirements for Version 1 of the GAMMA-J Web Store, which is planned for implementation on a plug-and-play USB Key device. Future versions will be based on other network appliances.
introduction.documentConventions: This document follows the IEEE 830-1998 standard for Software Requirements Specifications. Requirements are identified with structured prefixes: OE (Operating Environment), CO (Constraints), UD (User Documentation), AD (Assumptions and Dependencies), HI (Hardware Interfaces), SI (Software Interfaces), and CI (Communications Interfaces). System features are organized by functional area with description, stimulus/response sequences, and functional requirements for each. Quality attributes are categorized into nine areas: Performance, Safety, Security, Availability, Efficiency, Usability, Maintainability, Portability, and Testability. Appendices include a comprehensive glossary, detailed use cases for all user classes, analysis models, an issues list, and a complete data dictionary.
introduction.intendedAudience: This document is intended for: the GAMMA-J customer team for reviewing and validating requirements against their business needs; the development team for guidance on overall design and implementation of the Web Store system; the test and verification team for ensuring requirements are being met; and the tech writer for assisting with user documentation. The document is designed to be reviewed from beginning to end; however, readers new to technical documentation may want to refer to Appendix E: Data Dictionary first.
introduction.productScope: According to GAMMA-J's Functional Needs Statement, the **gamma j** Web Store shall manage customer accounts, manage an online store inventory, manage a customer's shopping cart, confirm orders, have an unambiguous interface to assist in browsing categories and products, use Secure Socket Layer (SSL) for security, have an availability of 99.999%, allow an optional mirror site for reliability and backups, and feature an interface for future software enhancement via plug-ins. The initial inventory capacity is 100 items, expandable with unique codes to a minimum total inventory of 20,000 items. Since this is a plug-and-play device, no software installation is necessary. Advanced needs can be added via plug-ins.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
GAMMA-J Web Store System Overview.
GAMMA-J Functional Needs Statement.
GAMMA-J Stakeholder Goals List.

overallDescription.productPerspective:
The **gamma j** Web Store is a new, self-contained e-commerce system designed for users new to online commerce. Unlike traditional web stores that require separate hosting, server configuration, and software installation, the GAMMA-J Web Store is delivered as a complete plug-and-play USB Key device with its own CPU and operating system. When plugged into a host system, the device becomes immediately operational as a fully functional web store.

The system architecture centers on an embedded Intel XScale PXA270 520-MHz chipset with 4GB Flash RAM and 128MB SDRAM, running Slackware Linux 2.6 with Apache Web Server and MySQL database. The USB Key hardware is manufactured by Yoggie Corporation, who provides the base hardware platform, USB interface drivers, and operating system. GAMMA-J's development team is responsible for the web store application layer built on top of this platform.

The system communicates with customers through standard web browsers via HTTPS, providing a familiar e-commerce experience. It integrates with external payment processing services for credit card validation, and uses email services for order confirmation and tracking notifications. The system also supports a plug-in API architecture enabling future functionality extensions without modifying the core application.
productFunctions: Account Management (AM) - High Priority: Allows users to create, edit, and view account information. Enables user login and logout functionality. Manages customer profiles including billing addresses, shipping addresses, and payment information.

Search Engine (SE) - Medium Priority: Assists users in finding specific items in the database. Receives search criteria, executes searches using Dijkstra's shortest path algorithm, and returns results efficiently.

Product Management (PM) - High Priority: Allows sales personnel to manage the product line shown on the web site. Supports adding, removing, and updating product details including name, ID, description, price, images, inventory count, and availability.

Shopping Cart (SC) - Medium Priority: Provides temporary storage for customers shopping on the web. Items from inventory can be reserved in a virtual cart until the customer decides to purchase them. Displays item count and total cost, with add and remove capabilities.

Purchasing and Payment (PP) - High Priority: Used to approve and transfer payment from buyers when purchasing items in the cart. Integrates with credit card validation services and generates order confirmations.

Plug-in API - High Priority: Provides an Application Programming Interface allowing customers to build custom plug-ins to meet their specific needs. Ensures system flexibility and tailoring to individual business requirements.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: Generally the owner responsible for maintenance of the Web Store system. Administrators assign account privileges, manage user accounts (add, remove, change properties), install and manage plug-ins, apply patches and software updates, and configure system settings. More than one individual should have administrator privileges to ensure availability. Full documentation is provided to assist with administrative processes. Administrators access the system via HTTPS through a dedicated administrative interface.

Generally the owner of the Web Store tasked with updating inventory and product line descriptions. Sales personnel can add, delete, and change product descriptions, pictures, prices, and availability flags. They manage the day-to-day product catalog and inventory levels. They access the system through a web browser with HTTPS and have dedicated use cases for product management operations.

An individual wishing to purchase inventory from the GAMMA-J Web Store. The store serves a variety of clientele depending upon the inventory loaded on the Key. When creating a new account, it defaults as a customer account; administrators can upgrade privileges if needed. Customers browse products, manage shopping carts, complete purchases, track orders, and manage their account details including shipping addresses and credit card information.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment:
**OE-1:** The **gamma j** Web Store shall operate with Microsoft Internet Explorer version 6 and 7, and Netscape Communicator Version 4 and 5.

**OE-2:** The system shall operate on an Intel-based system with Slackware Linux 2.6 and Apache Web Server, designed by the Yoggie Corporation. The developers are not responsible for the functionality of the operating system.

**OE-3:** The system shall use an SQL-based database (MySQL) to store inventory information.

**OE-4:** USB interface and drivers are provided by Yoggie Corporation.

**Hardware Specifications:** Intel XScale PXA270 520-MHz chipset, 4GB Flash RAM, 128MB SDRAM.
designAndImplementationConstraints: CO-1: Must use a SQL-based database. SQL standard is the most widely used database format, ensuring ease of use and compatibility for the Web Store.

CO-2: Compatibility is only tested and verified for Microsoft Internet Explorer version 6 and 7, Netscape Communicator Version 4 and 5. Other browsers such as Mozilla or Firefox may not be 100% compatible.
The system is constrained to the hardware capabilities of the Yoggie USB Key device including the Intel XScale PXA270 processor, 4GB Flash storage, and 128MB SDRAM.

Yoggie Corporation controls the base platform including the operating system, USB drivers, and hardware specifications. The development team depends on Yoggie for hardware delivery and baseline freezes.
userDocumentation: UD-1: Shall install online help for users via the web interface.
UD-2: Shall deliver Operations and Maintenance manual, Users Guide book, and Installation Instructions for the Administrator.
assumptionsAndDependencies: AD-1: Assume the delivery of development, test, and evaluation samples of the USB system from Yoggie Corporation.
AD-2: Assume Yoggie will freeze the baseline of the USB system after delivery.
Yoggie shall coordinate on future enhancement and features with the GAMMA-J organization.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces:
The **gamma j** Web Store presents a web-based interface accessible through supported browsers (IE 6/7, Netscape 4/5). The interface includes multiple distinct screens for different user roles and functions:

**Customer-Facing Screens:** Login screen with email and password fields supporting cookie-based session persistence; product browsing interface with multi-tiered category navigation showing product images, descriptions, and prices; shopping cart screen displaying item count, unit prices, quantities, shipping prices, and order totals; order summary screen showing the complete order with account details, shipping address, and masked credit card information; order confirmation screen with confirmation number and order details; and order status tracking screen.

**Administrative Screens:** System administrator login screen; product management interface with capabilities for adding (product name, ID, description, price, image upload, inventory count, availability), deleting, and updating products; user management interface for adding, removing, and modifying user accounts and privileges; plug-in management interface for installing, removing, and configuring plug-ins; and system maintenance interface for patch installation.

**Sales Personnel Screens:** Product management interfaces with identical CRUD capabilities for managing the product catalog.

The interface employs consistent symbols and colors for clear notifications, help bubbles to assist all user roles, easy-to-locate buttons, and friendly easy-to-read error messages.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: HI-1: The system interfaces with the Yoggie USB Key hardware, a plug-and-play device containing the complete web store system on an Intel XScale PXA270 520-MHz chipset with 4GB Flash RAM and 128MB SDRAM. The USB interface provides the physical connection between the device and the host system, with drivers provided by Yoggie Corporation.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces:
SI-1: WebOrder Browser Interface — The order database of WebOrder communicates with the account system through a programmatic interface for billing operations.

SI-1.2: Through a programmatic interface, WebOrder transmits information of items ordered by customers to the Inventory Management system.

SI-1.3: Plug-ins interface — The system provides a documented API allowing third-party plug-ins to interact with the web store system. Plug-in requests are routed through the API Service which validates results before returning them to the web server.

The system operates on Slackware Linux 2.6 with Apache Web Server and MySQL database. The operating system and USB interface components are developed and maintained by Yoggie Corporation.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces:
CI-1: The WebOrder system shall send an e-mail confirmation to the customer that the items they ordered will be delivered to the shipping address along with a tracking number.

CI-2: The WebOrder system shall send an e-mail to the System Administrator regarding any technical queries from customers or sales people.

All customer-facing communications are conducted over HTTPS using Secure Socket Layer (SSL) encryption. Session management uses browser cookies for maintaining user state across interactions.

systemFeatures.name: Customer Account Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature enables customers to create, manage, and secure their accounts within the GAMMA-J Web Store. Customers can register new accounts with personal and payment information, log in and out with session cookie management, edit their account details, and maintain their profiles. The system supports automatic cookie recognition for returning customers, password recovery via email, and secure HTTPS communication for all account operations. This is a **high priority** system feature as it underpins all customer interactions with the store. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Customer clicks Register button. Response: System prompts for first name, last name, billing address, shipping address, email address, and password. Upon validation, system creates account, generates session cookie, and displays account home page.

Stimulus: Customer clicks Login button. Response: System prompts for email and password, verifies credentials, creates session cookie, and displays account home page. Alternative: System recognizes existing cookie and proceeds directly to home page.
Stimulus: Customer enters incorrect login credentials. Response: System prompts customer to resend login details to registered email address. Upon confirmation, system sends recovery email.
Stimulus: Customer clicks Edit Account. Response: System displays account details for modification, verifies changes, and stores updated information.
Stimulus: Customer clicks Logout. Response: System terminates session cookie and displays home page.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow customers to create accounts storing customer profiles, contact information, billing address, shipping address, email address, and password with minimum 4 to maximum 10 character credentials.
The system shall support customer login with email and password verification and session cookie creation for persistent sessions.
The system shall recognize returning customers via browser cookies and allow automatic session restoration.
The system shall support password recovery by sending login details to the registered email address upon customer confirmation.
The system shall allow customers to edit their account details including profile information, addresses, and payment methods.
The system shall allow customers to log out, terminating the session cookie and returning to the home page.
The system shall store customer payment information including credit card numbers (16-digit numeric) and expiration dates (MM/YY format) with encryption.
The system shall support simultaneous customer accounts with up to 1000 concurrent logged-in users.

systemFeatures.name: Product and Inventory Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature allows sales personnel and administrators to manage the complete product catalog and inventory within the Web Store. Products are organized in multi-tiered categories where a category can contain sub-categories or products. Sales personnel can add new products with full details (name, ID, description, price, images, inventory count, availability), remove products, and update any product attributes. The initial inventory supports 100 items, expandable to a minimum of 20,000 items using unique expansion codes. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Sales Person clicks Add Product. Response: System prompts for product name, product ID, description, price, image upload, inventory count, and availability. System validates information, creates product page, and displays newly created page.

Stimulus: Sales Person clicks Remove Product. Response: System prompts to select a product by search or list view. System validates product information, removes the product page and data, and confirms deletion.

Stimulus: Sales Person clicks Update Product Attributes. Response: System prompts to select a product, displays all attributes for editing (name, ID, description, price, image, availability, inventory count). System validates changes, updates product page, and displays updated page.
functionalRequirements: The system shall organize products in multi-tiered categories where categories can contain sub-categories or individual products.
The system shall allow sales personnel to add products with name (max 50 chars), product ID (7-digit alphanumeric), description (max 100 chars), price, image, inventory count, and availability flag.
The system shall allow sales personnel to remove products from the catalog with confirmation.
The system shall allow sales personnel to update any product attribute including name, ID, description, price, image, availability, and inventory count.
The system shall support an initial inventory of 100 items expandable to a minimum of 20,000 items through unique expansion codes.
The system shall be able to retrieve 200 products per second to support efficient browsing and search operations.
The system shall automatically compress image files that are too large in size when uploaded for product listings.

systemFeatures.name: Shopping Cart and Order Processing
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides the complete shopping experience from product selection through order confirmation. Customers can add items to a virtual shopping cart with persistent cookie storage, view cart contents with quantities and pricing, modify quantities or remove items, proceed through checkout with payment and shipping confirmation, and receive email confirmation with order and tracking details. The system maintains cart state through cookies even if the browser is closed. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Customer clicks Add To Cart with specified quantity. Response: System adds items to cart, prompts customer to edit quantity or remove items, stores cookie with cart details upon confirmation.
Stimulus: Customer closes browser after adding items to cart and later returns. Response: System recognizes cookie and restores the cart with existing items.

Stimulus: Customer clicks Checkout. Response: System calculates order total, appends checkout flag to cookie, presents account details and payment methods for confirmation. Customer confirms order. System stores order details, sends email confirmation, and sets completed checkout flag.
Stimulus: Customer terminates browser during checkout and returns. Response: System recognizes checkout cookie and resumes at the account details and payment step.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow customers to add products to a shopping cart with specified quantities.
The system shall display the number of items in the cart along with total cost, including unit prices, quantities, shipping prices, and line totals.
The system shall allow customers to modify quantities or remove items from the shopping cart before checkout.
The system shall maintain cart state through browser cookies, allowing cart persistence across browser sessions.
The system shall calculate complete order totals including item costs, quantities, and shipping charges.
The system shall present order summary with account details, shipping address, and masked credit card information before final confirmation.
The system shall send email confirmation to the customer with order details and tracking number upon order completion.
The system shall be able to add products to the shopping cart in less than 2 milliseconds.
The system shall be able to acquire shipping charges in less than 2 seconds.
The system shall support order status checking allowing customers to track their orders after placement.

systemFeatures.name: Search and Product Discovery
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides customers with the ability to search for and discover products within the Web Store's catalog. The search engine receives criteria from the customer, executes optimized searches using Dijkstra's shortest path algorithm for efficient traversal, and returns matching results. The interface assists customers in browsing through multi-tiered categories and finding specific products. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Customer enters search criteria in the search interface. Response: System executes search using Dijkstra's algorithm, retrieves matching products, and displays results to the customer.
Stimulus: Customer browses product categories. Response: System navigates the multi-tiered category hierarchy displaying sub-categories and products at each level.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide a search engine that receives customer search criteria and returns matching products.
The system shall perform searches using Dijkstra's shortest path algorithm for efficient category and product traversal.
The system shall be able to search for a specified product in less than 1 second.
The system shall present an unambiguous interface to assist in browsing categories and products across the multi-tiered hierarchy.
The system shall be compatible with all major web browsers including Internet Explorer, Mozilla Navigator, Mozilla Firefox, Opera, and Safari for the browsing interface.

systemFeatures.name: Plug-in Extension Framework
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides an Application Programming Interface (API) enabling customers and third-party developers to build custom plug-ins that extend the Web Store's functionality. The plug-in architecture ensures the system's flexibility to be tailored to specific business needs without modifying the core application. Plug-ins are managed through the administrative interface with install, remove, and configuration capabilities. This is a **high priority** feature ensuring long-term extensibility. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator clicks Install Plug-ins and uploads a plug-in module. Response: System installs the plug-in, validates changes, and displays plug-in options to the Administrator.
Stimulus: Administrator clicks Remove Plug-ins and selects a module. Response: System removes the plug-in and validates that the removal does not break system integrity.
Stimulus: Administrator clicks Manage Plug-ins and selects a module. Response: System displays all plug-in options, allows configuration changes, and confirms changes with the Administrator.

Stimulus: Web browser request triggers a plug-in function. Response: Web Server routes request to API Service, API Service submits to Plug-in, Plug-in executes task and returns results, API Service validates results and returns to Web Server for display.
functionalRequirements: The system shall implement a well-documented Application Programming Interface allowing third-party plug-ins to interact with core system functions.
The system shall support administrator installation of plug-in modules through the web administrative interface.
The system shall support administrator removal of plug-in modules with integrity validation.
The system shall provide plug-in configuration management allowing administrators to view and modify plug-in options.
The system shall route plug-in requests through the API Service which validates results before returning them to the web server.
The system shall provide plug-in specifications and documentation for developers building custom extensions.
The system shall utilize interchangeable plugins that can be installed and removed without system restart.

systemFeatures.name: User Administration and System Maintenance
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides comprehensive administrative capabilities for managing users, system configuration, and software maintenance. Administrators can add new users (customers, sales personnel, or other administrators) with specified privileges, remove users, change user properties including passwords and privilege levels, and apply system patches and software updates. The system logs all changes, updates, and fixes for maintainability tracking. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator clicks Add Users. Response: System prompts for first name, last name, username, email address, password, and privilege level. System validates information, creates the account with desired privileges, and displays the admin home page.

Stimulus: Administrator clicks Remove User. Response: System prompts to select a user by search or list view. System displays user information, confirms deletion, removes the account, and returns to admin home page.
Stimulus: Administrator clicks Change User Properties. Response: System prompts to select a user, displays their information, allows property modifications, and confirms changes.
Stimulus: Administrator clicks Install Patch. Response: System prompts for patch upload. System automatically installs patches, reinitializes software, and confirms successful installation.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow administrators to add new user accounts with configurable privilege levels (customer, sales personnel, administrator).
The system shall allow administrators to remove user accounts from the system with confirmation.
The system shall allow administrators to modify user properties including passwords and privilege levels.
The system shall support software patch installation through the web administrative interface with automatic reinitialization.
The system shall create logs of all changes, updates, or fixes applied to the system for maintainability tracking.
The system shall be easily updatable for fixes and patches through the administrative interface.
The system shall support administrator login with email/password authentication and cookie-based session management identical to customer login flow.

performanceRequirements: Upon the USB being plugged in, the system shall be able to be deployed and operational in less than 1 minute.
The system shall be able to handle 1000 customers logged in concurrently at the same time.
The system shall be able to retrieve 200 products per second for catalog browsing.
The system shall be able to add a product to the shopping cart in less than 2 milliseconds.
The system shall be able to search for a specified product in less than 1 second.
The system shall be able to email customer and vendor in less than 1 second.
The system shall be able to validate credit card transactions in less than 2 seconds.
The system shall be able to acquire shipping charges in less than 2 seconds.
The system shall be able to restore 1000 records per second for data recovery operations.
safetyRequirements: The system shall perform periodic backups through a live internet connection to prevent data loss.
The system shall support an optional mirror site for reliability and backup purposes.
securityRequirements: The system shall validate credit cards against fraud before processing transactions.
The system shall encrypt all sensitive information via HTTPS using Secure Socket Layer (SSL) encryption.
The system shall encrypt all customer data stored in the database.
The system shall auto-detect IP-based Denial of Service (DOS) attacks and block offending IP addresses automatically.
The system shall detect consecutive failed login attempts and implement appropriate lockout or notification mechanisms.
The system shall be protected by the Firestarter open-source firewall (http://www.fs-security.com/).
softwareQualityAttributes: Availability: The system shall have an availability of 99.99%, with support for an optional mirror site for additional reliability and backup.

Efficiency: The system shall perform searches via Dijkstra's shortest path algorithm for optimized traversal. For returning customers, the system shall validate existing credit cards after each login. The system shall automatically compress oversized image files. The system shall employ on-demand asynchronous loading for faster page execution. The system shall validate email address existence.

Usability: The system shall be easy to use and easy to learn. It shall utilize help bubbles to assist managers, customers, and administrators. It shall employ easy-to-locate buttons, friendly easy-to-read error messages, and consistent symbols and colors for clear notifications.

Maintainability: The system shall utilize interchangeable plugins for extensibility. It shall be easily updatable for fixes and patches. It shall create logs of all changes, updates, and fixes. It shall be easy to upgrade.
Portability: The system shall be extremely portable via the USB drive. It shall be easy to migrate or back up via another USB drive.

Testability: The system should be able to run under debug mode. It should support test credit card transactions and test shipping orders. It should allow creating a test environment of the weborder system.

otherRequirements: The system hardware shall be fixed and patched via an internet connection.
Yoggie Corporation shall coordinate on future enhancements and features with the GAMMA-J organization.
The system shall adhere to the specified hardware requirements: 4GB Flash RAM, 128MB SDRAM, Intel XScale PXA270 520-MHz chipset, Apache web server OS, MySQL database.

glossary.definition: A computer feature that allows the addition of a new device, normally a peripheral, without requiring reconfiguration or manual installation of device drivers.
Synchronous DRAM, a type of DRAM that synchronizes with the CPU bus and is capable of running at 133 MHz, approximately three times faster than conventional FPM RAM.
A person playing a specific role, a software system, or a hardware device that interacts with a system to achieve a useful goal.
A high-level business objective of the organization that builds a product or of a customer who procures it.
A policy, guideline, standard, or regulation that defines or constrains some aspect of the business.
Servers working together as one machine to provide increased availability of applications.
A restriction imposed on the choices available to the developer for the design and construction of a product.
An analysis model that depicts a system at a high level of abstraction, showing external system interfaces.
A collection of definitions for the data elements, structures, and attributes important to the problem domain.
An analysis model depicting processes, data collections, terminators, and flows that characterize the behavior of a business process or software system.
A set of logically related functional requirements that provides a capability to the user and enables the satisfaction of a business objective.
A statement of a piece of required functionality or behavior that a system will exhibit under specific conditions.
The Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers.
A description of a property or characteristic that the system should exhibit, such as performance, security, or usability.
Redundant Array of Independent Drives, computer data storage schemes that divide and/or replicate data among multiple hard drives.
Secure Socket Layer, a protocol for transmitting private documents via the internet through encrypted connections.
Software Requirements Specification, a collection of functional and non-functional requirements for a software product.
A kind of nonfunctional requirement that describes a quality or property of a system.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This flowchart illustrates the complete customer purchase journey through the GAMMA-J Web Store, from initial browsing through account creation, shopping cart management, checkout, payment validation, and order confirmation with email notification.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    Browse("Customer Browses Store") --> HasAccount{"Has Account?"}
    HasAccount -->|No| Register["Register: Enter Name, Address, Email, Password"]
    Register --> CreateAccount["System Creates Account and Session Cookie"]
    CreateAccount --> BrowseProducts["Browse Multi-Tiered Product Categories"]
    HasAccount -->|Yes| Login["Login: Enter Email and Password"]
    Login --> ValidCreds{"Credentials Valid?"}
    ValidCreds -->|No| RecoverPassword["Send Recovery Email"]
    RecoverPassword --> Login
    ValidCreds -->|Yes| SetCookie["Create Session Cookie"]
    SetCookie --> BrowseProducts
    BrowseProducts --> SearchOrBrowse{"Search or Browse?"}
    SearchOrBrowse -->|Search| ExecuteSearch["Search via Dijkstra Algorithm"]
    ExecuteSearch --> ViewResults["Display Search Results"]
    SearchOrBrowse -->|Browse| NavCategories["Navigate Category Hierarchy"]
    NavCategories --> ViewResults
    ViewResults --> SelectProduct["Select Product"]
    SelectProduct --> AddToCart["Add to Shopping Cart"]
    AddToCart --> ContinueShopping{"Continue Shopping?"}
    ContinueShopping -->|Yes| BrowseProducts
    ContinueShopping -->|No| Checkout["Initiate Checkout"]
    Checkout --> ReviewOrder["Review Order and Payment Details"]
    ReviewOrder --> ConfirmOrder{"Confirm Order?"}
    ConfirmOrder -->|No| ModifyCart["Modify Cart Items"]
    ModifyCart --> Checkout
    ConfirmOrder -->|Yes| ValidateCC["Validate Credit Card"]
    ValidateCC --> CCValid{"Card Valid?"}
    CCValid -->|No| UpdatePayment["Update Payment Information"]
    UpdatePayment --> ReviewOrder
    CCValid -->|Yes| ProcessOrder["Store Order and Calculate Shipping"]
    ProcessOrder --> SendConfirmation["Send Email with Tracking Number"]
    SendConfirmation --> OrderComplete("Order Complete")
flowchartDiagram.caption: Customer Purchase Journey Through GAMMA-J Web Store

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This sequence diagram shows the detailed interaction between Customer, Web Browser, Web Server, API Service, Plugin, Database, and Email Service during a complete shopping and checkout flow, illustrating the plug-in API architecture and cookie-based session management.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Customer as Customer
    participant Browser as Web Browser
    participant Server as Apache Web Server
    participant App as WebOrder Application
    participant DB as MySQL Database
    participant API as Plug-in API Service
    participant Email as Email Service

    Customer->>Browser: Navigate to Web Store
    Browser->>+Server: HTTPS Request
    Server->>App: Parse Request
    App->>DB: Query Product Categories
    DB-->>App: Return Category Hierarchy
    App-->>Server: Generate Product Page
    Server-->>-Browser: Return HTML Response
    Browser-->>Customer: Display Product Catalog

    Customer->>Browser: Click Add To Cart
    Browser->>+Server: HTTPS POST Add Item
    Server->>App: Process Cart Addition
    App->>DB: Reserve Inventory Item
    DB-->>App: Confirm Reservation
    App-->>Server: Cart Updated Response
    Server-->>-Browser: Set Cart Cookie and Display Cart

    Customer->>Browser: Click Checkout
    Browser->>+Server: HTTPS POST Checkout
    Server->>App: Initiate Checkout
    App->>DB: Calculate Order Total
    DB-->>App: Return Order Details
    App-->>Server: Order Summary Page
    Server-->>-Browser: Display Order Summary

    Customer->>Browser: Confirm Order
    Browser->>+Server: HTTPS POST Confirm
    Server->>App: Process Payment
    App->>API: Validate Credit Card
    API-->>App: Card Valid
    App->>DB: Store Order Record
    DB-->>App: Order ID Generated
    App->>Email: Send Confirmation with Tracking
    Email-->>App: Email Sent
    App-->>Server: Confirmation Page
    Server-->>-Browser: Display Confirmation
    Browser-->>Customer: Order Confirmed
sequenceDiagram.caption: Shopping and Checkout Flow with Plug-in API Architecture

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This ER diagram models the core data entities of the GAMMA-J Web Store including users, products, categories, shopping carts, orders, and plug-ins. Relationships show the multi-tiered category architecture and the complete order processing data model.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    USER ||--o{ USER_ORDER : "places"
    USER ||--o| SHOPPING_CART : "owns"
    SHOPPING_CART ||--|{ CART_ITEM : "contains"
    PRODUCT ||--o{ CART_ITEM : "referenced_by"
    PRODUCT }|--|| CATEGORY : "belongs_to"
    CATEGORY ||--o{ CATEGORY : "parent_of"
    USER_ORDER ||--|{ ORDER_ITEM : "includes"
    PRODUCT ||--o{ ORDER_ITEM : "referenced_by"
    USER_ORDER ||--o| PAYMENT : "paid_by"
    PLUGIN ||--o{ PLUGIN_CONFIG : "configured_by"

    USER {
        string user_id PK
        string password
        string first_name
        string last_name
        string email UK
        string billing_address
        string shipping_address
        string city
        string state
        string zip_code
        string credit_card_no
        string cc_expiry_date
        string role
    }
    CATEGORY {
        int category_id PK
        string category_name
        int parent_category_id FK
        int depth_level
    }
    PRODUCT {
        string product_id PK
        int category_id FK
        string item_name
        float price
        string text_description
        string image_path
        int quantity_in_stock
        boolean available
        string location
    }
    SHOPPING_CART {
        int cart_id PK
        string user_id FK
        datetime created_at
        string cookie_token
    }
    CART_ITEM {
        int cart_item_id PK
        int cart_id FK
        string product_id FK
        int quantity
        float unit_price
    }
    USER_ORDER {
        string order_no PK
        string user_id FK
        datetime order_date
        float total_amount
        string shipping_address
        string tracking_no
        datetime shipping_date
        string status
    }
    ORDER_ITEM {
        int order_item_id PK
        string order_no FK
        string product_id FK
        int quantity
        float unit_price
        float shipping_price
        float line_total
    }
    PAYMENT {
        int payment_id PK
        string order_no FK
        string credit_card_no
        string cc_expiry_date
        string card_holder_name
        float amount
        string status
    }
    PLUGIN {
        int plugin_id PK
        string plugin_name UK
        string version
        string status
        datetime installed_at
    }
    PLUGIN_CONFIG {
        int config_id PK
        int plugin_id FK
        string config_key
        string config_value
    }
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: GAMMA-J Web Store E-Commerce Data Model

introduction.purpose: This Software Requirements Specification (SRS) document presents the business requirements for the **inventory** system (IUfA's Unified University Inventory System, UUIS). The document describes all functional and non-functional requirements using a technology-independent approach with primarily business terminology and use case modeling. UUIS integrates three faculty databases through a web interface that allows users to access and manage the unified inventory across a university's organizational hierarchy.
introduction.documentConventions: This document follows the IEEE 830-1998 standard for Software Requirements Specifications. Use case approach is used for modeling business requirements. Each use case is identified with a unique code (e.g., MOD.UC, EDT.UC, ANI.UC, CRQ.UC, RTI.UC, APR.UC, CHP.UC, ATH.UC, SRCH.UC, REP.UC) and includes description, goal, preconditions, assumptions, basic course, alternate courses, exceptional courses, post conditions, actors, included use cases, and notes. Permission identifiers follow a resource:action pattern (e.g., asset:create, request:list, location:edit). User levels are numbered 0 through 4 with hierarchical privilege inheritance.
introduction.intendedAudience: This document is intended for the COMP5541-W10 course project team (Team 4) and their instructor, system architects designing the multi-faculty inventory integration, university administrators who will use the system for asset management across faculties and departments, IT administrators responsible for system deployment, permission management, and maintenance, and quality assurance personnel validating functional and non-functional requirements.
introduction.productScope: The **inventory** system (UUIS) integrates three faculty databases into a unified web-based inventory management system for a university. The system supports the following core operations: transferring assets within and across departments, faculties, and outside the university with appropriate approval workflows; editing and modifying asset properties with authorization checks; adding inventory assets individually or via bulk entry; creating requests to borrow assets or reserve locations with basic, advanced, and exception request forms; returning assets with status updates (available or damaged); creating new locations through IT team exception request processing; approving or rejecting pending requests with email notification; authentication via username and password; delegating permissions from higher administrative levels to lower levels; and generating three types of output reports (asset by location, request, and user permission). The system manages three asset types: rooms/spaces, software licenses, and all other physical assets. Assets can be grouped (e.g., computer parts) and are organized within a university hierarchy spanning three faculties and six departments.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
Shari Lawrence Pfleeger and Joanne M. Atlee. Software Engineering: Theory and Practice. Prentice Hall, fourth edition, 2009. ISBN: 978-0-13-606169-4.
Object-Oriented Software Engineering: Practical Software Development using UML and Java, Timothy Lethbridge, ISBN: 0077109082, McGraw-Hill, Edition 2.
The Elements of UML 2.0 Style, Cambridge University Press, 2005, ISBN: 0-521-61678-6.
http://www.agilemodeling.com/style/useCaseDiagram.htm

overallDescription.productPerspective:
The **inventory** system (UUIS) is a new web-based application designed to unify three separate faculty databases into a single integrated inventory management platform. It replaces the fragmented, faculty-specific inventory tracking systems with a centralized solution that maintains the university's organizational hierarchy while enabling cross-faculty operations.

The system reflects the university's three-tier organizational structure: University level at the top, three Faculties (Arts and Science, Computer Science, Engineering) in the middle tier, and six Departments at the lowest tier (Biology and Sociology under Arts and Science; Software Engineering and Computer Theory under Computer Science; Engineering and Mathematics under Engineering). This hierarchical structure directly maps to the five-level user permission model that governs all inventory operations.

The system guarantees secure access to data from outside the university during working hours, providing a web interface that enables authorized users to manage the integrated inventory regardless of their physical location. The system integrates authentication, role-based access control, multi-level approval workflows, and email notification into a cohesive inventory management platform.
productFunctions: Transferring Assets: Supports intra-department transfers (direct database update), inter-department transfers (DA and faculty approval required), inter-faculty transfers (faculty or higher level approval), and transfers outside the university (university level approval required).
Editing and Modifying Assets: Authorized users can edit assets belonging to their organizational scope. All fields except IDs can be modified. Bulk entry files are supported for mass modifications.

Adding Inventory Assets: Department administrators or authorized inventory group members can add assets owned by their department. Faculty members can add inventory for all related departments. University group members can add any asset. Bulk entry is supported.

Creating Requests: Users can submit basic requests (text description), advanced requests (serial number, location, description with multi-asset support), or exception requests (free-form text). Forms provide search facilities for levels 1, 2, and 3.
Returning Assets: Inventory users check returned assets and update inventory status to available or damaged.
Creating Locations: IT group members create new spaces and modify floor structure upon receiving exception requests from administrative levels.
Approving Requests: Administrative users display and process pending requests within their privilege scope. Approved requests trigger email notifications and add items to the execution waiting list.
Authentication: Username and password authentication for all users, with administrative level users working on administration computers.
Changing Permissions: Administrative users can delegate all or some of their permissions to other users, who then acquire the role of inventory administrator.
Output Reports: Three report types — Asset by Location Report, Request Report, and User Permission Report — with filtering and sorting capabilities.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: Basic users who can only create inventory requests using the basic request form. They cannot directly manage assets, approve requests, or generate reports. They interact with the system primarily to borrow assets or reserve spaces.

Controls assets and locations owned by their department. Can approve requests within their departmental scope. Has the ability to delegate some or all permissions to inventory administrators within their department. Works on administration computers for authenticated access.

Controls assets and locations within their faculty and inherits all Level 1 permissions. Can approve inter-department transfers within their faculty. Has delegation capabilities for faculty-wide permission management.

Controls all assets and locations across the entire university and inherits all Level 2 permissions. Required to approve transfers outside the university. Has the broadest delegation capabilities and inventory oversight.

Has all possible permissions in the system. Can create different categories of permissions and assign them to new groups. Maintains the system, creates new locations, modifies floor structures, and manages the database servers locally. Responsible for system-wide security and infrastructure maintenance.

A delegated role that can be assigned by any administrative level user. Permissions vary depending on assigned tasks and the delegating authority. Can be assigned to any administrative or organizational level according to their tasks. This is not a fixed user class but rather a flexible role assigned through permission delegation.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The **inventory** system operates as a web-based application accessible through standard web browsers including Internet Explorer, Firefox, Chrome, Opera, and Safari. The application must be installable on both Microsoft Windows and Unix platforms, ensuring cross-platform compatibility for the server infrastructure. The system provides web-based access during university working hours with secure remote access from outside the university. Database servers are accessed locally by IT team members only for maintenance and security purposes.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The system must integrate three separate faculty databases into a unified inventory while maintaining organizational hierarchy boundaries for permission checks.
Asset IDs are immutable and cannot be modified through the edit or modify operations.
Administrative level users are required to work on designated administration computers for authenticated access.
Queries that exceed 1 minute execution time are automatically killed to prevent system resource exhaustion.
Permission delegation cannot exceed the delegator's own permission level — administrators cannot assign permissions beyond their own scope.
Backup operations must be conducted outside of working hours to maintain system availability.
userDocumentation: The system shall provide web-based help and documentation accessible through the browser interface.
User training materials designed for a learning time of 2 to 4 hours maximum, considering that many tasks are delegated to working students.
assumptionsAndDependencies: The hosting system and servers support all the required operations for the inventory management system.
The three faculty databases can be integrated into a unified schema while preserving existing data relationships.
University organizational hierarchy (faculties, departments) is stable and changes infrequently.
Users have basic experience with internet and office applications, sufficient to navigate the web interface without extensive training.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces:
The **inventory** system provides a web-based user interface accessible through multiple browsers (IE, Firefox, Chrome, Opera, Safari). The interface uses clear and consistent terminology designed so that users with basic internet and office experience find the application easy to use.

Key interface components include: authentication screens with username and password fields; asset management interfaces for searching, editing, modifying, and adding assets (individually and via bulk entry); request creation forms in three variants — basic form (text description), advanced form (serial number, location, description with multi-asset support via add another asset button), and exception form (free-form text); request approval dashboards showing pending requests filtered by the user's privilege level; permission management interfaces for delegating and modifying user permissions; and report generation interfaces for three report types (Asset by Location, Request, User Permission) with filtering and sorting capabilities.

Error handling provides descriptive messages for common scenarios: asset not found, insufficient privileges, asset not available for borrowing, permission delegation exceeding own permissions, and authentication failures.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The system interfaces with standard server hardware capable of running Microsoft Windows or Unix operating systems. Database servers are accessed locally by IT team members for maintenance. Client devices require only a standard web browser with internet connectivity.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The system integrates three separate faculty database systems into a unified inventory database. The authentication system operates as a separate component interfacing with the main inventory system for user verification. The system uses email services for sending notification messages when requests are approved or rejected.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The system communicates via HTTP/HTTPS protocols through web browsers. Email notifications are sent to users when their requests are processed (approved or rejected). The system supports secure remote access from outside the university during working hours.

systemFeatures.name: Asset Transfer Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature implements the complete asset transfer workflow with multi-level approval based on transfer scope. Transfers within the same department update the database directly without approval. Inter-department transfers require Department Administrator and Faculty group member approval. Inter-faculty transfers require Faculty group or higher level approval. Transfers outside the university require University group approval. The system enforces the hierarchical approval chain while allowing higher-level groups to bypass lower-level requirements. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Inventory Admin initiates an intra-department asset transfer. Response: System updates the database directly without requiring approval since the transfer stays within the same department.

Stimulus: User initiates an inter-department transfer within the same faculty. Response: System creates a pending request requiring approval from both the Department Administrator group member and Faculty group member. Email notification is sent upon approval/rejection.

Stimulus: User initiates an inter-faculty transfer. Response: System creates a pending request requiring Faculty group or higher level approval. The request is added to the approval queue for authorized administrators.
Stimulus: User initiates a transfer outside the university. Response: System creates a pending request requiring University group approval, the highest authorization level for transfers.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow intra-department asset transfers with direct database updates without approval workflows.
The system shall require Department Administrator and Faculty group member approval for inter-department transfers unless initiated by a higher-level group.
The system shall require Faculty group or higher level approval for inter-faculty transfers.
The system shall require University group approval for asset transfers outside the university.
The system shall send email notifications to users when transfer requests are approved or rejected.
The system shall add approved transfer requests to the waiting for execution list.
The system shall update the inventory database when the user receives the requested asset.

systemFeatures.name: Asset Editing and Modification
systemFeatures.description: This feature enables authorized users to search for, edit, and modify asset properties within the inventory system. All asset fields except IDs can be modified. The system enforces permission checks based on the user's organizational scope — department-level users can edit department assets, faculty-level users can edit faculty assets, and university-level users can edit any asset. Bulk entry files are supported for mass modifications. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Inventory Admin searches for an asset and selects edit. Response: System displays the asset properties for modification. Admin modifies the desired fields (excluding IDs) and submits. System validates and updates the database.
Stimulus: Inventory Admin attempts to edit an asset outside their authorization scope. Response: System displays an error message indicating insufficient privileges.
Stimulus: Inventory Admin searches for a non-existent asset. Response: System displays a 'no asset found' error message.
Stimulus: Inventory Admin attempts to modify an asset that is currently out of inventory. Response: System displays an error message indicating the asset cannot be modified.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow authorized users to edit assets belonging to their organizational scope (department, faculty, or university level).
The system shall allow modification of all asset fields except asset IDs.
The system shall support bulk entry files for modifying multiple assets simultaneously.
The system shall display appropriate error messages when users lack sufficient privileges, assets are not found, or assets are currently out of inventory.
The system shall enforce organizational hierarchy boundaries when checking edit/modify permissions.

systemFeatures.name: Inventory Asset Addition
systemFeatures.description: This feature enables authorized users to add new assets to the inventory system. Department administrators or authorized inventory group members can add assets owned by their department. Faculty members can add inventory for all related departments. University group members can add any asset. Bulk entry is supported for adding multiple assets at once. When an asset type does not exist, an exception request can be submitted to IT to create the new type with common properties. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrator initiates new asset creation. Response: System prompts for asset type selection, location selection, owner selection, and all asset property fields. System validates and creates the asset in the inventory database.

Stimulus: Administrator selects an asset type that does not exist in the system. Response: System enables the administrator to send an exception request to IT for adding the new type with its common properties.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow Department Administrator group members or authorized inventory group members to add assets owned by their department.
The system shall allow Faculty members to add inventory assets for all departments within their faculty.
The system shall allow University group members to add any asset to the inventory.
The system shall support bulk entry for adding multiple assets simultaneously.
The system shall require asset type, location, owner, and property fields when creating a new asset.
The system shall support exception requests to IT when the needed asset type does not exist in the system.

systemFeatures.name: Request Creation and Processing
systemFeatures.description: This feature enables users to create requests for borrowing assets or reserving locations. Requests can be submitted through three form types: basic (level 0 users — text description only), advanced (levels 1-3 — serial number, location, description with multi-asset support), and exception (free-form text for unusual situations). Created requests enter a pending state awaiting approval from the appropriate administrative level. The system supports adding multiple assets to a single request. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Level 0 user selects basic request form, enters description, and submits. Response: System creates a pending request in the approval queue for the appropriate administrative level.

Stimulus: Authorized user selects advanced request form, enters asset serial number and location, adds description, optionally adds multiple assets, and submits. Response: System creates a structured request with all specified assets pending approval.
Stimulus: User selects exception request form and enters a text description of the exception. Response: System creates an exception request for processing by IT or appropriate administrative level.
Stimulus: User searches for an asset to borrow but asset is not available. Response: System displays error message indicating asset is not available for borrowing.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide basic request forms for level 0 users with text description fields.
The system shall provide advanced request forms for levels 1, 2, and 3 with search facilities, serial number, location, and description fields.
The system shall allow users to add multiple assets to a single advanced request via an 'add another asset' button.
The system shall provide exception request forms with free-form text input.
The system shall place all new requests in a pending state awaiting approval.
The system shall display error messages when requested assets are not available for borrowing.

systemFeatures.name: Request Approval Workflow
systemFeatures.description: This feature enables administrative users to review and process pending requests within their authorization scope. The system displays only requests that the logged-in administrator has privileges to approve, enforcing the hierarchical permission model. Administrators can approve or reject requests, triggering email notifications to the requesting users. Approved requests are added to the execution waiting list, and the inventory is updated when the user receives the requested asset. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Inventory Admin displays pending request list. Response: System shows only requests within the admin's privilege scope. Admin selects a request and approves it. System confirms approval, sends email notification to the user, and adds the request to the execution waiting list.
Stimulus: Inventory Admin selects a request and rejects it. Response: System confirms rejection and sends email notification to the requesting user with the rejection status.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display only pending requests that the authenticated administrative user has privileges to approve.
The system shall support both approval and rejection of pending requests.
The system shall send email notifications to users when their requests are approved or rejected.
The system shall add approved requests to the waiting for execution list.
The system shall update the inventory database when the user receives the requested asset.

systemFeatures.name: Asset Return Processing
systemFeatures.description: This feature enables inventory administrators to process returned assets by updating their status in the inventory system. When an asset is returned, the inventory user inspects the asset and sets its status to either available (if in good condition) or damaged (if the asset has been damaged during the borrowing period). Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Inventory Admin initiates asset return process. Response: System presents the return interface. Admin selects asset status as 'available'. System updates inventory to reflect the asset's available status.
Stimulus: Inventory Admin initiates asset return for a damaged item. Response: Admin selects asset status as 'damaged'. System updates inventory to reflect the damaged status.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow inventory administrators to process asset returns by updating the asset status.
The system shall support two return status options: available and damaged.
The system shall update the inventory database immediately upon return status confirmation.

systemFeatures.name: Authentication and Authorization
systemFeatures.description: This feature implements the authentication system using username and password credentials for all users. Administrative level users are required to use designated administration computers. The system enforces hierarchical role-based access control across five user levels (0-4) with 32 permissions organized in 8 categories. Permission delegation allows higher-level administrators to share their privileges with other users who then acquire the inventory administrator role. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User enters username and password and clicks login. Response: System authenticates credentials, logs user into the application main page with their assigned role and permissions.
Stimulus: User enters incorrect credentials. Response: System displays authentication failure error message.

Stimulus: Administrator selects a user and modifies their permissions. Response: System validates that the administrator is not assigning permissions beyond their own scope, applies changes, and confirms the permission modification.
Stimulus: Administrator attempts to assign permissions exceeding their own level. Response: System displays an error message preventing the escalation.
functionalRequirements: The system shall authenticate all users via username and password.
The system shall enforce five user levels (0-4) with hierarchical permission inheritance where each level inherits all permissions from the level below.
The system shall manage 32 permissions across 8 categories: request (5), asset (4), location (5), universityPart (5), search (2), report (2), user (3), and audit (2).
The system shall allow levels 1, 2, and 3 to delegate some or all of their permissions to any user.
The system shall prevent administrators from assigning permissions beyond their own authorization scope.
The system shall require administrative level users to authenticate from designated administration computers.
Level 4 (IT) users shall have all possible permissions and the ability to create and assign new permission groups.

systemFeatures.name: Search and Reporting
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides authorized users with basic and advanced search capabilities across the inventory, plus three types of output reports. Basic search uses simple text queries, while advanced search provides multiple field-based filtering. Reports include Asset by Location, Request Report, and User Permission Report, all supporting filtering and field sorting. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Authorized user fills in the search field and submits. Response: System executes the search and displays matching results.
Stimulus: User clicks advanced search, fills multiple fields, and submits. Response: System executes the advanced search with multiple criteria and displays results.
Stimulus: User searches but no results match. Response: System displays 'no results found' message.
Stimulus: User clicks on a report type. Response: System generates the general report, which can be filtered and sorted by fields.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide basic search functionality accessible to authorized users.
The system shall provide advanced search with multiple field-based filtering criteria.
The system shall display appropriate messages when no search results are found.
The system shall generate Asset by Location reports with filtering and sorting capabilities.
The system shall generate Request reports with filtering and sorting capabilities.
The system shall generate User Permission reports with filtering and sorting capabilities.
The system shall restrict search and report access based on user permissions (search:simple, search:advanced, report:list, report:show).

systemFeatures.name: Location and Space Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature enables IT group members to create new locations and modify floor structures within the inventory system. New space creation and floor structure modifications are initiated through exception requests from administrative level users, processed exclusively by IT (Level 4) personnel. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Administrative user submits an exception request for a new space or floor structure change. Response: IT group member receives the request, creates the new space or modifies the floor structure, and updates the inventory system.
functionalRequirements: The system shall allow IT group members to create new spaces in the inventory system.
The system shall allow IT group members to modify floor structures when processing exception requests from administrative levels.
The system shall restrict location creation and modification to IT group (Level 4) members only.

performanceRequirements: Queries that take more than 1 minute to execute shall be automatically terminated to prevent system resource exhaustion.
safetyRequirements: Backup operations shall be executed periodically to protect against data loss.
Backup operations shall be conducted outside of working hours to avoid impacting system availability.
securityRequirements: All users shall be authenticated by username and password before accessing any system functionality.
Permissions shall be assigned to users according to their organizational roles within the 5-level hierarchy.
Only IT team members shall be allowed to access and maintain database servers, restricted to local access only.
Administrative level users shall authenticate from designated administration computers.
softwareQualityAttributes: Usability: Learning time for the system shall be between 2 to 4 hours maximum, as many tasks are delegated to working students. The web interface shall use clear and consistent terminology so that users with basic internet and office experience find the application easy to use.
Availability: The application shall be available at all times during working hours. Maintenance and backup operations shall be conducted outside of working time.

Portability: The application shall be installable on both Microsoft Windows and Unix platforms. The web application shall be compatible with Internet Explorer, Firefox, Chrome, Opera, and Safari browsers.
Maintainability: The system shall be designed to facilitate future evolution and simplify maintenance operations.
businessRules: Permission inheritance follows the organizational hierarchy: Level 1 controls department resources, Level 2 inherits Level 1 and controls faculty resources, Level 3 inherits Level 2 and controls university resources, Level 4 has all permissions.
Permission delegation cannot exceed the delegator's own permission scope.
Intra-department transfers do not require approval workflows; all cross-boundary transfers require hierarchical approval.
Asset IDs are immutable once created.

otherRequirements: COCOMO cost estimation: 3-month project (14 weeks), 8 people at 10 hours/week = 1120 hours (7 person-months), estimated 1.078 KSLOC, pricing at $20/hour, total project cost of $22,400.

glossary.definition: Unified University Inventory System, the integrated web-based inventory management platform for managing assets across multiple university faculties and departments.
The organizational entity requesting the UUIS system, responsible for integrating three faculty databases.
Department Administrator, a Level 1 user who controls inventory assets and locations within their department.
A delegated role assigned by administrative level users. Permissions vary based on assigned tasks and delegating authority.
A file-based method for adding or modifying multiple inventory assets simultaneously rather than individually.
A special request type used for non-standard operations such as creating new asset types or modifying floor structures, processed by IT team.
COnstructive COst MOdel, a software cost estimation model used to estimate the project effort, schedule, and cost based on source lines of code (KSLOC).
Thousands of Source Lines of Code, the unit used in COCOMO cost estimation.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This flowchart illustrates the complete asset request lifecycle from creation through multi-level approval to execution and return, showing the three request form types and the hierarchical approval chain based on transfer scope.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    UserLogin("User Authenticates") --> SelectForm{"Select Request Type"}
    SelectForm -->|Level 0| BasicForm["Fill Basic Request Form"]
    SelectForm -->|Level 1-3| AdvancedForm["Fill Advanced Form with Search"]
    SelectForm -->|Exception| ExceptionForm["Fill Exception Request Form"]
    BasicForm --> SubmitRequest["Submit Request"]
    AdvancedForm --> AddMore{"Add Another Asset?"}
    AddMore -->|Yes| AdvancedForm
    AddMore -->|No| SubmitRequest
    ExceptionForm --> SubmitRequest
    SubmitRequest --> PendingQueue["Request Added to Pending Queue"]
    PendingQueue --> DetermineScope{"Transfer Scope?"}
    DetermineScope -->|Within Department| DirectUpdate["Direct DB Update - No Approval"]
    DetermineScope -->|Inter-Department| DAApproval["DA + Faculty Approval Required"]
    DetermineScope -->|Inter-Faculty| FacultyApproval["Faculty or Higher Approval Required"]
    DetermineScope -->|Outside University| UniApproval["University Group Approval Required"]
    DetermineScope -->|Exception/IT| ITApproval["IT Level 4 Processing"]
    DAApproval --> ReviewRequest{"Approved?"}
    FacultyApproval --> ReviewRequest
    UniApproval --> ReviewRequest
    ITApproval --> ReviewRequest
    ReviewRequest -->|Rejected| NotifyRejection["Email Rejection Notification"]
    ReviewRequest -->|Approved| NotifyApproval["Email Approval Notification"]
    NotifyApproval --> ExecutionList["Add to Execution Waiting List"]
    ExecutionList --> AssetReceived["User Receives Asset"]
    AssetReceived --> InventoryUpdated["Inventory Database Updated"]
    DirectUpdate --> InventoryUpdated
    InventoryUpdated --> ReturnAsset{"Asset Returned?"}
    ReturnAsset -->|Good Condition| StatusAvailable["Set Status: Available"]
    ReturnAsset -->|Damaged| StatusDamaged["Set Status: Damaged"]
    StatusAvailable --> InventoryFinal("Inventory Cycle Complete")
    StatusDamaged --> InventoryFinal
flowchartDiagram.caption: Asset Request Lifecycle from Creation to Return

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This sequence diagram shows the interaction between a User, the Authentication System, the Inventory System, an Approving Administrator, and the Email Service during a complete asset borrowing request lifecycle from login through request creation, approval, asset receipt, and return.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant User as User Level 0-3
    participant Auth as Authentication System
    participant Inv as Inventory System
    participant Admin as Approving Admin
    participant Email as Email Service
    participant DB as Database

    User->>Auth: Enter Username and Password
    Auth->>DB: Validate Credentials
    DB-->>Auth: Authentication Result
    Auth-->>User: Login Success - Display Main Page

    User->>Inv: Select Request Form Type
    Inv-->>User: Display Appropriate Form
    User->>Inv: Fill Form and Submit
    Inv->>DB: Create Pending Request
    DB-->>Inv: Request ID Generated
    Inv-->>User: Request Submitted - Pending Approval

    Admin->>Auth: Authenticate on Admin Computer
    Auth-->>Admin: Login Success
    Admin->>Inv: View Pending Request List
    Inv->>DB: Fetch Requests Within Admin Scope
    DB-->>Inv: Return Filtered Pending Requests
    Inv-->>Admin: Display Approvable Requests

    Admin->>Inv: Approve Selected Request
    Inv->>DB: Update Request Status to Approved
    Inv->>DB: Add to Execution Waiting List
    Inv->>Email: Send Approval Notification
    Email-->>User: Approval Email Received

    Note over User,DB: Asset is physically delivered to User

    User->>Inv: Confirm Asset Received
    Inv->>DB: Update Inventory - Asset Assigned to User

    Note over User,DB: Borrowing period elapses

    Admin->>Inv: Process Asset Return
    Inv-->>Admin: Display Return Status Options
    Admin->>Inv: Set Status Available or Damaged
    Inv->>DB: Update Asset Status in Inventory
    DB-->>Inv: Inventory Updated
    Inv-->>Admin: Return Confirmed
sequenceDiagram.caption: Asset Borrowing Request Lifecycle Sequence
