revisionHistory.description: Initial release of the THEMAS Software Requirements Specification
revisionHistory.author: THEMAS Team

introduction.purpose:
This Software Requirements Specification (SRS) specifies the qualification requirements for **The Energy Management System (THEMAS)**. It provides a technical description of all software requirements of the system, provides traceability of software capability requirements to the Statement of Work, and identifies external interfaces. This document is intended for use by the Design Requirements team, Principle Software Architect, and other parties interested in the development and progression of THEMAS.

Document Number: D_THEMAS_SRS_001.
introduction.documentConventions: This document is prepared in accordance with ANSI/IEEE STD 830-1984, the American National Standards Institute / Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers Guide to Software Requirements Specifications. Only conditions expressed with the imperative 'shall' are to be interpreted as binding requirements. The document uses a hierarchical numbering scheme (e.g., SRS-001 through SRS-018) for individual requirements. Each requirement follows a structured format with Introduction, Inputs, Processing, and Outputs subsections. In the event of conflict between referenced documents and this SRS, this document shall overrule all others except the Statement of Work. The SRS includes appendices for Traceability Matrix, Dataflow Diagrams (Levels 0-2), and Data Dictionary.
introduction.intendedAudience: This document is intended for the Design Requirements team who will create the software design from these requirements, the Principle Software Architect responsible for system design oversight, the Principle Requirements Architect and Principle Design Architect who approve the SRS, the Principle Implementation Architect who approves the SRS, and other parties interested in the development and progression of THEMAS.
introduction.productScope:
**THEMAS (The Energy Management System)** is an embedded building energy management system that monitors and controls the heating and cooling units in a multi-story office building. The scope is confined to only the software requirements; hardware requirements are contained in a separate system specification.

The system manages heating/cooling (H/C) units across a 3-floor office building, with each floor divided into 4 quadrants. Each quadrant has one thermostat paired with one H/C unit (12 thermostats and 12 H/C units total). The software monitors temperature data from thermostats, determines when H/C units should be activated based on trigger and overtemperature thresholds, manages utilization by limiting concurrently running units, generates alarms for out-of-range conditions, logs all events to a database, and provides operational history and statistical reports.

The SRS also provides a prototype of a simulated thermostat for verification and validation of THEMAS reliability.
references: THEMAS_SOW_001: Statement of Work
ANSI/IEEE STD 830-1984: Guide to Software Requirements Specifications

overallDescription.productPerspective: The THEMAS system operates independently of any other system or any components of the heating and cooling system to which it is attached. It is composed of both hardware and software portions, with this SRS specifying only the software requirements. The system interfaces with external thermostats (which provide temperature values and settings) and external heating/cooling units (which receive on/off control signals but provide no feedback). The system runs on a Microsoft Windows NT operating system and stores event data in a Microsoft Access database on the supervisor's computer.
productFunctions: Monitor Temperature: Receives valid temperature and system parameters, determines temperature status through trigger and overtemperature comparisons, and issues H/C requests when conditions warrant activation.

Determine Utilization: Receives H/C requests and utilization parameters, manages the maximum number of concurrently running units via a LIFO queue, and generates unit unavailable events or approved H/C signals.

Initialize System: Loads all operational parameters from initialization files including H/C unit definitions, thermostat definitions, utilization parameters, trigger values, overtemperature values, and valid temperature ranges. Establishes a known state by turning off all units first, then evaluating if any need activation.

System Reports: Stores all system events in a database and provides two report types: operational history for the past 12 months and monthly statistical summaries including per-unit on-time percentages and request grant/deny ratios.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: Primary user responsible for maintaining the heating and cooling systems in the building. Monitors system status, receives alarm notifications, changes thermostat settings, and reviews operational/statistical reports. The system should not require intervention from outside users other than the supervisor. Interface should not contain excessive information which could confuse the supervisor. Should provide information in the form of reports so the system can be run efficiently.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The THEMAS system runs on Microsoft Windows NT operating system. All internal process communications are designed for this OS. Communication with thermostats and heating/cooling units is through hardware interfaces that also run on this OS. The system manages a 3-floor office building with 4 quadrants per floor, totaling 12 thermostat-H/C unit pairs. Event data is stored in a Microsoft Access database on the supervisor's computer.
designAndImplementationConstraints: Thermostats shall only provide temperature values and temperature settings — no other data.
Heating and cooling units provide no feedback to the THEMAS system; when a signal is sent to a unit, no confirmation signal is available to determine if the signal was realized.
All internal process communications shall be designed for Microsoft Windows NT.
Communication with thermostats and H/C units shall be through defined hardware interfaces running on Windows NT.
Temperature values must be validated against a configurable valid temperature range before processing.
The maximum number of concurrently running H/C units is configurable through initialization parameters.
userDocumentation: Reports generated as ASCII files with user-selectable location and filename.
Two report types: operational history (12 months) and monthly statistical summary.
assumptionsAndDependencies: The thermostat is capable of returning the current temperature and the current desired temperature setting to the THEMAS system with no real-time delay.
The thermostat can be set and controlled by a user of the THEMAS system.
All data sent by the thermostat is in the correct format for the THEMAS system to use.
The heating/cooling unit is incapable of returning its current on/off status to the THEMAS system.
The heating/cooling unit has no real-time delay when receiving status signals from the THEMAS system.
The heating/cooling unit shall have the capability of being turned off and on by the supervisor.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The supervisor's interface shall display available thermostats and their individual current temperature settings. The supervisor shall be able to select a thermostat and choose a new temperature value from a list of valid temperatures. The interface shall provide mechanisms to select between operational history reports and statistical summary reports. Alarm conditions are presented as audible beeps on the supervisor's computer with different frequency patterns for different alarm types. The supervisor manually resets alarms through the interface window. The system should not display excessive information that could confuse the supervisor.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The THEMAS system interfaces with two types of external hardware: (1) **Thermostats** — one per quadrant per floor (12 total), each with a unique identifier, capable of returning current temperature (T) and current temperature setting (TSET) continuously with no real-time delay; (2) **Heating/Cooling Units** — one per thermostat (12 total), receiving on/off control signals but incapable of returning their current status. The H/C unit control signal interface has not yet been defined (TBD). Each quadrant on each of three floors has exactly one thermostat paired with one H/C unit.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The THEMAS software interfaces with: (1) **Microsoft Windows NT** — operating system for all internal process communications and hardware interface drivers; (2) **Microsoft Access** — database residing on the supervisor's computer for recording all system events with descriptions and timestamps, used as the data source for generating operational and statistical reports; (3) **Initialization File** — contains H/C unit definitions, thermostat definitions (unique identifiers), utilization parameters (max concurrent units), trigger delta values (TD), overtemperature delta values (OD), and valid temperature range values.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: Internal communications between THEMAS software processes are through Windows NT interprocess communication mechanisms. External communications with thermostats and H/C units are through hardware-specific interfaces running on the Windows NT operating system. The thermostat interface provides continuous data flow with no real-time delay. The H/C unit interface is one-directional (control signals outbound only, no feedback inbound).

systemFeatures.name: Initialize Operational Parameters
systemFeatures.description: Loads all configuration data from the initialization file to establish the THEMAS operational environment. This includes H/C unit definitions (which units exist), thermostat definitions (unique identifiers), utilization parameters (maximum concurrent units), trigger delta values, overtemperature delta values, and valid temperature range boundaries. This data forms the Operational Parameters used by all other system functions. Priority: Critical.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: System startup initiated. Response: THEMAS reads initialization file, loads all 6 parameter sets (H/C definitions, thermostat definitions, utilization parameters, trigger values, overtemp values, valid temperature range), and stores them as operational parameters.
functionalRequirements: SRS-001: The system shall read the initialization data file to determine which heating and cooling units are part of the THEMAS system. There is one H/C unit corresponding to one thermostat in each of four quadrants on each of three floors.
SRS-002: Each thermostat shall have a unique identifier loaded from the initialization file during system initialization.
SRS-003: The maximum number of heating or cooling units that can run concurrently shall be read from the initialization file and stored.
SRS-004: Trigger values (TD) shall be read from the initialization file and established for use in temperature monitoring.
SRS-005: Overtemperature values (OD) shall be read from the initialization file. The temperature reported by a thermostat shall not exceed a maximum deviation of 3 degrees Fahrenheit.
SRS-006: The valid temperature range shall be read from the initialization file and established for temperature validation.

systemFeatures.name: System Initialization
systemFeatures.description: Establishes a known operational state for the entire THEMAS system by first turning off all H/C units, then evaluating each thermostat to determine if any units need activation based on current conditions. This ensures a safe, deterministic starting state. Priority: Critical.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: THEMAS system initialization command issued (after operational parameters loaded). Response: System turns off all H/C units, reads current temperature and settings from each thermostat, evaluates conditions per SRS-009 and SRS-010 rules, and issues H/C requests for any thermostats requiring activation.
functionalRequirements: SRS-007: When the system is initialized, it shall first turn off all heating and cooling units, then check all thermostats and determine if any thermostat's settings require a H/C unit to be turned back on, following the rules in SRS-009 and SRS-010.

systemFeatures.name: Temperature Validation
systemFeatures.description: Validates all temperature data received from thermostats against the configured valid temperature range before allowing it into the system. Both current temperature values and temperature setting values are validated. Invalid temperatures trigger alarm conditions. Priority: Critical.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Temperature value received from thermostat. Response: System compares value against valid range. If within range, outputs valid temperature status. If outside range, outputs invalid temperature status triggering alarm generation.

Stimulus: Temperature setting received from thermostat. Response: System compares setting against valid range. If within range, realizes the new setting. If outside range, outputs invalid temperature status.
functionalRequirements: SRS-008: A current temperature value strictly less than the lower bound or strictly greater than the upper bound of the valid range shall be identified as invalid and shall output an invalid temperature status. Otherwise, a valid temperature status is output.
SRS-008: A temperature setting value outside the valid range shall also be identified as invalid. Otherwise, the system shall realize the value for that thermostat's setting.

systemFeatures.name: Temperature Monitoring
systemFeatures.description: Continuously monitors validated temperatures from all thermostats and determines when heating or cooling action is needed. Uses a two-tier threshold system: trigger values (TD) determine when H/C units should activate, and overtemperature values (OD) determine when critical alarm conditions exist. Priority: Critical.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Valid temperature received where LO <= T <= UO (within overtemp range). Response: System checks trigger conditions — if LT <= T <= UT (within trigger range), outputs request to turn off both H/C units. If T is between trigger and overtemp limits, outputs request to turn on appropriate H/C unit.
Stimulus: Valid temperature received where T < LO or T > UO (outside overtemp range). Response: System outputs temperature limit exceeded condition, triggering alarm generation.
functionalRequirements: SRS-009: The system shall compare reported temperature to TSET using overtemperature delta (OD). If T < (TSET - OD) or T > (TSET + OD), the temperature limit exceeded condition shall be output.
SRS-009: If (TSET - OD) <= T <= (TSET + OD), the temperature status shall be output for trigger evaluation.
SRS-010: If (TSET - TD) <= T <= (TSET + TD) — condition 1 (satisfied) — the system shall request turning off both the heating and cooling units.

SRS-010: If (TSET - OD) <= T < (TSET - TD) — heating needed — the system shall request turning on the heating unit. If (TSET + TD) < T <= (TSET + OD) — cooling needed — the system shall request turning on the cooling unit.

systemFeatures.name: Utilization Management
systemFeatures.description: Manages the allocation of H/C units ensuring the maximum number of concurrently running units is not exceeded. Uses a LIFO queue to buffer denied requests and automatically processes queued requests when units become available. Generates events for denied requests to support statistical reporting. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: H/C request to turn on a unit when maximum concurrent units already running. Response: Request is added to the LIFO queue. A unit unavailable event is generated and recorded.

Stimulus: H/C request to turn off a unit. Response: Unit is turned off. System checks LIFO queue — if not empty, removes one request, re-evaluates the thermostat's current need, and if still needed, submits request to turn that unit on.
Stimulus: H/C request to turn on a unit when capacity available. Response: System generates an approved request to turn on the unit.
functionalRequirements: SRS-011: The system shall maintain the ON/OFF status of each H/C unit. When a turn-on request is received and the maximum number is already running, the request shall be added to a LIFO queue.

SRS-011: When a turn-off request is received, the system shall check the LIFO queue. If not empty, it shall remove one request, check if that thermostat still needs H/C, and if so, submit a request to turn that unit on.

SRS-012: When a request is denied, a system event shall be generated containing the event type (request denied), the requesting thermostat designation, and the H/C unit designation that was not turned on.
SRS-013: When a request to turn on or off a H/C unit is approved, the system shall generate a request including the unit designation and an on/off flag.

systemFeatures.name: H/C Signal Generation
systemFeatures.description: Translates approved H/C requests into actual control signals for the hardware units and provides status feedback to the requesting thermostat. Also generates system events for operational history tracking each time a unit's status changes. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Approved H/C request received. Response: System generates the appropriate hardware control signal (heating or cooling, on or off), outputs unit status information back to the requesting thermostat, and generates a system event recording the status change.
functionalRequirements: SRS-014: The system shall recognize the requested H/C unit and ON/OFF status and generate the necessary hardware control signal. The signal interface is designated TBD.
SRS-014: The system shall output the status information of the H/C unit back to the requesting thermostat.
SRS-014: The system shall generate a system event each time a change is made to the status of a H/C unit, containing the unit identification.

systemFeatures.name: Alarm Generation
systemFeatures.description: Generates audible alarm notifications on the supervisor's computer for two distinct alarm conditions: invalid temperature (sensor malfunction) and temperature limit exceeded (overtemperature). Each alarm type has a distinctive audio pattern to allow the supervisor to identify the alarm type by sound. Alarms persist until manually acknowledged. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Invalid temperature detected from a thermostat. Response: System outputs continuous alternating 500 Hz and 700 Hz beeps with 3/4-second duration each until supervisor manually resets.
Stimulus: Temperature limit exceeded on a thermostat. Response: System outputs continuous alternating 1000 Hz and 1500 Hz beeps with 1/2-second duration each until supervisor manually resets.
functionalRequirements: SRS-015: Invalid temperature shall trigger continuous alternating 500 Hz and 700 Hz beeps on the supervisor's computer, each with 3/4-second duration, continuing until manually reset.
SRS-015: Temperature limit exceeded shall trigger continuous alternating 1000 Hz and 1500 Hz beeps on the supervisor's computer, each with 1/2-second duration, continuing until manually reset.
SRS-015: Each alarm request shall generate an alarm event recorded for operational and statistical reports.

systemFeatures.name: Event Management
systemFeatures.description: Records all system events (alarm events, H/C status changes, denied requests) in a Microsoft Access database with descriptions and timestamps. Provides the data foundation for the reporting subsystem. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Any system event occurs (alarm, H/C status change, denied request). Response: System identifies event type, formats appropriate message with description and current system time, and records it in the Access database.
functionalRequirements: SRS-016: The system shall identify each event type and format an appropriate event message containing a description and the current system time.
SRS-016: All events shall be recorded in a Microsoft Access database residing on the supervisor's computer.

systemFeatures.name: Thermostat Setting Control
systemFeatures.description: Provides the supervisor with a mechanism to remotely change the temperature setting of any thermostat in the system through the supervisor interface. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Supervisor selects a thermostat and chooses a new temperature setting. Response: System updates the thermostat's setting to the selected value, which then triggers normal temperature monitoring processing.
functionalRequirements: SRS-017: The supervisor's interface shall display available thermostats and their current temperature settings.
SRS-017: The supervisor shall be able to select a thermostat and choose a new temperature value from a list of valid temperatures.

systemFeatures.name: Report Generation
systemFeatures.description: Provides two types of reports for the supervisor: operational history (all events sorted chronologically for the past 12 months) and monthly statistical summaries (per-unit on-time percentages and per-thermostat request grant/deny ratios). Reports are generated as ASCII files with user-selectable location and name. Priority: Medium.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Supervisor selects operational history report. Response: System retrieves all events from the database, sorts by date/time, and generates an ASCII report file at the supervisor-specified location.

Stimulus: Supervisor selects statistical summary and chooses a month. Response: System generates an ASCII report with per-unit daily on-time percentages and per-thermostat request grant/deny ratios for the selected month.
functionalRequirements: SRS-018: The supervisor shall be able to select between an operational history report and a statistical summary report.
SRS-018: Reports shall be ASCII files with user-selectable location and filename.
SRS-018: Operational history reports shall contain all events sorted by date and time.

SRS-018: Statistical reports shall contain: (1) the percentage of time each H/C unit was on for each day of the selected month, and (2) for each thermostat, the ratio of granted to denied H/C requests.

performanceRequirements: Temperature data from thermostats shall be received continuously with no real-time delay.
H/C signal transmission shall have no real-time delay to the units.
The LIFO queue shall process deferred requests immediately upon unit availability.
safetyRequirements: The system shall detect and alarm on invalid temperatures that may indicate sensor malfunction.
The system shall detect and alarm on temperatures exceeding the overtemperature threshold (3°F deviation from setting), indicating the H/C unit cannot satisfy the temperature requirement.
All H/C units shall be turned off during initialization to establish a known safe state before re-evaluation.
securityRequirements: Access to thermostat setting changes shall be limited to the building supervisor.
Reports and system event data shall reside on the supervisor's computer.
softwareQualityAttributes: Reliability: The SRS provides a prototype of a simulated thermostat for verification and validation of THEMAS reliability.
Traceability: All 18 requirements (SRS-001 through SRS-018) are traced to Statement of Work paragraphs via the Traceability Matrix (Appendix A).
Maintainability: System parameters are configurable through initialization files, avoiding hardcoded values.
Auditability: All system events are timestamped and recorded in a database for operational history review.

otherRequirements: The THEMAS system shall be designed to run on Microsoft Windows NT.
All internal process communications shall be designed for the Windows NT operating system.
Communication with thermostats and H/C units shall be through hardware interfaces running on Windows NT.

glossary.definition: American National Standards Institute.
Cooling — refers to the cooling function or cooling unit in the H/C system.
Database — the Microsoft Access database used for event storage.
Data Dictionary — Appendix C of this SRS defining all data items.
Data Flow Diagram — visual representations of data flows in the system (Appendix B).
Heating — refers to the heating function or heating unit in the H/C system.
Heating/Cooling — refers to either or both heating and cooling units controlled by THEMAS.
Lower Overtemperature Value — calculated as TSET minus OD; temperature below this triggers a limit exceeded alarm.
Lower Trigger Value — calculated as TSET minus TD; temperature below this triggers a heating request.
Overtemperature Delta Value — the maximum deviation from TSET before an alarm condition (3°F).
Software Design Document.
Software Requirements Specification.
Current Temperature Value — the actual temperature reported by a thermostat.
Trigger Delta Value — the deviation from TSET that triggers an H/C unit activation request.
The Energy Management System — the complete building energy management software and hardware system.
Current Temperature Setting — the desired temperature value set on a thermostat.
Upper Trigger Value — calculated as TSET plus TD; temperature above this triggers a cooling request.
Upper Overtemperature Value — calculated as TSET plus OD; temperature above this triggers a limit exceeded alarm.
Last In, First Out queue used to buffer denied H/C requests when maximum concurrent units are already running.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This flowchart shows the complete temperature monitoring and H/C unit control decision process, from thermostat data receipt through validation, trigger evaluation, utilization management, and signal generation.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    ThermData["Thermostat Sends Temperature Data"] --> Validate{"Validate Temperature"}
    Validate -->|Invalid| AlarmInvalid["Generate Invalid Temp Alarm"]
    AlarmInvalid --> Beeps500["Alternating 500/700 Hz Beeps"]
    Beeps500 --> ManualReset["Supervisor Manually Resets"]
    Validate -->|Valid| CheckOvertemp{"Check Overtemp Limits"}
    CheckOvertemp -->|"T < LO or T > UO"| AlarmLimit["Generate Limit Exceeded Alarm"]
    AlarmLimit --> Beeps1000["Alternating 1000/1500 Hz Beeps"]
    Beeps1000 --> ManualReset
    CheckOvertemp -->|"LO <= T <= UO"| CheckTrigger{"Check Trigger Values"}
    CheckTrigger -->|"LT <= T <= UT"| TurnOff["Request Turn Off Both H/C Units"]
    CheckTrigger -->|"T < LT"| HeatReq["Request Turn On Heating Unit"]
    CheckTrigger -->|"T > UT"| CoolReq["Request Turn On Cooling Unit"]
    HeatReq --> CheckUtil{"Max Units Running?"}
    CoolReq --> CheckUtil
    TurnOff --> GenSignal["Generate H/C Signal"]
    CheckUtil -->|No| ApproveReq["Approve H/C Request"]
    CheckUtil -->|Yes| QueueReq["Add to LIFO Queue"]
    QueueReq --> GenEvent["Generate Unit Unavailable Event"]
    ApproveReq --> GenSignal
    GenSignal --> UnitSignal["Send H/C Unit Signal"]
    GenSignal --> StatusFeedback["Update Thermostat Status"]
    GenSignal --> LogEvent["Record System Event"]
flowchartDiagram.caption: THEMAS Temperature Monitoring and H/C Control Decision Flow

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This sequence diagram shows the interaction between the Supervisor, Thermostat, THEMAS Core System, H/C Unit, and Event Database during a complete temperature monitoring cycle including a denied request scenario.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Therm as Thermostat
    participant Val as Validate Temperature
    participant Mon as Monitor Temperature
    participant Util as Determine Utilization
    participant Sig as Generate H/C Signal
    participant HC as H/C Unit
    participant DB as Access Database
    participant Sup as Supervisor

    Therm->>Val: Temperature Data (T, TSET)
    Val->>Val: Compare T against valid range
    alt Valid Temperature
        Val->>Mon: Valid Temperature
        Mon->>Mon: Calculate LO, UO, LT, UT
        alt LT <= T <= UT - Satisfied
            Mon->>Util: Request Turn Off H/C
            Util->>Sig: Approved Request OFF
            Sig->>HC: Turn Off Signal
            Sig->>DB: Record Status Change Event
        else T < LT - Heating Needed
            Mon->>Util: Request Turn On Heating
            alt Capacity Available
                Util->>Sig: Approved Request ON Heating
                Sig->>HC: Turn On Heating Signal
                Sig->>Therm: Unit Status Update
                Sig->>DB: Record Status Change Event
            else Max Units Running
                Util->>Util: Add to LIFO Queue
                Util->>DB: Unit Unavailable Event
            end
        else T > UO - Limit Exceeded
            Mon->>Sup: Temperature Limit Alarm
            Note over Sup: 1000/1500 Hz Beeps
            Mon->>DB: Alarm Event
        end
    else Invalid Temperature
        Val->>Sup: Invalid Temperature Alarm
        Note over Sup: 500/700 Hz Beeps
        Val->>DB: Alarm Event
    end
sequenceDiagram.caption: THEMAS Temperature Monitoring Cycle with Utilization Management

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This ER diagram shows the relationships between key entities: Building structure (Floors/Quadrants), Thermostat/H/C pairs, Operational Parameters, Events, and Reports.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    FLOOR ||--|{ QUADRANT : "has"
    QUADRANT ||--|| THERMOSTAT : "contains"
    QUADRANT ||--|| HC_UNIT : "contains"
    THERMOSTAT ||--|| HC_UNIT : "paired_with"
    THERMOSTAT ||--o{ TEMPERATURE_READING : "produces"
    THERMOSTAT ||--o{ HC_REQUEST : "generates"
    HC_UNIT ||--o{ HC_SIGNAL : "receives"
    HC_REQUEST ||--o| LIFO_QUEUE : "queued_in"
    HC_REQUEST ||--o{ SYSTEM_EVENT : "triggers"
    HC_SIGNAL ||--o{ SYSTEM_EVENT : "generates"
    ALARM ||--o{ SYSTEM_EVENT : "generates"
    SYSTEM_EVENT ||--o{ REPORT : "included_in"
    INIT_FILE ||--|| OPERATIONAL_PARAMS : "loads"

    FLOOR {
        int floor_number PK
        int quadrant_count
    }
    QUADRANT {
        string quadrant_id PK
        int floor_number FK
        string position
    }
    THERMOSTAT {
        string thermostat_id PK
        string quadrant_id FK
        float current_temp
        float temp_setting
    }
    HC_UNIT {
        string unit_id PK
        string thermostat_id FK
        string type
        string status
    }
    OPERATIONAL_PARAMS {
        int max_concurrent_units
        float trigger_delta
        float overtemp_delta
        float valid_range_lower
        float valid_range_upper
    }
    SYSTEM_EVENT {
        int event_id PK
        string event_type
        string description
        datetime timestamp
    }
    REPORT {
        string report_id PK
        string report_type
        string file_path
        datetime generated_at
    }
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: THEMAS System Entity Relationships

tbdList: The interface to control the heating/cooling units has not been defined — the signal generation process is designated TBD (SRS-014).

traceabilityMatrix.description: Determine Status of All H/C Units

revisionHistory.description: Draft Software Requirements Specification for DII COE XML Services, prepared for Defense Information Systems Agency (DISA)
revisionHistory.author: DII COE AOG Data Access Services Technical Working Group (DATATWG), Semi-Structured Data and Metadata Sub-panel (SSD-MD), Jim Pipher & Jim Schoening, Co-Chairs

introduction.purpose: This Software Requirements Specification (SRS) establishes the functional, performance, and verification requirements for the **XML Services (XS)** functional area of the **Defense Information Infrastructure (DII) Common Operating Environment (COE)**. The XS functional area includes Registration of terms (tags and structures), Design of XML schemas, and Generation and Consumption of XML documents. This document outlines software capabilities required for XS components for the DII COE.
introduction.documentConventions: This SRS follows MIL-STD-498 (Software Development and Documentation, DOD, Dec 1994) conventions. Data Item Description DI-IPSC-81433 is referenced for detailed interface specifications. Section 2 lists applicable documents. Section 3 provides functional capabilities. Section 4 identifies qualification provisions. Section 5 outlines requirements and verification traceability matrix. Section 6 contains applicable notes. The precedence and criticality codes used are: 1 = Essential (E), 2 = Desirable (D), 3 = Optional (O). Qualification methods include Test (T), Demonstration (D), Analysis (A), and Inspection (I).
introduction.intendedAudience: This document is intended for DOD system developers integrating XML Services into DII COE-compliant systems, the Defense Information Systems Agency (DISA) as the sponsoring agency, the DISA Configuration Manager responsible for distribution of the XS product, data consumers and data/database managers who will use XS services, network information infrastructure resource managers, the COE Chief Engineer who approves Communities of Interest point-of-contacts, and the COE Data Engineering Team responsible for tag consolidation and analysis.
introduction.productScope:
**DII COE XML Services (XS)** provide infrastructure services for mission and support applications using XML technology within the U.S. Department of Defense. These services isolate vendor-unique implementations of data access and provide applications a means of avoiding dependencies on physical access models. XS provides data management functions for access to distributed (local and remote) database management systems as one of the solutions for interoperability.

The XS functional area delivers three inter-related service groups: (1) Register Terms and Structures — managing an XML Registry for tags, DTDs, DCDs, and associated metadata; (2) Design XML Schemas — structure and content services for creating and validating XML document schemas; (3) Generate and Consume XML Documents — production and parsing of XML documents with conformance checking. The system interfaces with COTS RDBMS products (Sybase, Oracle, Informix) through the DBIF and DAC layers.
references: DII Shared Data Environment (SHADE) CAPSTONE DOCUMENT Version 1.0, 11 July 1996
ACCS-A1-100-006: System Specification for ATCCS, 22 March 1995
ATCCS-A1-302-001A: Army Tactical Command and Control System CASS Systems/Segment Specification
CDRL A142: AWIS Software Requirements Specification (ASRD), July 1992
CDRL ML03: AWIS Systems Management Manual (SMM), 31 Oct 1994
D18664A: STACCS System Design Document Version 1.1/As Built, 1 Oct 1993
AAN-SDA001A: STACCS System Specification, Version 1.1/As Built, 1 Oct 1993
GCCS Integration Standard, Version 1.0, October 1994
GCCS Common Operating Environment Baseline, DISA, 28 November 1994
User Interface Specifications for GCCS, Version 1.0, October 1994
Draft Architectural Design Document for GCCS COE, Version 3, 24 July 1994
MIL-STD-498: Military Standard — Software Development and Documentation, DOD, Dec. 1994
FIPS PUB 127-2: Database Language SQL, 2 June 1993
MIL-STD-6040: Military Standard
ISO/IEC 9070: Formal Public Identifier
W3C Working Draft: Namespaces in XML
Donald Lewine, 'POSIX Programmer's Guide', O'Reilly & Associates, Inc., 1991

overallDescription.productPerspective: The DII COE XML Services functional area operates within the Defense Information Infrastructure Common Operating Environment, a standardized computing platform for U.S. Department of Defense systems. XS provides the XML-based data interoperability layer that sits above commercial RDBMS products (Sybase, Oracle, Informix) and below mission and support applications. The system interfaces internally (transparent to operators) with existing data elements of the DBIF (Database Interface), DAC (Discretionary Access Control), and COTS RDBMS products. The Registry provides a centralized, authoritative source for approved XML data and metadata components across DOD Communities of Interest.
productFunctions: Register Terms and Structures: Manage an XML Registry for tags, DTDs, DCDs, and associated metadata through Producer, Consumer, and Manager services, supporting the full lifecycle of information resource submission, review, approval, and retirement.

Design XML Schemas: Provide Structure and Content services for recording schemas, validating against standards, referencing existing tags, creating new semantic structures, versioning metadata objects, and generating standard views.

Generate XML Documents: Create XML documents with schema references (by reference or value), generate language/charset values, check conformance (well-formed and valid), extract data views, disassemble documents for alternate query results, render documents, and transmit sufficient metadata for alternate semantically-valid views via XSL.

Consume XML Documents: Parse documents using DOM and SAX parsers, check conformance (well-formed and valid), create validation constraints for meaningful schema sharing, extract semantically-valid views via XSL, disassemble hierarchical views to relational representations, and render documents.
Process ANSI Standard SQL: Support SQL as specified in FIPS PUB 127-2 for registry data access.
Catalog entities and attributes within servers to enable browsing, searching, and retrieving of data related to XML sources.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: End users of XS services who examine the XML Registry to select tag structures for reuse in applications that exchange data according to pre-defined agreements. Need discovery, browsing, searching, and downloading capabilities. Interact through web-based interfaces.

Responsible for managing XML tags, schemas, and registry content. Review and approve submissions, monitor changes to data models, and manage information resource lifecycles across status levels (developmental, candidate, approved, rejected, retired).

Manages the broader infrastructure in which XS operates. Requires capabilities for system administration, network administration, and security administration through internal COE management services APIs.

Agents who contribute metadata for inclusion into the XML Registry. Must be able to receive, complete, and submit Information Resource Submittal Packages containing XML Tag Specifications, XML Specs (DTDs/DCDs), and sample XML documents. Can submit via online interactive or offline batch processes.
Approves single Points of Contact (POCs) for Communities of Interest (COIs) to manage tags within each COI. Reserves ultimate authority to mediate unresolved disputes within all COIs.
Analyzes tags and semantics to identify consolidation opportunities, reducing redundancy toward minimal representations. Uses a 'market forces' model to identify strong and weak tag candidates.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The XS software shall be portable and execute on COE-compliant platforms within the DII infrastructure. The system supports three COTS Relational Database engines: Sybase, Oracle, and Informix. The platform spans fixed (static) environments (base or data processing megacenter) where workloads and database sizes change frequently, and changing (dynamic) environments with deployed military units where workloads may be more predetermined. The system must operate in degraded communications environments where session time-out values need adjustment. The XS software must adhere to COE compliance level requirements described in the I&RTS (Integration & Runtime Specification).
designAndImplementationConstraints: XS shall be segmented per COE architecture requirements.
COE sponsors shall adhere to compliance level requirements described in the I&RTS.
XS shall process ANSI standard SQL as specified in FIPS PUB 127-2.
XS shall internally interface (transparent to the operator) with existing DBIF, DAC, and COTS RDBMS data elements.
Registry software shall be portable and execute on COE-compliant platforms.
Naming conventions shall not conflict with DOD data standards conventions and shall establish relationships to other naming conventions specified in I&RTS.
XML_Attributes should be used sparingly; well-recognized terms outside their container should be designated as elements.
Information resource definitions should include descriptive definitions AND synonyms for effective substring search.
All metadata recorded in the Registry must conform to ISO/IEC 9070 Formal Public Identifier standards.
userDocumentation: Personnel requirements shall be determined by the developers of the system in which the XS module is embedded.
Training requirements shall be determined by the developers of the system in which the XS module is embedded.
assumptionsAndDependencies: The system depends on COE-compliant platform availability for deployment.
COTS RDBMS products (Sybase, Oracle, or Informix) are available at deployment sites.
Communities of Interest (COIs) are established with designated POCs approved by the COE Chief Engineer.
The DOD data standards convention exists and is maintained separately from this system.
Network connectivity between fixed and deployed environments is available, though potentially degraded.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The XS provides web-based interfaces for XML Registry interaction. Producers receive and complete Information Resource Submittal Package forms from the web containing XML Tag Specifications, XML Specs (DTDs/DCDs), and sample documents. Packages are compressed and emailed or sent via FTP. Consumers browse and search the Registry through web pages — each Information Resource has its own web page displaying all pertinent information by resource type. Users can view XML tags via tree/hierarchy structure or tabular format, with multiple search options including substring search across names and definitions. Tags can be viewed by ancestor/descendant relationships, uses/used-by relationships, data type information, versioning relationships, reference sets, and amplifying information.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: Not explicitly specified. The system operates on COE-compliant hardware platforms spanning fixed data processing megacenters and deployed military units. Hardware interfaces to specific platform types are determined by the COE compliance requirements.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: Internal interfaces to COE components include: (1) Management Services API — incorporating Network Administration, System Administration, Security Administration; (2) Communications Services API — incorporating Communications and Network Services; (3) Distribution and Object Management Services — incorporating Distributed Computing Services and Data Interchange Services; (4) Presentation Services — incorporating Executive Manager and Multi-Media Support; (5) Common Support Applications — incorporating Office Automation, Message Processing, Correlation, MCG&I, Alerts, On-line Help; (6) Software Development Services — incorporating Developers Toolkit. External interfaces to non-COE components and commercial products (Sybase, Oracle, Informix) will be specified during the design phase.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The system must support operation in degraded communications environments with configurable session time-out values. Connect descriptors include fully qualified object names with address (protocol/host/port) and instance name. The XS shall have the ability to redefine or reset connect descriptor names to database server instances. Session time-out values shall be at minimum user-definable and resettable prior to session initialization, with a goal of dynamically changing values based on current network performance.

systemFeatures.name: XML Registry — Producer Services
systemFeatures.description: Enables authorized agents to contribute metadata to the XML Registry for ensuring maximum semantic understanding of XML tags across DOD systems. Producers submit Information Resource Submittal Packages containing tag specifications, XML specs, and sample documents through online interactive or offline batch processes. The registry supports submission, modification, association, assembly, and notification workflows. Priority: Essential.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Producer downloads Information Resource Submittal Package from the web. Response: System provides forms, instructions, and tools for preparing a submission.

Stimulus: Producer submits completed package via email or FTP. Response: System parses submitted forms, populates the Registry database, and routes the submission to the appropriate COI POC for review.
Stimulus: Producer modifies existing information resource metadata. Response: System updates the specified terms, definitions, and status in the Registry.
Stimulus: Information resource status changes. Response: System notifies subscribed parties of changes to Information Resources or authorized producers.
functionalRequirements: The system shall produce and display submittal forms as part of the Information Resource Submittal Package from the web.
The system shall support download of Information Resource Submittal Packages containing forms, instructions, and tools.
The system shall accept submission of prescribed metadata via online interactive process and offline batch process.
The system shall parse submitted XML Registry specification forms.
The system shall populate the XML Registry database with parsed submission data.
The system shall support modification of specified terms, definitions, and status of Information Resources.

The system shall associate Information Resources according to the defined association types (uses/used_by, describes/described_by, is_XML_spec_for/defined_by_XML_spec, versioning, domain, namespace, element/attribute, model).
The system shall assemble registered information resources to form new components, including assembling new DTDs from current specifications.
The system shall produce DTD as an instantiation for modeling environments (including database schema and message definition variants).
The system shall notify subscribers of changes to Information Resources.
The system shall provide capability to post planned changes to a registry.
The system shall support approve and reject submission workflows.
The system shall forward requests to different registries per explicit federation agreements.

systemFeatures.name: XML Registry — Consumer Services
systemFeatures.description: Enables users to discover, browse, search, and retrieve XML tags and associated metadata from the Registry. Supports multiple views of tag relationships (ancestor/descendant, uses/used-by, data types, versions, reference sets, amplifying information) and provides download capabilities for selected information resources. Priority: Essential.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Consumer searches for a specific tag by name or definition substring. Response: System displays matching tags with associated metadata and relationship information.
Stimulus: Consumer views a tag's ancestor/descendant hierarchy. Response: System displays the tag's origin and parent/child relationships in tree or tabular format.
Stimulus: Consumer downloads selected information resources. Response: System provides the tag specifications, XML specs, and related artifacts for download.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide discovery capabilities with views of ancestor/descendant relationships, uses/used-by relationships, data type information, versioning relationships, reference sets, and amplifying information.
The system shall display XML tags via tree/hierarchy structure or tabular format.
The system shall provide multiple search options to find specific tags, including search by subscriber/author.
Each Information Resource shall have its own web page displaying all pertinent information according to resource type.
The system shall display XML Tag Specification forms to information resource authors for input.
The system shall provide capabilities to download XML Tags and other selected Information Resources.
The system shall catalog entities and attributes within servers to enable browsing, searching, and retrieving of data related to XML sources.
The system shall process ANSI standard SQL as specified in FIPS PUB 127-2.
The system shall provide subscription capability for notification of changes to Communities of Interest or Information Resources.

systemFeatures.name: XML Registry — Manager Services
systemFeatures.description: Provides administrative capabilities for managing the XML Registry including user management, naming conventions, metadata tag definitions, submission review/approval, and change monitoring. Priority: Essential.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Manager creates new username or superuser account. Response: System provisions the account with appropriate permissions.
Stimulus: Manager reviews a submitted information resource. Response: System presents the submission for review and supports approve/reject workflow.
Stimulus: Manager detects changes to registered data models. Response: System monitors and logs all changes for audit and notification purposes.
functionalRequirements: The system shall support creation and management of usernames and superusers.
The system shall establish naming conventions not in conflict with DOD data standards, with relationships to other naming conventions specified in I&RTS.
The system shall create a naming structure within the COE architecture expressing context and relationship of naming conventions.
The system shall define metadata tags, meta-metadata, and related terms for maintenance and control of XML tags.
The system shall support review and approval of submissions.
The system shall monitor changes to data models recorded in the Registry.
The system shall support passing requests to federated registries per explicit federation agreements.

systemFeatures.name: XML Schema Design Services
systemFeatures.description: Provides structure and content services for designing XML document schemas, including recording structures, validating against standards, referencing existing tags, creating new semantic structures, and versioning metadata objects. Priority: Essential.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Developer creates a new XML schema. Response: System records the structure, checks it against standards (e.g., style guide wizard for tags), validates it, and generates standard views.
Stimulus: Developer references existing tags in a new schema. Response: System retrieves tag definitions from the Registry and integrates them into the schema design.
functionalRequirements: The system shall record XML document structures.
The system shall check structures against standards (e.g., style guide wizard for tags).
The system shall validate designed schemas.
The system shall support use and reference of existing tags and associated semantic structures from the Registry.
The system shall support creation of new tags and associated semantic structures.
The system shall support interchange with other design tools and environments.
The system shall record semantic structures in JTA-compliant formalism.
The system shall generate standard views of schemas.
The system shall version metadata objects.

systemFeatures.name: XML Document Generation
systemFeatures.description: Generates XML documents with schema references, conformance checking (well-formed and valid), data view extraction, document rendering, and metadata transmission for alternate semantically-valid views. Priority: Essential.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Application requests generation of an XML document. Response: System generates the document with schema reference (by value or by reference), validates conformance, and provides for rendering.
Stimulus: User requests alternate view of generated document. Response: System transmits sufficient metadata to construct semantically-valid alternate views via XSL.
functionalRequirements: The system shall generate documents that include schemas by reference or by value.
The system shall develop schemes to permit dynamic cross-referencing and indexing of XML objects.
The system shall generate language (natural language) and charset values.
The system shall check conformance for well-formedness and validity.
The system shall extract views of data from documents.
The system shall disassemble documents to materialize different query results into different tree structures.
The system shall render generated documents.
The system shall interrogate documents.
The system shall transmit sufficient metadata to construct alternate, semantically-valid views (e.g., XSL).
The system shall express document version information.

systemFeatures.name: XML Document Consumption
systemFeatures.description: Parses and processes incoming XML documents using DOM and SAX parsers, with conformance checking, semantic validation, view extraction, and relational representation conversion. Priority: Essential.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Application receives an XML document. Response: System parses the document (DOM or SAX), checks well-formedness and validity, and makes content available for processing.
Stimulus: User requests relational view of hierarchical XML data. Response: System disassembles the hierarchical view into a relational representation.
functionalRequirements: The system shall provide DOM and SAX parsers for XML document consumption.
The system shall check consumed documents for well-formedness and validity.
The system shall create validation constraints enabling meaningful sharing of XML-based schemas and related information.
The system shall extract semantically-valid views from XML documents (e.g., via XSL).
The system shall disassemble hierarchical views to relational representations.
The system shall render consumed documents.

performanceRequirements: In fixed (static) mode, the system shall have the capability of being tuned by on-site personnel to adjust for varying workloads and database sizes.
In changing (dynamic) environments, the system shall operate with predetermined workloads and require access to fewer administrative capabilities.
Session time-out values shall be at minimum user-definable and resettable prior to session initialization.
The system should dynamically change session time values based on current communications performance identified by network management or DBMS capabilities.
safetyRequirements: Not explicitly specified — personnel and training requirements are delegated to embedding system developers.
securityRequirements: The system shall support Discretionary Access Controls (DAC) — restricting access to objects based on identity of subjects or groups.
The system shall support Mandatory Access Control (MAC) — mediating access based on clearance level and sensitivity labels, always enforced above discretionary controls.
The system shall interface with COE Security Administration services.
The system shall establish rules to ensure maximum semantic understanding of terms as they appear in XML documents, preventing misuse or misinterpretation.
softwareQualityAttributes: Portability: Registry software shall be portable and execute on COE-compliant platforms.
Interoperability: XS shall provide data management functions for access to distributed database management systems as a solution for interoperability.
Maintainability: The XS developer is responsible for software maintenance, support, and updates.
Compliance: XS shall be segmented per COE requirements and adhere to I&RTS compliance levels.
Location Transparency: Physical location of data shall be transparent to applications and users of the database system.

otherRequirements: The XS software shall be delivered in accordance with DII COE guidelines.
The DISA Configuration Manager is responsible for distribution of the XS product to system developers.
Requirements traceability is provided under a separate document.
The precedence and criticality mapping table for Section 3 requirements is designated TBD.

glossary.definition: Army Command and Control Systems.
Application Programming Interface.
Army Tactical Command and Control Systems.
Army WWMCCS Information System.
Common ACCS Support Software.
Common Operating Environment — the standardized computing platform for DII.
Community of Interest — a group of users/systems sharing common data exchange requirements.
Commercial Off-The-Shelf software.
Discretionary Access Control — restricting access based on identity of subjects or groups.
Database Interface.
Database Management System.
Document Content Description — an XML schema formalism alternative to DTD.
Defense Information Infrastructure — the DOD-wide information technology infrastructure.
Defense Information Systems Agency.
Document Type Definition — the schema for an XML document.
Global Command and Control Systems.
Integration and Runtime Specification — defines COE compliance levels.
Mandatory Access Control — access mediated by clearance level and sensitivity labels.
Redundant Array of Inexpensive Disks — storage system providing reliability through redundancy at multiple levels (RAID 0 through 6).
Remote Data Access — ISO 9579 application layer interoperability standard for accessing remote DBMSs over OSI networks.
Standing Request for Information — a CASS capability for monitoring occurrence of conditions and notifying applications (timer-based, data-based, or message-based).
Standard Theater Army Command and Control System.
XML Services — the functional area providing XML-based data access and registry services for the DII COE.
Extensible Stylesheet Language — used for constructing alternate semantically-valid views of XML documents.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This flowchart shows the XML Services information resource lifecycle from submission through review, approval/rejection, and consumption by DOD applications.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    Dev["Developer Reviews Registry Tags"] --> Decision{"Existing Tags Sufficient?"}
    Decision -->|Yes| Adopt["Adopt Existing Tags"]
    Decision -->|No| PrepPkg["Prepare Submittal Package"]
    PrepPkg --> FillForms["Complete XML Tag Specification"]
    FillForms --> AddRelations["Specify Tag Relationships"]
    AddRelations --> AddAmplifying["Include Amplifying Info"]
    AddAmplifying --> CompressSend["Zip and Email/FTP Package"]
    CompressSend --> Parse["Registry Parses Submission"]
    Parse --> Populate["Populate Registry Database"]
    Populate --> Review{"COI POC Reviews"}
    Review -->|Approve| ApproveStatus["Status: Approved"]
    Review -->|Reject| RejectStatus["Status: Rejected"]
    ApproveStatus --> Notify["Notify Subscribers"]
    RejectStatus --> AnnotateReject["Annotate Rejection"]
    Adopt --> UseInApp["Use Tags in Application"]
    ApproveStatus --> UseInApp
    UseInApp --> GenDoc["Generate XML Document"]
    GenDoc --> Validate{"Conformance Check"}
    Validate -->|Well-formed + Valid| Transmit["Transmit Document"]
    Validate -->|Invalid| Fix["Fix Schema Issues"]
    Fix --> GenDoc
    Transmit --> ConsumeDoc["Consumer Parses Document"]
    ConsumeDoc --> ExtractViews["Extract Semantically-Valid Views"]
flowchartDiagram.caption: DII COE XML Services Information Resource Lifecycle

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This sequence diagram shows the interaction for a producer submitting a new XML tag to the registry and a consumer discovering and using that tag.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Prod as Producer
    participant Web as Web Interface
    participant Reg as XML Registry
    participant POC as COI POC
    participant DataEng as Data Engineering Team
    participant Cons as Consumer
    participant App as Application

    Prod->>Web: Download Submittal Package
    Web-->>Prod: Forms + Instructions + Tools
    Prod->>Prod: Complete XML Tag Spec
    Prod->>Prod: Add Relationships and Amplifying Info
    Prod->>Reg: Submit Package via Email/FTP
    Reg->>Reg: Parse Submitted Forms
    Reg->>Reg: Populate Registry Database
    Reg->>POC: Route for Review
    POC->>Reg: Approve Submission
    Reg->>Reg: Update Status to Approved
    Reg->>Prod: Notify of Approval
    DataEng->>Reg: Analyze for Consolidation

    Cons->>Web: Search Registry for Tags
    Web->>Reg: Query by Name/Definition
    Reg-->>Web: Matching Tags with Metadata
    Web-->>Cons: Display Results
    Cons->>Web: View Tag Hierarchy
    Web->>Reg: Retrieve Relationships
    Reg-->>Web: Ancestor/Descendant Tree
    Cons->>Web: Download Selected Tags
    Web-->>Cons: Tag Specs + DTDs + Samples

    App->>Reg: Reference Approved Tags
    App->>App: Generate XML Document with Schema
    App->>App: Validate Well-formedness
    App->>App: Check Validity
    App->>App: Transmit Document
sequenceDiagram.caption: XML Registry Producer Submission and Consumer Discovery Flow

informationResourceTypes.description: Tag, either complex or terminal node.
Characteristic of an element, may be constrained by enumeration.
A representation using a formalism such as IDEF1x or Object Modeling.
