introduction.purpose: This Software Requirements Specification (SRS) defines the requirements for the Dallas/Ft. Worth (DFW) Regional **Center-to-Center (C2C) Communications Network**, a TxDOT project that interconnects dissimilar Traffic Management Centers (TMCs) in the DFW Metroplex. It implements a common repository for traffic data and provides mechanisms to exchange device control information between TMCs using ITS standards.
introduction.documentConventions: Requirements are organized by functional area in table format. Each requirement has a three-part identifier: <C2C Project Mnemonic>-<Requirement Category Mnemonic>-<Requirement Number>. Category mnemonics include IF (Interface), DS (Data Store), DT (Data Transmission), MP (Map), GI (Incident GUI), CG (Remote Control GUI), DC (Design/Construction), OP (Operational). Tables contain requirement number, description, and rationale columns.
introduction.intendedAudience: DFW Regional Software Task Force, North Central Texas Council of Governments (NCTCOG), Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), Traffic Management Center operators, ITS software developers, system integrators, and municipal/county traffic engineering agencies in the DFW Metroplex.
introduction.productScope: The **C2C Communications Network** provides a standards-based infrastructure for sharing traffic data and remotely controlling ITS field devices across the DFW Metroplex. The system supports: (1) A common repository for traffic information using TMDD data elements and message sets; (2) A WWW-based graphical traffic conditions map; (3) A Microsoft Windows application allowing agencies without formal TMCs to participate; (4) Center-to-center command/control/status for 20 types of ITS field devices; (5) An extensible architecture using building blocks configurable for local, regional, and statewide repositories.
references: Concept Of Operations Framework For The Dallas/Ft. Worth Regional Center-to-Center Communications Network, Version 1.0, Southwest Research Institute, November 2001
ITS Traffic Management Data Dictionary (TMDD) standard
DATEX/ASN message encoding standard
SICD and CICD Version 2.3
NTCIP (National Transportation Communications for ITS Protocol)

overallDescription.productPerspective: The C2C infrastructure interconnects multiple dissimilar traffic management systems in the DFW Metroplex. Each existing TMC interfaces to the C2C 'cloud' through system-specific adapters that convert local proprietary formats to ITS standard formats (TMDD). The architecture uses configurable building blocks that can be deployed in multiple instances — individual agencies deploy configured building blocks, local repositories link partners, regional repositories link local ones, and statewide repositories link regional ones. New ITS-standards-based systems integrate directly into the cloud without adapters.
productFunctions: Collect and store roadway network data (links, nodes with geometry and attributes)
Exchange real-time traffic conditions (speed, volume, density, travel time, delay, occupancy)
Share incident data (type, severity, location, status, affected lanes, weather, road conditions)
Share lane closure data (location, schedule, affected lanes, contact information)

Provide status and remote control for DMS, LCS, CCTV (including video snapshots/switching), Ramp Meters, HAR, Traffic Signals, ESS, HOV lanes, Parking Lots, School Zones, Railroad Crossings, Reversible Lanes, Dynamic Lanes
Exchange transit information (bus stops, bus locations, light/commuter rail, park & ride, vehicle priority)
Generate WWW-based traffic conditions map with color-coded speeds and incident display
Provide Incident GUI for agencies without TMCs to enter incident/lane closure data
Provide Remote Control GUI for device command/control over public networks

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: Professional traffic management center operators at TxDOT, Dallas, Ft. Worth, and other DFW agencies. Use existing TMC systems that interface to C2C through adapters. Monitor traffic conditions, manage incidents, control field devices.

Staff at municipal or county agencies without formal Traffic Management Centers. Use the Remote Control GUI and Incident GUI to participate in C2C information sharing and device control. Require authentication (username/password) for device commands.

Public users and transportation professionals accessing the WWW-based traffic conditions map. View color-coded highway speeds, incident locations, DMS messages, LCS signals, and CCTV status via web browser.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: Microsoft Windows NT for C2C server and GUI applications. TCP/IP networking for inter-center communications. ESRI ARC IMS for web map generation. DATEX/ASN runtime library required on all communicating computers. Public network (Internet) connectivity for Remote Control GUI. NCTCOG Geo-Data warehouse for basemap data.
designAndImplementationConstraints: C2C server shall execute in Microsoft Windows NT environment [C2C-DC-01, C2C-DC-04]
C2C shall be implemented in C/C++ programming language [C2C-DC-05]
Web interface shall use ESRI ARC IMS product [C2C-DC-03, C2C-DC-06]
Incident GUI and Remote Control GUI shall use C/C++ with ESRI Map Objects [C2C-DC-07, C2C-DC-08]
DATEX/ASN runtime library required on all communicating computers [C2C-DC-02]
TMDD standard (including message sets) shall be used for data transmission [C2C-DT-01]
DATEX/ASN shall encode TMDD message sets [C2C-DT-02]
TCP/IP shall be used for transport including connection management [C2C-DT-03]
userDocumentation: Software Requirements Specification (this document)
Concept of Operations Framework
System Interface Control Document (SICD/CICD v2.3)
assumptionsAndDependencies: Existing TMCs have operational traffic management systems with available data feeds
TMDD standard and associated message sets are available and evolving
ESRI ARC IMS and Map Objects products are licensed and available
TCP/IP network connectivity exists between all participating agencies
DATEX/ASN encoding libraries are available for Windows NT
NCTCOG Geo-Data warehouse provides basemap data for web maps
Ft. Worth will not support momentary Pan/Tilt/Zoom/Iris/Focus CCTV commands
Dallas will not support Tour video switch command
Associating bus/rail stops with roadway links may have configuration management challenges across separately managed centers

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces:
**Web Map (C2C-MP series)**: Browser-based graphical map displaying interstates and state highways with color-coded speed depictions (Green/Yellow/Red thresholds configurable). Shows incident icons (clickable for details), DMS locations with current messages, LCS locations with current signals, CCTV locations with status. Supports zoom and pan (N/S/E/W). Tabular incident listing with location, type, severity, status, direction, affected lanes.

**Incident GUI (C2C-GI series)**: Windows application for entering/modifying/deleting incident and lane closure data without requiring a TMC. Supports incident fields: location, description, status, affected lanes, detection/response times, queue length, estimated clear time. Lane closure fields: location, description, affected lanes, date, start/end times. Lists previously entered records.

**Remote Control GUI (C2C-CG series)**: Windows application executing over public network for equipment command/control. Requires username/password authentication. Supports device selection from lists for DMS (message, beacons), LCS (signal pattern), CCTV (pan/tilt/zoom, switching, presets), Ramp Meters (plan), HAR (text), Traffic Signals (plan), HOV (plan), School Zones (plan), Reversible Lanes (plan), Dynamic Lanes (plan). Displays command status responses in scrollable list.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: ITS field devices: Dynamic Message Signs (DMS), Lane Control Signals (LCS), Closed Circuit Television cameras (CCTV with video switching), Ramp Meters, Highway Advisory Radios (HAR), Traffic Signals, Environmental Sensor Stations (ESS), High Occupancy Vehicle lane controllers, Parking Lot sensors, School Zone flashers, Railroad Crossing detectors, Reversible Lane controllers, Dynamic Lane controllers. All devices have network identifier, device identifier, location (latitude/longitude), and status fields.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: Existing TMC software systems (various proprietary formats converted to TMDD standards at interface boundary). ESRI ARC IMS for web map image generation. ESRI Map Objects for GUI mapping components. DATEX/ASN runtime library for message encoding. TMDD-compliant data elements and message sets.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: TCP/IP for all center-to-center data transmission. DATEX/ASN protocol encoding for TMDD message sets. Public network (Internet) connectivity for Remote Control GUI with firewall/gateway considerations. Video switching for CCTV camera feeds between centers.

systemFeatures.name: Roadway Network and Traffic Data Exchange
systemFeatures.description: Core data sharing infrastructure for roadway network topology, real-time traffic conditions, incidents, and lane closures across the DFW Metroplex. Uses TMDD standard data elements. Priority: Essential.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: TMC publishes traffic conditions update. Response: Data Collector stores TMDD-formatted speed, volume, density, travel time, delay, and occupancy data per link.

Stimulus: Operator creates incident via Incident GUI or TMC. Response: Incident data (19 fields including location, type, severity, weather, road conditions) stored and shared with all connected centers.
Stimulus: Lane closure scheduled. Response: Closure data (21 fields including schedule, affected lanes, contact) shared across C2C infrastructure.
functionalRequirements: C2C-IF-IS01: Roadway network data — identifier, name, link count, node count, link/node lists
C2C-IF-IS02: Link data — 13 fields including identifier, road number, type, direction, length, capacity, speed limits, lane count
C2C-IF-IS03: Node data — 8 fields including identifier, name, jurisdiction, owner, lat/lon, type
C2C-IF-IS04: Traffic conditions per link — 10 fields including delay, travel time, volume, speed, density, occupancy
C2C-IF-IS05: Incident data — 19 fields including severity, type, road conditions, weather, confirmed/cleared timestamps
C2C-IF-IS06: Lane closure data — 21 fields including start/end locations, affected lanes, schedule, contact
C2C-DS-01: Data Collector stores TMDD data elements and message set information

systemFeatures.name: ITS Field Device Status and Control
systemFeatures.description: Provides status monitoring and remote command/control for 17 types of ITS field devices across center boundaries. Each device type has specific status and control message formats. Authentication required for all control commands. Priority: Essential.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Center requests DMS status. Response: Returns 8 fields including sign geometry, status, current MULTI message, beacon state.
Stimulus: Operator sends DMS control command. Response: Center authenticates user, applies message and beacon settings to target DMS.
Stimulus: CCTV lock request. Response: Center locks camera to requesting user, enabling pan/tilt/zoom control with preset positions.
Stimulus: Railroad crossing activation detected. Response: Status data updated with rail type, estimated clearing time, and minutes to train arrival.
functionalRequirements: C2C-IF-IS07/08: DMS status (8 fields) and control (6 fields with MULTI message, beacons)
C2C-IF-IS09/10: LCS status (8 fields) and control (5 fields with signal pattern)
C2C-IF-IS11-14: CCTV status (15 fields), control (pan/tilt/zoom/focus/iris), video snapshots (JPEG), video switching
C2C-IF-IS15/16: Ramp Meter status (8 fields) and control (plan, duration)
C2C-IF-IS17/18: HAR status (7 fields) and control (message text, duration)
C2C-IF-IS19/20: Traffic Signal status (11 fields including preemption) and control (plan, duration)
C2C-IF-IS21: ESS status (10 fields including type, reading, units, alarm)
C2C-IF-IS22/22.1: HOV status (11 fields) and control (lane plan, duration)
C2C-IF-IS23: Parking Lot status (10 fields including capacity, utilization, restrictions)
C2C-IF-IS24/25: School Zone status (9 fields) and control (plan)
C2C-IF-IS26: Railroad Crossing status (10 fields including estimated train timing)
C2C-IF-IS27/28: Reversible Lane status (10 fields) and control (plan, duration)
C2C-IF-IS29/30: Dynamic Lane status (9 fields) and control (lane plan, duration)
C2C-IF-IS36: Network device status summary — counts and status data for all 17 device types
C2C-IF-IS37/38: Command timeframe request/response for device-type-dependent acceptance windows

systemFeatures.name: Transit Data Exchange
systemFeatures.description: Supports sharing of transit information including bus stops, bus locations, light/commuter rail stops and locations, park and ride lots, and vehicle priority requests. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Bus location update received. Response: C2C stores and shares 8 fields including schedule adherence, vehicle attributes, and capacity.
Stimulus: Vehicle priority request. Response: Transit center shares priority request with traffic signal center including intersection identifier, service strategy, and desired arrival time.
functionalRequirements: C2C-IF-IS30: Bus stop data — 8 fields including link identifier, routes, frequency
C2C-IF-IS31: Bus location data — 8 fields including schedule adherence, vehicle attributes, capacity
C2C-IF-IS32: Light/Commuter Rail stop data — 7 fields including routes, frequency
C2C-IF-IS33: Light/Commuter Rail location data — 8 fields including schedule adherence, capacity
C2C-IF-IS34: Park and Ride lot data — 10 fields including capacity, utilization, restrictions
C2C-IF-IS35: Vehicle priority data — 10 fields including intersection, priority, vehicle classification, service strategy

systemFeatures.name: WWW Traffic Conditions Map
systemFeatures.description: Web-based graphical map application displaying real-time traffic conditions, incidents, and device status for the DFW Metroplex using ESRI ARC IMS. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User accesses web map. Response: Map displays interstates and state highways with color-coded speed depictions and incident icons.
Stimulus: User clicks incident icon. Response: Detailed incident information displayed.
Stimulus: User zooms/pans. Response: Map updates view with appropriate level of detail.
functionalRequirements: C2C-MP-01: Display interstates and state highways on graphical map
C2C-MP-03: Basemap data from NCTCOG Geo-Data warehouse
C2C-MP-03: User zoom capability (magnification levels)
C2C-MP-04: User pan in N/S/E/W directions
C2C-MP-05/06: Color-coded link speeds (Green/Yellow/Red with configurable thresholds)
C2C-MP-07: Display current incidents as clickable icons
C2C-MP-08: Incident details on click
C2C-MP-09: Tabular incident listing with location, type, severity, status, direction, affected lanes
C2C-MP-10: Display DMS locations with current messages
C2C-MP-11: Display LCS locations with current signals; CCTV locations with status

systemFeatures.name: Incident and Remote Control GUIs
systemFeatures.description: Windows applications for agencies without TMCs to participate in the C2C infrastructure — Incident GUI for creating/managing incident and lane closure data, Remote Control GUI for authenticated device command/control over public networks. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: User launches Remote Control GUI. Response: Prompts for username/password authentication.
Stimulus: Authenticated user selects center and DMS. Response: GUI allows entering target DMS message and beacon settings; sends TMDD device control message to center.
Stimulus: User creates incident via Incident GUI. Response: Incident data entered and shared with C2C infrastructure without requiring a TMC.
functionalRequirements: C2C-GI-01: Incident GUI allows incident/lane closure entry without a Center
C2C-GI-02: Incident entry — 7 fields including location, description, status, queue length, estimated clear time
C2C-GI-03: Lane closure entry — 6 fields including location, description, affected lanes, schedule
C2C-GI-04/05/06: List, modify, delete previously entered incidents
C2C-GI-07/08/09: List, modify, delete previously entered lane closures
C2C-CG-01: Remote Control GUI executes on public network, sends TMDD device control messages
C2C-CG-02: Login with username/password
C2C-CG-03: Network identifier selection for device commands
C2C-CG-04 through CG-14: Device selection and control for DMS, LCS, CCTV switching/PTZ, Ramp Meter, HAR, Traffic Signal, HOV, School Zone, Reversible Lane, Dynamic Lane
C2C-CG-15: Device command status display in scrollable list

performanceRequirements: System must support real-time data exchange between multiple TMCs across the DFW Metroplex
TMDD-based data transmission must support continuous traffic conditions updates
Web map must display current conditions with configurable color-coded speed thresholds
safetyRequirements: Device control commands require authenticated username/password for all remote operations
Ft. Worth will not support momentary Pan/Tilt/Zoom/Iris/Focus to prevent unsafe camera operations
Command timeframe windows restrict when centers accept remote device commands by device type
securityRequirements: All device control commands require username and password authentication [C2C-CG-02]
Remote Control GUI operates over public network — firewall/gateway connectivity not addressed by SRS
CCTV lock mechanism prevents concurrent control conflicts between operators
softwareQualityAttributes: Extensibility: Building block architecture allows local→regional→statewide repository scaling
Reusability: ITS standards (TMDD) create a system reusable for other ITS application areas
Configurability: Building blocks configurable for specific agency deployment without code changes
Interoperability: ITS standards ensure dissimilar TMC systems can exchange data
businessRules: New ITS-standards-based systems integrate directly without adapters
Building blocks support hierarchical repository linking (local → regional → statewide)
Transit data linkage to roadway networks may have configuration management challenges across separately managed centers

otherRequirements: C2C-OP-01: Normal mode — receives data from all connected systems, combines into single data store
C2C-OP-02: Test mode — performs normal operations plus activity logging for development/testing

glossary.definition: Center-to-Center Communications — the ITS infrastructure for sharing traffic data and device control between Traffic Management Centers
Traffic Management Data Dictionary — the ITS standard defining data elements and message sets for traffic management information exchange
Traffic Management Center — facility where traffic management systems are operated and monitored
Dynamic Message Sign — electronic sign displaying variable text messages to motorists
Lane Control Signal — signal controlling individual lane usage (open/closed/merge)
Closed Circuit Television — camera system for traffic surveillance with pan/tilt/zoom capabilities and video switching
Highway Advisory Radio — roadside radio transmitter broadcasting traffic advisory messages
Environmental Sensor Station — weather and environmental monitoring device providing readings and alarms
High Occupancy Vehicle — designated lanes requiring minimum vehicle occupancy
Data Exchange / Abstract Syntax Notation — protocol encoding standard for transmitting TMDD message sets
Mark-Up Language for Transportation Information — text encoding format for DMS messages
National Transportation Communications for ITS Protocol — communication protocol standards for ITS devices
North Central Texas Council of Governments — regional planning organization for the DFW Metroplex
Texas Department of Transportation — state agency managing transportation infrastructure
Environmental Systems Research Institute's Arc Internet Map Server — GIS product for creating web-based map images
Advanced Traffic Management System — comprehensive system for managing traffic operations

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This flowchart shows the C2C infrastructure architecture with building blocks connecting dissimilar TMCs through a standards-based cloud.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    subgraph TMCs["Traffic Management Centers"]
        D["Dallas TMC"]
        FW["Ft. Worth TMC"]
        DART["DART Transit"]
        Other["Other DFW Agencies"]
    end

    subgraph Adapters["System-Specific Adapters"]
        AD["Dallas Adapter\nProprietary → TMDD"]
        AFW["Ft. Worth Adapter\nProprietary → TMDD"]
        ADART["DART Adapter\nProprietary → TMDD"]
    end

    subgraph Cloud["C2C Standards-Based Cloud"]
        DC["Data Collector\nTMDD Data Store"]
        DT["DATEX/ASN\nover TCP/IP"]
        BB["Configurable\nBuilding Blocks"]
    end

    subgraph Apps["User Applications"]
        WM["WWW Traffic Map\nESRI ARC IMS"]
        IG["Incident GUI\nC/C++ + Map Objects"]
        RG["Remote Control GUI\nC/C++ + Map Objects"]
    end

    subgraph Devices["ITS Field Devices"]
        DMS_D["DMS"]
        LCS_D["LCS"]
        CCTV_D["CCTV"]
        RM_D["Ramp Meters"]
        HAR_D["HAR"]
        TS_D["Traffic Signals"]
        ESS_D["ESS"]
    end

    D --> AD --> DT
    FW --> AFW --> DT
    DART --> ADART --> DT
    Other -->|"No TMC"| IG
    Other -->|"No TMC"| RG
    DT --> DC
    DC --> BB
    BB --> WM
    IG --> DT
    RG --> DT
    DT --> Devices
flowchartDiagram.caption: DFW C2C Infrastructure Architecture with Building Blocks

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This sequence diagram shows the remote device control flow for a DMS message change via the Remote Control GUI.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant User as Agency Operator
    participant RG as Remote Control GUI
    participant C2C as C2C Infrastructure
    participant TMC as Owning TMC
    participant DMS as Target DMS

    User->>RG: Launch application
    RG->>User: Prompt username/password
    User->>RG: Enter credentials
    RG->>C2C: Connect via TCP/IP
    User->>RG: Select network identifier
    C2C-->>RG: Return device list
    User->>RG: Select target DMS
    User->>RG: Enter message + beacon setting
    RG->>C2C: TMDD Device Control Message\n(network ID, DMS ID, user, pass, message, beacons)
    C2C->>TMC: Forward DATEX/ASN encoded command
    TMC->>DMS: Apply message and beacon settings
    DMS-->>TMC: Status confirmation
    TMC-->>C2C: Command response
    C2C-->>RG: Status response
    RG-->>User: Display status in scrollable list
sequenceDiagram.caption: Remote Device Control Flow for DMS Message Change

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This ER diagram shows the core data entities in the C2C system and their relationships.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    NETWORK ||--|{ LINK : contains
    NETWORK ||--|{ NODE : contains
    LINK ||--o{ TRAFFIC_CONDITION : has
    LINK ||--o{ INCIDENT : occurs_on
    LINK ||--o{ LANE_CLOSURE : affects
    NETWORK ||--o{ DEVICE : manages
    DEVICE ||--o| DEVICE_COMMAND : receives
    NETWORK ||--o{ TRANSIT_STOP : contains
    NETWORK ||--o{ TRANSIT_VEHICLE : tracks

    NETWORK {
        string network_id PK
        string network_name
        int link_count
        int node_count
    }
    LINK {
        string link_id PK
        string road_number
        string direction
        float length
        int speed_limit
        int num_lanes
    }
    NODE {
        string node_id PK
        string jurisdiction
        float latitude
        float longitude
        string node_type
    }
    TRAFFIC_CONDITION {
        string link_id FK
        float speed
        float volume
        float density
        float travel_time
    }
    INCIDENT {
        string incident_id PK
        string severity
        string status
        string incident_type
        datetime confirmed
        datetime cleared
    }
    DEVICE {
        string device_id PK
        string device_type
        string device_name
        float latitude
        float longitude
        string status
    }
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: C2C Data Model Entity Relationships

dataDictionary.definition: Unique identifier for a traffic management network, used to distinguish between different TMC jurisdictions
A directional segment of roadway defined by start and end nodes, with attributes for type, length, capacity, speed limits, and lane count
A point on the roadway network (intersection or bridge) defined by latitude/longitude, jurisdiction, and owner
Mark-Up Language for Transportation Information encoded text for DMS message display
Configuration of lane control signal heads indicating lane status (open, closed, merge)
Device-type-dependent window specifying days and times when a center will accept remote control commands

Published by: Elforsk AB, Stockholm, Sweden.

introduction.purpose:
This specification defines the functional requirements for establishing and operating a system for **data communication between the control system of a wind turbine and remote computers (SCADA)**. It serves as guidance during procurement of communication solutions for wind power plants. The specification applies to both single wind turbine installations and wind farms. It covers requirements for data transfer and handling — which data needs to be transferred and how — but explicitly does NOT specify how the data shall be used by the SCADA system, nor does it describe or specify characteristics of SCADA systems, HMI interfaces, or control algorithms.

The specification was developed by a Danish-Swedish working group representing Elforsk AB, Sydkraft Vind AB, Tech-wise A/S (for Elsam A/S), and SEAS Distribution A.m.b.A (for Energi E2 A/S). It was created as the first step toward an IEC TC88 international communication standard for wind turbine supervision and control.
introduction.documentConventions: The document is structured into: (1) Glossary of terms limited to document scope with IEC references; (2) Introduction with background, intention, participants; (3) Scope and system outline with figures; (4) System functional requirements for operational and system management functions; (5) Communication requirements covering data transfer principles, data kinds, security, and performance; (6) Plant data covering information structure, naming conventions, and signal specifications; (7) References to IEC standards; (8) Appendices with technical solutions, protocol comparisons, and data lists. The suffix '[No ref.]' indicates definitions created for this specification; numbered references [1], [2], [3] point to IEC standards.
introduction.intendedAudience: Wind power plant operators and owners, wind turbine manufacturers and vendors, SCADA system integrators, communication system designers, electrical system operators (transmission network operators of 100 kV or more), electrical network operators at point of common connection, IEC TC88 Wind Turbine Systems standardisation body, procurement and tendering specialists.
introduction.productScope:
The specification covers **communication systems supporting functions mainly for remote operation and supervision of wind power plants**. It defines: the operational functions (supervision, control, alarm management, event logging, data retrieval, parameter changes, disturbance record retrieval) and system management functions (network management, time synchronisation, self-checking, software management, configuration management, test mode, security management) that the communication system must support. It specifies the data types (12 kinds), transfer principles (5 types), performance requirements, security requirements, and plant data structure including a comprehensive naming convention and data object model.

The specification explicitly **excludes**: local functionality (portable PC hook-up, Internet access, email), voice and video communication, actor-specific functions (such as energy accounting for network operators), process automation functions operating directly on process data without operator involvement, SCADA system characteristics, HMI design, and control algorithms.
references: [1] IEC 50(415), International Electrotechnical Vocabulary – Chapter 415: Wind Turbine Systems, Terminology
[2] IEC 870-1-3, Power System Control and Associated Communications, Glossary
[3] IEC61850-02, Communication Networks and Systems in Substations, Glossary

overallDescription.productPerspective:
The communication system operates between wind turbine control systems and remote SCADA computers. Wind turbines are autonomously controlled — all necessary control and safety systems for proper operation are self-contained and do not depend on external control devices. However, turbines may be supervised and controlled by a central system (SCADA) for coordination with other turbines and the electrical network. The system must support three deployment topologies:

**Network System 1**: Interface at individual wind turbine nodes. Wind Farm LAN connects to operator WAN/LAN. Standards apply at individual nodes. Internal proprietary methods at each node.

**Network System 2**: Interface at Wind Farm Server. Server acts as transparent gateway between Wind Farm LAN and operator LAN. Standards apply at server level, not individual nodes.

**Network System 3**: Interface to legacy RTU control systems via dial-up connections. Both wind farms and single turbines may use this topology.

The physical environment at wind power plant locations typically has wide spans of temperature, moisture, salinity, and vibration levels. The communication system shall not be used for safe and secure operation of the plant — all safety functions must be self-contained in the process or devices. No functions regarding safety of persons shall be based on the communication system.
productFunctions: Remote supervision of wind turbine operational status, measurements, and state changes
Remote control of wind turbine operation including start, stop, parameter changes, and set points
Alarm management — generation, transmission, visualisation, acknowledgement, and clearing of operational alarms triggered by automatic stops, emergency stops, or alarm-causing events
Event and log management — continuous scanning of devices for alarms, operator actions, and state changes with chronological date/time recording
Data retrieval of configuration data, parameter settings (names, values, units, default vs. current values)
Disturbance and fault record retrieval for display and bulk data storage
Access security management with authentication and role-based access control
System management: network management, time synchronisation, self-checking, software version management, configuration management, operative mode control, test mode, and system security management

Data interchange for secondary systems: beacons, fire protection, emergency alarm, intruder alarm, power supplies, meteorological stations, safety systems, data loggers, condition monitoring (spectres, trends, statistics)
Support coexistence of verbal (telephone) and visual (video) communication on the same transmission media

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: Operator of transmission networks of 100 kV or more. Applicable to wind farms. Not applicable to single turbines/clusters (may become relevant if turbines connect directly to transmission network in future). Requires advanced functions for grid stability regulation, power quality monitoring, and reactive power compensation.

Operator of the network at the point of common connection. Applicable to wind farms, not applicable to single turbines/clusters. Requires monitoring of power quality and reactive power. Energy accounting functions outside scope.

Operation and maintenance responsibility. Applicable to both wind farms and single turbines/clusters. Primary user class. For wind farms: requires remote supervision with on-line functions and frequent data updates; advanced function needs increase with installed capacity (especially 100+ MW). For single turbines: needs are very modest; primarily concerned with maximising electricity production; typically uses dial-up requesting systems over public telephone network.

Applicable to both wind farms and single turbines/clusters. May be specialised power production companies (wind farms) or private individuals/corporations (single turbines). Needs operational and production data.

Applicable to both wind farms and single turbines/clusters. Includes turbine manufacturers providing maintenance services, condition monitoring specialists, etc. Access through gateways to communication protocol and media of their choice.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: Physical environment at wind power plant locations encompassing wide spans of temperature, moisture, salinity, and vibration levels. Physical transmission media may include direct phone connections (ISDN or analogue), Internet, GSM, or radio link. Network based on Ethernet standards and TCP/IP. System must allow multiple protocols and procedures simultaneously on the same network (e.g., file transfer concurrent with data transfer). At each physical location, multiple node addresses may be needed for different subsystems within a single wind turbine. For wind farms (>10 MW installed capacity), a local Wind Farm LAN typically connects turbines to a Wind Farm Main Controller (WFMC). Offshore wind farms will increasingly require condition monitoring as a standard function.
designAndImplementationConstraints: Communication system shall be based on open and widely accepted methods with high degree of interface possibilities
System shall be robust and reliable, but NOT used for safe and secure operation of the plant
Faults in the communication system shall not cause malfunction of any individual wind turbine
Faults of a sub-system shall interfere as little as possible with communication system as a whole
The system must be flexible in supporting future requirements and future developments
The system must be open: 'anyone shall be able to get information on anything from anywhere' once authorised
The protocol shall have flexibility that new data can be defined without disturbing old versions of compliant equipment
System shall be adapted to individual users with services provided through configurations and set-ups
Network based on Ethernet standards operating on TCP/IP standards and procedures
Multiple protocols and procedures simultaneously on the same network
At each physical location, multiple IED addresses (nodes) shall be definable
Connection/association establishment, maintenance, and release handled by communication system
Messages received in same order as sent (guaranteed with specified availability)
Message priority may be needed in case of limited capacity
Flow control and error control may be provided
All analogue process values accessible in standard SI-units or other physical units
All data must have defined total set, naming, type, and default values per specification
Each device/object shall be self-descriptive (generic part) with identify request returning list of all objects, names, and descriptions
Text shall use ASCII or Unicode (UTF16) readable text, at least optional in operator's language
Hierarchical name structure with object data dictionary specialised for wind power plants
userDocumentation: This specification (Elforsk rapport 01:25)
IEC 50(415) Wind Turbine Systems Terminology
IEC 870-1-3 Power System Control Glossary
IEC 61850-02 Communication Networks Glossary
Concept of Operations (separate from this specification)
assumptionsAndDependencies: Wind turbines are autonomously controlled — all safety systems self-contained
Communication system is NOT safety-critical; all safety functions must be self-contained in process/devices
If communication system is completely inaccessible, plant may be forced to shutdown by alternative means
Existing plants interface to new communication system via gateways for proprietary protocols
Interface to existing plants provides a subset of specified functions and data
IEC TC88 standardisation group will develop international standard based on this specification
Condition monitoring will be a standard function in all larger wind turbines, especially offshore
Practical validation tests planned during 2001 at wind turbines in both Sweden and Denmark

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The specification explicitly excludes HMI design requirements. However, it specifies that all data values (analogue, binary, alarms, counters) shall be available for display on operator HMI as well as for storage (databases). Text information shall use standard readable text (ASCII or Unicode UTF16), at least optional in operator language. Condition monitoring status and analysis reports (spectres, trends, statistics, time tracking) shall be available for HMI display. The presentation of information itself is out of scope.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces:
**Physical Transmission Media**: Direct phone connection (ISDN or analogue), Internet, GSM, radio link, Ethernet.

**Wind Turbine Control Units (CU)**: Each wind turbine contains a Control Unit with the local control system that interfaces to the communication system. Multiple node addresses may exist at a single physical turbine location for different subsystems.

**Wind Farm Main Controller (WFMC)**: For wind farms, an overall control unit conducting coordinated governing of plant output and grid compatibility. Connected to individual CUs via local communication system.

**Wind Farm Server**: Gateway between Wind Farm LAN and operator WAN/LAN. Transparent for all operational/control data.

**Sensors and Actuators**: Temperature sensors (CTnnn naming), vibration sensors (CYnnn), pressure sensors (CPnnn), oil pumps (APnnn), cooling fans (ANnnn). Gearbox subsystem includes LSS main bearing, HSS bearings 1&2, oil sump temperature, lube oil filter, oil cooler.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces:
**Candidate Protocol Stacks** (Appendix A/B):
- **OPC**: OLE for Process Control on Microsoft COM/DCOM. De facto standard in Windows environment. Data Access, Alarm and Event Handling, Historical Data Access. TCP/IP on Ethernet transport. Thousands of installations worldwide.
- **IEC 60870-5-101/-104**: Companion standard for telecontrol of RTUs and IEDs. 101 for basic telecontrol (point-to-point), 104 for telecontrol over TCP/IP network. Supports status, position, measurements, totals, events, parameters, commands, set points, file transfer.
- **IEC 60870-6 / TASE.2 (ICCP)**: Inter-control Center Communication Protocol. MMS companion standard for real-time indications, events, control, time series, scheduling, accounting. Over 100 realisations. EPRI recommended for all real-time communication.
- **IEC 61850**: Standard for substation device data integration. Object models of device functions (logical devices, logical nodes, data objects). MMS-based. TCP/IP or OSI TP4 transport.
- **XML (OWX)**: Proposed Open Windmill Exchange Format using extensible markup language for data interchange over TCP/IP or RS232.

**PICOM Model**: Uses CIGRE WG34.03 Piece of Information for Communication concept for data type identification with attributes: format, length, accuracy, tag information, type, kind, importance, data integrity, value, name, source, sink, time tag, priority, time requirements.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces:
**Network Architecture**: Interconnected LANs — Wind Farm LAN (any topology) connected to Operator LAN/WAN. TCP/IP networking standard.

**Data Transfer Principles** (Section 5.2):
- **A. Periodic data transfer**: All data or only changed data, at configurable intervals
- **B. Data transfer on demand**: Request/response (interrogation command from control centre)
- **C. Event driven (spontaneous) data transfer**: Initiated by application upon events or data changes
- **D. Command transfer**: Control commands to wind turbine
- **E. Set point transfer**: Set point values sent to wind turbine with confirmation

**Data Kinds** (Section 5.3, 12 types):
I. Measurements/analogue data; II. Set points; III. Binary signals/status; IV. Binary control commands; V. Alarms; VI. Events; VII. Counters; VIII. Timers; IX. Data structures; X. Time series data; XI. Short text messages; XII. Flat files

systemFeatures.name: Remote Supervision and Control of Wind Power Plants
systemFeatures.description: Operational functions for daily wind power plant operation, including local or remote supervision of operational status and state changes, remote control of wind turbine equipment (start, stop, switchgear, protection), parameter set switching (single parameters or pre-defined parameter sets with only one active set), and access security management with role-based authentication restricting clients to pre-determined services and objects. Priority: Essential.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Operator requests wind turbine start. Response: WTStart high-priority binary command sent to turbine CU; turbine transitions to 'Operation' main state with OperationAccepted substate; response confirmation returned to operator.

Stimulus: Operator stops turbine. Response: WTStop high-priority binary command sent; turbine transitions to 'Stopped' main state via controlled shutdown sequence; status update and event log entry generated.
Stimulus: Operator changes active power set point. Response: SetWTPower (high priority) or SetWTPctPower (high priority) set point command sent with confirmation; turbine adjusts output accordingly.

Stimulus: Operator resets turbine error. Response: WTErrorReset low-priority binary command sent; turbine attempts to clear fault condition and transitions from InternalFault/ExternalFault/SystemFault substate.
Stimulus: Operator requests DMS measurements on-demand. Response: All analogue values for requested object returned with current values, time tags, and quality flags.

Stimulus: Operator selects reactive power control mode. Response: One of WTCosFiRegOn, WTKVArRegOn, WTTanFiRegOn commands activates the selected regulation mode; corresponding BINSIG reflects active state.
functionalRequirements: Access control shall restrict authenticated clients to pre-determined set of services and objects
Remote or local monitoring of status and state changes (indications) for all operational devices
Control function allows operator or automatic function to operate equipment (switchgear, transformers, protection) subject to safety filters
Parameter changes support single parameters AND pre-defined parameter sets with only one active set at a time
All analogue process values accessible in standard SI-units or other physical units
Analogue on-line values updatable down to 1 second intervals
All averaged values stored in plant controller for retransmission on demand
For averaged values, accuracy of start time shall be better than 10 ms
All binary values accessible as real-time on-line instant data, stored and displayed at level shift with date/time tag, updatable to 1 second intervals
Set point commands require confirmation of update
Handshake procedure required for all commands that start/stop mechanical components, influence turbine status/operation mode, or change software
All other control commands shall give a response with the result of the command
Binary commands may include activation/deactivation of programs and parameter changes

systemFeatures.name: Alarm, Event, and Disturbance Management
systemFeatures.description: Comprehensive alarm management with immediate transmission upon triggering of automatic stops, emergency stops, or alarm-causing events. Event logging with chronological recording. Disturbance and fault record retrieval for analysis. All operational alarms use WTErrorXXXX naming with True/Reset state pairs. Priority: Essential.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Wind turbine automatic stop triggered (e.g., generator overspeed). Response: Alarm WTErrorXXXX transmitted immediately from CU to SCADA with True value, date+time tag, and counter increment; alarm displayed on operator HMI; turbine transitions to ShutDown main state.
Stimulus: Operator acknowledges alarm. Response: Alarm status updated in alarm log; if conditions cleared, WTErrorXXXX Reset value transmitted with date+time tag.

Stimulus: Operator requests event log retrieval. Response: Event log (WTLogXXXX entries) transferred on demand from plant controller; includes remote/manual reset, start, stop, parameter change, and service mode events.

Stimulus: Fault condition triggers FIFO buffer recording. Response: Selected analogue values sampled at up to 25 Hz, buffered from 1 minute before to 1 minute after triggering event; binary values recorded; data available for transfer without real-time synchronism.
functionalRequirements: Operational alarms transmitted immediately after triggering (automatic stop, emergency stop, or alarm-causing event)
Alarms available for display on operator HMI and for storage in databases
Alarm log buffer depth shall be at least one year
Alarm management functions allow operator to visualise, acknowledge, and clear alarms
Event log stored in plant controller for transmission on demand with chronological date/time information
Event log buffer depth shall be at least one year
Events include: remote reset, remote pause, remote start, remote stop, manual reset, manual pause, manual start, manual stop, parameter change (with parameter number and new value), service mode
Disturbance/fault record data retrieval for display and bulk data storage
FIFO buffers for selected analogue values with sampling rate sufficient to characterise events (typical 25 Hz)
Buffer time span: 1 minute before triggering to 1 minute after triggering
Additional manually triggered scanning of selected analogue values at up to 25 Hz with selectable duration
All scanning data stored in plant controller for transfer without real-time synchronism
All binary values stored in plant controller for retransmission on demand
Transfer of buffered data carried out without synchronism with real-time

systemFeatures.name: Data Storage and Counter/Timer Management
systemFeatures.description: Local data storage in plant controllers with FIFO buffers, production counters (energy totals/subtotals in MWh), activation counters for all operational states and equipment, and accumulated time timers for all operational states. Counter buffer size supports at least 20 years at 5000 full load hours/year. Priority: Essential.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Operator requests counter values. Response: All counter values (COU type) returned with current accumulated counts and date+time tags.
Stimulus: Operator resets timer. Response: Timer reset to zero; Reset Date stored as separate item; timer resumes accumulating from zero.
Stimulus: Operator requests production counters. Response: Six production counters returned: ProdGen1Total, ProdGen2Total, ProdPotentialTotal (in MWh) plus corresponding subtotals.
functionalRequirements: Counters accumulate values over time: hour counters, production counters, operational mode counters, timers
Counter values available for HMI display and database storage with date/time tags, updatable to 1 second
Counter buffer size at least 20 years of operation with 5000 full load hours per year

Timers track accumulated time for all operational states: AutoRewind, AwaitingAutoReset, AwaitingAutoRewind, AwaitingManualReset, AwaitingRemoteReset, ExternalFault, HighWind, Idling, InternalFault, IsolatedOperation, ManualMode, Operation, OperationAccepted, ShutDown, Stopped, SystemFault, YawingAccepted, all equipment activation states
Timers resettable with Reset Date stored as separate item
Production counters in MWh: ProdGen1Total, ProdGen2Total, ProdPotentialTotal, ProdGen1Subtotal, ProdGen2Subtotal, ProdPotentialSubtotal with accuracy class 1
Condition monitoring system provides status and analysis reports (spectres, trends, statistics, time tracking)
Condition monitoring values updatable down to 1 second interval, stored in plant controller for transmission on demand

systemFeatures.name: Communication Security and Performance
systemFeatures.description: Security framework including server/client authentication, role-based access control, data integrity (non-corruption with acceptable residual error rate), and data confidentiality (encryption). Performance requirements define time-critical function transfer times and reliability including redundancy support. Priority: Essential.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Client connects to wind turbine communication system. Response: Server authentication verifies client is on intended site; client authentication verifies authorised operator; role-based access grants services and data visibility per user level.
Stimulus: Time-critical control command issued. Response: Short high-priority message transmitted with overall transfer time not more than 0.5 seconds; acknowledge-on-receive returned promptly.
Stimulus: Communication channel failure detected. Response: Automatic detection of communication faults; redundant channel activated; stored data transferred to central storage after restoration.
functionalRequirements: Server authentication shall ensure client application is operating on intended site
Client authentication shall ensure authorised client/operator is operating equipment
Rights for each user to operate functions and see data at different levels in object hierarchy settable
Data integrity: ability to deliver data from originator to destination with acceptable residual error rate, preventing malicious and false operation
Data confidentiality: data items may be encrypted to prevent malicious operation, false operation, and eavesdropping
Most operational functions and communication response time within 1 second (human time scale)
System management function delay not more than 2 seconds
Time-critical functions (set points for power control, start/stop commands) shall use short messages with high priority
Overall transfer time for time-critical services shall not exceed 0.5 seconds
Data retransmission, reconstruction, or reprocessing if lost or inaccessible
Sequence of events must be restorable including proper ordering
Local recovery procedures may incorporate redundancy of selected functions and backup of data
Communication system shall transfer stored data to central storage after restoration
Redundant communication channels shall be supported to prevent data transfer interruptions
Automatic procedures for detection of communication faults and managing redundancy of system components
Physical transport media should possibly be redundant depending on site conditions

systemFeatures.name: Plant Data Structure and Naming Convention
systemFeatures.description: Hierarchical object-based data model for wind power plant equipment with standardised naming convention, self-descriptive devices, and PICOM (Piece of Information for Communication) attributes for data interchange. All data time-stampable with 10ms accuracy. Priority: Essential.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: SCADA sends 'identify' request to wind turbine. Response: List of all objects returned with names, short descriptions, types, attributes, and available services for each object.
Stimulus: Configuration data requested. Response: PICOM attributes transmitted including format, length, accuracy, tag information, type, kind, importance, data integrity for each data object.
Stimulus: Data message received. Response: Message parsed using PICOM structure: value, name, source, sink, time tag, priority, time requirements.
functionalRequirements: Communication and objects shall be object-based (e.g., gear, generator as separate objects including measurements, calculated data, and control services)
System shall manage naming of objects and variables in hierarchical naming system in several levels

Naming convention format: AN NN BBBNN CCNNN DDNN (18 characters minimum, extendable): A=Wind power plant, NN=Number within plant, BBB=Object/system (e.g., MCD=gear, MKD=generator), CC=Component (CT=temp sensor, CY=vibration sensor, CP=pressure sensor), DD=Variable (XA=instantaneous, ZA01=average 10min, ZA02=max 10min, ZA03=min 10min, ZA04=std dev 10min, ZA21=RMS, ZA22=FFT spectrum, ZA23=FFT-enveloped spectrum, XB=binary, XQ=input)
Each device/object self-descriptive via identify request returning all objects, names, descriptions
During data transmission, messages shall include: Name, Value(s), Scan frequency, Time tag, Quality
All data time-stampable with 'last updated date + time' (UTC) at minimum 10 ms accuracy and resolution
All analogue measured values shall have readable properties: 'signal quality' and 'scanning rate'
Averaging time and measurement/averaging method documented for all data
Grouped reading of analogue and binary values in single transaction shall be possible
PICOM configuration attributes: format, length, accuracy, tag information, type (analogue/binary/file), kind (alarm/event/status/command), importance (high/normal/low), data integrity
PICOM transmission attributes: value, name, source, sink, time tag (7 bytes absolute time), priority of transmission, time requirements (cycle time or response times)

performanceRequirements: Most operational function response times within 1 second (human time scale) [Section 5.7]
System management function delay not more than 2 seconds [Section 5.7]
Overall transfer time for time-critical services (power set points, start/stop commands) not more than 0.5 seconds [Section 5.7.1]
Analogue on-line values updatable down to 1 second interval [Section 6.3]
Binary on-line values updatable down to 1 second interval [Section 6.5]
Counter values updatable down to 1 second interval [Section 6.9]
Analogue high-rate scanning up to 25 Hz for fault recording [Section 6.12.1]
Averaged value start time accuracy better than 10 ms [Section 6.3]
Time stamp accuracy and resolution at least 10 ms [Section 5.5]
Condition monitoring values updatable down to 1 second interval [Section 4.1.1]
safetyRequirements: Communication system shall NOT be used for safe and secure operation of the plant [Section 5.7]
No functions regarding safety of persons shall be based on the communication system [Section 5.7]
No functions regarding safety of plant and electrical network shall be based on the communication system [Section 5.7]
All safety functions must be self-contained in the process or in the devices where systems interface and will trip automatically [Section 5.7]
In situations where communication system is completely inaccessible, plant may be forced to shutdown by alternative means [Section 5.7]
Faults in communication system shall not cause malfunction of individual wind turbine [Section 4.1]
Faults of sub-system shall interfere as little as possible with communication system as a whole [Section 4.1]
securityRequirements: Server authentication ensures client application operates on intended site [Section 5.6.1]
Client authentication ensures authorised client/operator operates equipment [Section 5.6.1]
Role-based access control: rights for each user to operate functions and see data on different levels in object hierarchy [Section 5.6.1]
Data integrity: delivery of data from originator to destination with acceptable residual error rate [Section 5.6.2]
Data confidentiality: encryption to prevent malicious/false operation and eavesdropping [Section 5.6.3]
softwareQualityAttributes: Reliability: data retransmission, reconstruction, or reprocessing if lost or inaccessible; sequence of events restorable with proper ordering [Section 5.7.2]
Redundancy: redundant communication channels supported; processing on multiple units simultaneously; automatic fault detection and redundancy management [Section 5.7.2]
Interoperability: open and widely accepted methods; vendor-independent; high degree of interface possibilities [Section 4.1]
Extensibility: flexible to support future requirements and developments; new data definable without disturbing old compliant equipment [Section 4.1, 6.1]
Maintainability: software version control, download, activation, and retrieval; configuration data download, activation, and retrieval [Section 4.2.2]
Backward compatibility: existing plants interface via gateways for proprietary protocols [Section 5.8]
businessRules: When using specification for tendering/purchase, employer/user must specify supplementary/more detailed specifications and which parts apply [Section 1.2]
IEC TC88 Wind Turbine Systems will use this specification as basis for international communication standard [Sammanfattning]
Practical validation tests planned during 2001 at wind turbines in Sweden and Denmark

otherRequirements: Operational modes: Normal mode (standard operation), Test mode (function checking with process outputs blocked) [Section 4.2.2]
Operative mode control: authorised operator may start/stop functions or objects within system including manual activation/reset of subsystems [Section 4.2.2]
System shall support coexistence of verbal (telephone) and visual (video) communication on same transmission media [Section 2.2]

Gearbox signal naming example with LSS main bearing, HSS bearings 1&2, oil sump temperature, vibration sensors (RMS, FFT spectrum, FFT-enveloped spectrum), lube oil pump, oil cooler fan, differential pressure filter [Section 6.2.2]

glossary.definition: System that converts kinetic energy in the wind into electrical energy. [IEC 50(415)]
General name for one or more wind turbines that converts kinetic energy in the wind into electrical energy within a geographically confined area.

A wind power plant characterised by large size (typically >10 MW installed capacity), its own high voltage feeder network with small substations, a main controller for coordinated control, and specific requirements for power quality and reactive power compensation. May need to participate actively in network stability regulation.
Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition — a system that supervises and controls a geographically distributed process. [IEC 870-1-3]

Intelligent Electronic Device — e.g., numeric protection relay, bay controller, or multi-function electronic meter. May have connections as client, server, or both. Any device incorporating one or more processors with capability to receive or send data/control. [IEC 61850-02]

Piece of Information for Communication — a given data element on a given logical path with given communication attributes. Defined by CIGRE WG34.03 for data flows. Main components: data, type, performance, path.
Manufacturing Message Specification — standard ISO 9506. Used as application layer protocol in IEC 61850 and TASE.2. [IEC 61850-02]

Telecontrol Application Service Element / Inter-control Center Communication Protocol — IEC 60870-6 MMS companion standard for exchange of telecontrol data, time series, scheduling, and accounting between control centres.

OLE for Process Control — non-proprietary specification based on Microsoft OLE/COM technology defining standard interfaces for interoperability between automation/control applications, field systems, and business applications. Managed by OPC Foundation.
Remote Terminal Unit — acts as interface between communication network and substation/plant equipment. [IEC 61850-02]
Cyclic Redundancy Check — performed for each frame, value included when transmitted, used by recipient to check and if possible correct errors during transmission. [IEC 61850-02]
Ratio of total hours in a period (excluding hours when turbine could not operate due to maintenance or fault) to total hours in the period, expressed as percentage. [IEC 50(415)]
Wind Farm Main Controller — overall control unit conducting coordinated governing of plant output and grid compatibility for a wind farm.
Single Wind Power Unit — an individual wind turbine installation.
Control Unit — the local control system in a wind turbine that interfaces to the communication system.
Human Machine Interface — display screen presenting relevant data in logical format with windows, icons, menus, pointers, and keypad for user interaction. [IEC 61850-02]
Information for attracting attention to some abnormal state. [IEC 870-1-3]
Monitored information on the change of state of operational equipment. [IEC 870-1-3]
The ability of two or more IEDs, from same or different vendors, to communicate, exchange data, and use that information for correct operation. [IEC 61850-02]
A set of characteristics of a connection described in terms of quality parameters, normally negotiated between peer entities. [IEC 870-1-3]

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This flowchart shows the three wind farm network system topologies defined in the specification.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Operator["Operator LAN/WAN"]
        CS["Communication\nServer"]
        AS["Application\nServer"]
        HMI_OP["HMI for Supervision\n& Maintenance"]
        MS["Maintenance\nServer"]
    end

    subgraph NS1["Network System 1:\nInterface at Individual Nodes"]
        WFS1["Wind Farm Server"]
        CU1A["CU Turbine 1"]
        CU1B["CU Turbine 2"]
        CU1C["CU Turbine N"]
        IL1["Interface Level:\nAt each CU node"]
    end

    subgraph NS2["Network System 2:\nInterface at Wind Farm Server"]
        WFS2["Wind Farm Server\n(Gateway)"]
        CU2A["CU Turbine 1\n(Proprietary)"]
        CU2B["CU Turbine 2\n(Proprietary)"]
        IL2["Interface Level:\nAt WFS only"]
    end

    subgraph NS3["Network System 3:\nLegacy RTU Interface"]
        WFS3["Wind Farm Server"]
        RTU3A["RTU Dial-up\nSingle Turbine"]
        RTU3B["RTU Dial-up\nCluster"]
        IL3["Interface Level:\nAt gateway"]
    end

    Operator --> NS1
    Operator --> NS2
    Operator --> NS3
    WFS1 --- CU1A & CU1B & CU1C
    WFS2 --- CU2A & CU2B
    WFS3 --- RTU3A & RTU3B

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This sequence diagram shows the control flow for starting a wind turbine remotely and the subsequent alarm handling if a fault occurs.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Op as Operator (SCADA HMI)
    participant Comm as Communication System
    participant CU as Wind Turbine CU
    participant WT as Wind Turbine Process

    Op->>Comm: Authenticate (username, role)
    Comm-->>Op: Access granted (role-based services)
    Op->>Comm: WTStart command (HI priority)
    Comm->>CU: BINCOM WTStart via TCP/IP
    CU->>WT: Execute start sequence
    WT-->>CU: Status: Operation=True
    CU-->>Comm: BINSIG Operation=True (Date+time)
    Comm-->>Op: Status update displayed
    CU-->>Comm: EV WTLogXXXX RemoteStart (Date+time)
    Note over Op,WT: Normal operation...
    WT->>CU: Generator overspeed detected!
    CU->>CU: Automatic stop triggered
    CU-->>Comm: AL WTErrorXXXX Gen1Overspeed=True (Date+time)
    Comm-->>Op: ALARM displayed immediately (<0.5s)
    CU->>WT: Emergency shutdown
    WT-->>CU: Status: ShutDown=True
    CU-->>Comm: BINSIG ShutDown=True (Date+time)
    CU->>CU: Store FIFO buffer (25Hz, ±1 min)
    Op->>Comm: Request fault record
    Comm->>CU: Retrieve FIFO buffer
    CU-->>Comm: Analogue data + binary data
    Comm-->>Op: Disturbance record displayed
    Op->>Comm: WTErrorReset command
    Comm->>CU: BINCOM WTErrorReset
    CU-->>Comm: AL WTErrorXXXX=Reset (Date+time)

dataDictionary.definition: ActivePower (kW), ActivePowerPot (kW), AmbTemp (°C), BladeAngle (°), BrakeTemp (°C), BrakeWear (%), CosFi (-), Frequency (Hz), GearOilHotSpot (°C), GearOilInTemp (°C), GearOilOutTemp (°C), Gen1InTemp (°C), Gen1OutTemp (°C), Gen2InTemp (°C), Gen2OutTemp (°C), Generator1RPM (RPM), Generator1Temp1/2/3 (°C), Generator2RPM (RPM), Generator2Temp1/2/3 (°C), HSBearingTemp (°C), HSGen1Temp (°C), HSGen2Temp (°C), HydraulicPres (Bar), HydraulicTemp (°C), L1/L2/L3Current (A), L1/L2/L3Voltage (V), LSBearingTemp (°C), MainBearingTemp (°C), MSBearingTemp (°C), NacelleHumid (%RH), NacelleTemp (°C), ReactivePower (kVAr), RotorPosition (°), RotorRPM (RPM), TanFi (-), WindSpeed (m/s), YawAngle (°), YawError (°), YawMotor1Temp (°C), YawMotor2Temp (°C). All with 1% accuracy.
Low priority: SetWTReactivePower (kVAr), SetWTRotorPosition (°), SetWTFlicker (-), SetWTTanFi (-), SetWTCosFi (-). High priority: SetWTPower (kW, absolute), SetWTPctPower (%, relative to potential).

dataDictionary.term: BINSIG — Binary Status Signals (31 main states)
dataDictionary.definition: Main states: AutoRewind, AwaitingAutoReset, AwaitingAutoRewind, AwaitingManualReset, AwaitingRemoteReset, AwaitingTimeDelay, ExternalFault, HighWind, Idling, InternalFault, IsolatedOperation, ManualMode, NacelleOutOfWind, Operation, OperationAccepted, ShutDown, Stopped, SystemFault, YawingAccepted. Each with True/False pairs, date+time tagged, counter increment on shift to true.

BalanceRegOn, BladeBrakeOn, Compensation1On, Compensation2On, CompensationOn, CosFiRegOn, DiscBrakeOn, EmergencyStopOn, FlickerRegOn, FreqRegOn, GearOilFilterOn, GearOilFlowOn, GearOilMagnetOn, GearOilPumpOn, GearOilPumpThermalOut, Gen1MagneticOut, Gen1ThermalOut, Gen2MagneticOut, Gen2ThermalOut, GenCoolingFlowOn, Generator1On, Generator2On, GeneratorCoolingOn, GradientRegOn, HubArrestOn, HydraulicPumpOn, KVArRegOn, LocalReactivePowerRefOn, LVCircuitBreakerOn, MVCircuitBreakerOn, ProductionLimitOn, RemoteControlOn, RemotePowerRefOn, RemoteReactivePowerRefOn, TanFiRegOn, VibrationSensorOn, YawArrestOn, YawCCW/CWRequestOn, YawDriveCCW/CWOn. Each True/False with binary value 1/0.

Low priority (22): WTBalanceRegOff/On, WTCompOff/On, WTCosFiRegOff/On, WTErrorReset, WTFlickerRegOff/On, WTFreqRegOff/On, WTGradientRegOff/On, WTIdle, WTKVArRegOff/On, WTProductionLimitOff/On, WTTanFiRegOff/On, WTYawDriveCCW/CWOn/Off. High priority (8): WTLVCircuitBreakerOff/On, WTMVCircuitBreakerOff/On, WTRemotePowerRefOff/On, WTStart, WTStop. All with date+time tagging and counter increment.

All named WTErrorXXXX with True/Reset state pairs. Categories include: Active power regulation faults, balance regulation faults, blade angle faults, brake wear, CosFi/TanFi regulation faults, reactive power faults, circuit breaker trips, external system faults, flicker/frequency regulation faults, gear oil system alarms (filter, flow, magnet, pump thermal), gear bearing temperature highs (HS, LS, MS), generator magnetic/thermal trips, generator overspeed, generator cooling flow, main bearing temperature, hydraulic temperature/pressure, nacelle humidity/temperature, communication faults (internal/external), yaw drive thermal, yaw error, cooling media temperatures (Gen1/Gen2 in/out), air/sea marking system faults, LV circuit breaker trips, safety stop, measurement out of range, network frequency out of range, emergency power system faults, voltage/current phase faults, vibration sensor activation, winding temperatures high (Gen1/Gen2 measurement 1/2/3, yaw drive 1/2), gear hotspot/oil temperatures. Each alarm has date+time tag with counter +1 on shift to true.

WTLogXXXX entries: Remote reset performed, Remote pause performed, Remote start performed, Remote stop performed, Manual reset performed, Manual pause performed, Manual start performed, Manual stop performed, Parameter change performed (with parameter number and new value), Service mode entered. All with date+time tag and counter increment.

Counters for every binary operational signal activation: CountBalanceRegOn through CountYawiDriveCWOn, matching the BINSIG operational signal list. Each with date+time tag and counter increment on shift to true.
ProdGen1Total (MWh), ProdGen2Total (MWh), ProdPotentialTotal (MWh), ProdPotentialSubtotal (MWh), ProdGen1Subtotal (MWh), ProdGen2Subtotal (MWh). All accuracy class 1, with reset time.

Timers (in seconds) for all operational states and equipment activations: TimeAutoRewind through TimeYawiDriveCWOn and TimeYawingAccepted, matching BINSIG status/operational signals plus TimeTotal (total time since reset), TimeWindOK (time with acceptable wind speed), TimeGen1Subtotal, TimeGen2Subtotal. All with accuracy 1, reset time tagged.

introduction.purpose:
This Software Requirements Specification (SRS) identifies the requirements for the **ASPERA-3 Processing and Archiving Facility (APAF)** ground data system for the **Mars Express** mission of the European Space Agency (ESA). The APAF is a ground data system responsible for processing all ASPERA-3 telemetry. The APAF acquires telemetry data via NISN, processes data into IDFS data sets, distributes IDFS data sets to the ASPERA-3 team, provides web-based displays of the most current data for public view, stores telemetry and IDFS data sets on a local SwRI archive, and submits ASPERA-3 IDFS data sets to NASA PDS for long-term archival.

The ASPERA-3 instrument package (Analyzer of Space Plasma and Energetic Atoms, 3rd version) will be flown on the Mars Express mission and is scheduled for launch in June 2003. ASPERA-3 contains a number of different sensors that measure particles, neutral atoms, and fields in the near Martian environment. Southwest Research Institute (SwRI) provides the data system to produce data products in a form suitable for analysis and archiving using the Instrument Data File Set (IDFS) format.

**Project Identification:**
- Project Title: ASPERA for Mars Express
- Project Numbers: 15-02853 / 15-03561
- Contract Numbers: NASW-99030 / NASW-00003
- Principal Investigator: John D. Winningham (SwRI)
- Project Manager: John R. Scherrer (SwRI)
- Software Project Manager: Sandee J. Jeffers (SwRI)
- Start Date: June 14, 1999
- End Date: September 30, 2007
introduction.documentConventions:
Requirements are categorised by functional area in table format. Each requirement has a project-unique identifier using a prefix mnemonic system: FR = Functional Requirement, PR = Privacy Requirement, CR = Computer Resource Requirement, LR = Logistical Requirement, DR = Delivery Requirement. Identifiers follow the pattern APAF-<category>-<number> (e.g., APAF-FR-01). External interface identifiers use APAF-DS-II for input interfaces and APAF-DS-OI for output interfaces.

Each requirement is annotated with:
- **Verification Method(s)**: Demonstration (observing function without special equipment) or Analysis (processing accumulated calibration/test data through simulated telemetry)
- **Source**: Traceability to originating document (proposal, program-level requirements, review minutes, plans)
- **Rationale & Comments**: Why the requirement exists and how it relates to expected operations

Requirement criteria: each requirement must be Necessary (condition for acceptance), Verifiable (objective test definable), Attainable (technically feasible within schedule/budget), and Clear (singular, concise, simple).
introduction.intendedAudience: Swedish Institute of Space Physics (IRF) in Kiruna, Sweden (Principal Investigator institution), ASPERA-3 science team including all Co-Investigators (Co-I's), NASA Discovery Program Office, Southwest Research Institute software team (developers and operators), European Space Agency (ESA) Mars Express mission operations, European Science Operations Center (ESOC) in Darmstadt for telemetry interfaces, Finnish Meteorological Institute (FMI) and Umeå University (UMU) for packet definitions, NASA Planetary Data System (PDS) archival team.
introduction.productScope:
The **APAF ground data system** provides:
1. Automated daily acquisition of ASPERA-3 and Mars Express Orbit/Attitude telemetry from ESOC via NISN
2. Processing of all ASPERA-3 science data into IDFS (Instrument Data File Set) data sets
3. Processing of engineering and ancillary information for calibration and science validation into IDFS data sets
4. Generation of intermediate cleaned-up telemetry files if not provided by ESOC
5. Local archiving at SwRI of all telemetry, IDFS data sets, and intermediate files
6. Web-based public displays of most current ASPERA-3 data for Education and Public Outreach (E/PO) and instrument performance monitoring
7. Password-protected web-based displays for ASPERA-3 team science analysis (any available data, not just most current)
8. Distribution of IDFS data sets to all ASPERA-3 Co-I's within 24 hours of telemetry acquisition (if error-free)
9. Submission of calibrated and validated ASPERA-3 IDFS data to NASA PDS within 6 months of acquisition in PDS-compliant form
10. Provision of IDFS data processing algorithms, data access software, and science analysis software to IRF and Co-I's

**The seven components of the ASPERA-3 package:**
1. **DPU** — Data Processing Unit
2. **ELS** — Electron Spectrometer
3. **IMA** — Ion Mass Analyzer
4. **NPD** — Neutral Particle Detector
5. **NPI** — Neutral Particle Imager
6. **SU** — Scanning Unit
7. **OA** — Orbit/Attitude (from Mars Express spacecraft)

Each component is divided into logical groups (virtual instruments) formatted as IDFS data sets with associated Software Design Documents defining all data products.

overallDescription.productPerspective:
The APAF is a ground data system within the broader Mars Express mission infrastructure operated by ESA. It interfaces with ESOC (European Science Operations Center) in Darmstadt, Germany for telemetry acquisition via NISN (NASA Integrated Services Network). The APAF processes raw spacecraft telemetry into standardised IDFS (Instrument Data File Set) format developed by SwRI. IDFS data sets are the primary data product format for ASPERA-3 science analysis and archival.

The system feeds into two archival pipelines: (1) a local SwRI archive for ongoing availability and reprocessing, and (2) the NASA Planetary Data System (PDS) for long-term public archival. The APAF also supports Education and Public Outreach (E/PO) through web-based public data displays.

The ASPERA-3 experiment addresses fundamental questions about the Martian plasma environment, measuring integral ENA (Energetic Neutral Atom) fluxes (0.1-60 keV), mass/energy resolved neutral hydrogen and oxygen atom spectra (0.1-100 keV), ion energy/mass/angle resolved spectra (0.001-40 keV, 1-1e6 amu/q, 4π steradian), and electron spectra (0.001-20 keV, 4π steradian).
productFunctions: APAF-FR-01: Acquire from ESOC the telemetry data of the ASPERA-3 Experiment and Mars Express Orbit/Attitude — to automatically process the data on a daily basis (Verification: Demonstration; Source: ESOC Data Disposition system Interface Document)

APAF-FR-02: Process all ASPERA-3 science data into IDFS data sets (Verification: Demonstration and Analysis; Source: Proposal, NASA Discovery Office Program-Level Requirements Section 4.1.1 Baseline Science Requirements)

APAF-FR-03: Process the engineering and ancillary information necessary for calibration and science validation into IDFS data sets (Verification: Demonstration and Analysis; Source: NASA Discovery Office Program-Level Requirements Section 4.5.1 Science Data Management)

APAF-FR-04: Generate intermediate files of cleaned-up ASPERA-3 and MEX OA telemetry in the event that cleaned-up telemetry is not provided by ESOC (Verification: Demonstration and Analysis; Source: APAF Software Requirements Review Minutes)
APAF-FR-05: Store ASPERA-3 and MEX OA telemetry data on a local SwRI archive — for data availability and re-processing (Verification: Demonstration; Source: APAF Project Data Management Plan)
APAF-FR-06: Store ASPERA-3 and MEX OA IDFS data sets on a local SwRI archive — for data availability and analysis (Verification: Demonstration; Source: APAF Project Data Management Plan)

APAF-FR-06a: Store any APAF-generated intermediate files of ASPERA-3 and MEX OA cleaned-up telemetry on a local SwRI archive — for data availability and re-processing, and to support the ASPERA-3 team (Verification: Demonstration; Source: APAF Software Requirements Review Minutes)

APAF-FR-07: Provide web-based displays of the most current ASPERA-3 data for public view — for E/PO and to monitor instrument performance (Verification: Demonstration; Source: APAF Project Data Management Plan)

APAF-FR-08: Provide web-based displays defined by the ASPERA-3 team where any available ASPERA-3 data (not just most current) can be used for science analysis (Verification: Demonstration; Source: APAF Software Requirements Review Minutes)

APAF-FR-08a: The web-based displays defined by the ASPERA-3 team for science analysis shall be password protected until the ASPERA-3 data is made public (Verification: Demonstration; Source: APAF Software Requirements Review Minutes)
APAF-FR-09: The APAF ground data system shall have built-in error handling — for better data integrity (Verification: Demonstration; Source: APAF Project Data Management Plan)

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: Experienced with data system and IDFS processing software development. Responsible for APAF system development, maintenance, and software support. No training-related requirements identified. Use APAF Operations Procedures Document for operational guidance.

International team of Co-Investigators receiving IDFS data sets for science analysis. Located at multiple institutions including IRF (Sweden), FMI (Finland), UMU (Sweden). Require data access software and science analysis software. Access password-protected web displays for science analysis with any available data (not just most current).

Principal Investigator institution located in Kiruna, Sweden. Receives IDFS data processing algorithms from SwRI. Prof. Rickard Lundin serves as PI, Herman Andersson as Project Manager. Responsible for instrument integration, test, calibration, and mission operations oversight.

General public accessing web-based displays of the most current ASPERA-3 data. No authentication required for public displays. Displays serve Education and Public Outreach purposes and instrument performance monitoring.
Receives calibrated and validated ASPERA-3 IDFS data in PDS-compliant form within 6 months of acquisition. Provides long-term archival of science data for the general scientific community.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: UNIX-based computers at Southwest Research Institute (SwRI) in San Antonio, Texas, USA. Development computer: phobos.space.swri.edu (UNIX-based). Production computer: UNIX-based with archival system (to be provisioned). Web server: www.aspera-3.org. Network connection between SwRI and NISN (NASA Integrated Services Network) for telemetry data transfers from ESOC in Darmstadt, Germany.
designAndImplementationConstraints: No requirements that constrain the design and implementation of the APAF software [Section 3.9.5]
UNIX-based development and production computers required [APAF-CR-01, APAF-CR-02]
Web server required for APAF system [APAF-CR-03]
Network connection between SwRI and NISN required for data transfers [APAF-CR-04]
All ASPERA-3 data must be provided to NASA PDS in PDS-compliant form [APAF-DR-02a]
Data must be calibrated and validated prior to depositing in NASA PDS [APAF-DR-02b]
All data must be provided to NASA PDS no later than 6 months after acquisition [APAF-DR-02c]
Data processing software must meet IRF delivery requirements to support ESA Mars Express launch date [Program-Level Requirements Section 4.3]
IDFS data sets distributed electronically within 24 hours of telemetry acquisition (if transmission and processing are error-free) [APAF-DR-01a, DR-01b, DR-01c]
Distribution mechanisms determined by needs and resources available six months prior to MEX launch, documented in APAF Operations Procedures [APAF-DR-07]
