assumptionsAndDependencies: ASPERA-3 instrument package successfully integrated and launched on Mars Express in June 2003
ESOC provides telemetry data via NISN connection to SwRI; if cleaned-up telemetry is not provided by ESOC, APAF generates intermediate files [APAF-FR-04]
ASPERA-3 Packet Definitions from FMI and UMU are finalized (preliminary document from FMI issued July 2000)
Mars Express Space/Ground Interface Control Document (SGICD) defines telemetry format
ESOC Data Disposition System Interface Control Document (DDID) defines data acquisition interface
Virtual Instrument Descriptions from Software Design Documents define IDFS data set structures, instrument parameters, spacecraft parameters, and science information
The APAF data system is not required to operate in more than one state or mode [Section 3.9.1]
No installation-dependent requirements; any identified will be documented in Operations Procedures [Section 3.9.2]
SwRI software team experienced with IDFS processing; no training requirements [Section 3.9.7]
No requirements of precedence and criticality identified [Section 3.9.8]

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces:
**Public Web Displays (APAF-FR-07)**: Web-based displays at www.aspera-3.org showing most current ASPERA-3 data for public view and E/PO purposes. No authentication required.

**Science Analysis Web Displays (APAF-FR-08, APAF-FR-08a)**: Password-protected web-based displays defined by ASPERA-3 team where any available ASPERA-3 data (not just most current) can be used for science analysis. Password protection maintained until data is made public.

**Operator Input Interface (APAF-DS-II-4)**: Operator input defined in APAF Operations Procedures Document (APAF-OPD-15-03561).
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces:
**APAF-CR-01**: UNIX-based APAF system development computer (phobos.space.swri.edu — already provisioned).

**APAF-CR-02**: UNIX-based APAF system production computer with archival system (to be provisioned).

**APAF-CR-03**: Web server for APAF system (www.aspera-3.org — already provisioned).

**APAF-CR-04**: Network connection between SwRI and NISN for data transfers and distribution.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces:
**Input Interfaces:**
- **APAF-DS-II-1** (System Parameters): Configuration defined in Programmers Guide to the ASPERA-3.org Web Server, describing phobos.space.swri.edu setup.
- **APAF-DS-II-2** (ASPERA-3 and Mars Express OA Telemetry Data): Telemetry via ESOC DDID and Mars Express SGICD (ME-ESC-IF-5001), with packet definitions from FMI and UMU.
- **APAF-DS-II-3** (Virtual Instrument Descriptions): Definitions from APAF SRS, Program-Level Requirements, and Software Design Documents describing instrument parameters, spacecraft parameters, and science information. IDFS information maintained at www.ASPERA-3.org.
- **APAF-DS-II-4** (Operator Input): Configuration and operations via APAF Operations Procedures Document.

**Output Interfaces:**
- **APAF-DS-OI-1** (Distribution of ASPERA-3 and MEX OA IDFS Data Sets): Distribution per APAF Project Data Management Plan and Operations Procedures.
- **APAF-DS-OI-2** (System Logs): Generated per APAF Operations Procedures.
- **APAF-DS-OI-3** (Local Archive of Telemetry & IDFS Data): Local SwRI archive per Operations Procedures and archive configuration files.
- **APAF-DS-OI-4** (Web-based Displays): Hosted at www.ASPERA-3.org per Operations Procedures.

**IDFS Data Access and Analysis Software:**
- IDFS data access software developed by SwRI to be provided to Co-I's [APAF-DR-05]
- Southwest Data Display and Analysis System (SDDAS) for analysing IDFS-formatted output
- Science analysis software developed by SwRI to be provided to Co-I's [APAF-DR-06]
- Science analysis software integrated into NASA approved data repository [APAF-DR-04]
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: NISN (NASA Integrated Services Network) connection between SwRI and ESOC for automated daily telemetry acquisition. Electronic distribution of IDFS data sets to ASPERA-3 Co-I's at multiple international institutions. PDS data submission pipeline for NASA Planetary Data System archival.

systemFeatures.name: Telemetry Acquisition and Processing
systemFeatures.description: Automated daily acquisition of ASPERA-3 and Mars Express Orbit/Attitude telemetry from ESOC via NISN, processing into IDFS data sets covering all 7 instrument components (DPU, ELS, IMA, NPD, NPI, SU, OA). Includes generation of intermediate cleaned-up telemetry files if not provided by ESOC. Built-in error handling for data integrity. Priority: Essential.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: New ASPERA-3 telemetry arrives via NISN from ESOC. Response: APAF system automatically acquires telemetry, processes science data into IDFS data sets for each virtual instrument within each component, processing engineering and ancillary calibration data, and stores all outputs on local archive.
Stimulus: ESOC does not provide cleaned-up telemetry. Response: APAF generates intermediate files of cleaned-up ASPERA-3 and MEX OA telemetry, stores on local archive, and uses for IDFS processing.
Stimulus: Processing error occurs. Response: Built-in error handling detects and reports error; affected data flagged for manual review or re-processing.
functionalRequirements: APAF-FR-01: The APAF system shall acquire from ESOC the telemetry data of the ASPERA-3 Experiment and Mars Express Orbit/Attitude. Verification: Demonstration. Source: ESOC DDID. Rationale: To automatically process the data on a daily basis.

APAF-FR-02: The APAF system shall process all ASPERA-3 science data into IDFS data sets. Verification: Demonstration and Analysis. Source: Proposal, NASA Discovery Office Program-Level Requirements Section 4.1.1 Baseline Science Requirements; APAF PDMP.

APAF-FR-03: The APAF system shall process the engineering and ancillary information necessary for calibration and science validation into IDFS data sets. Verification: Demonstration and Analysis. Source: NASA Discovery Office Program-Level Requirements Section 4.5.1 Science Data Management.

APAF-FR-04: Intermediate files of cleaned-up ASPERA-3 and MEX OA telemetry shall be generated in the event that cleaned-up telemetry is not provided by ESOC. Verification: Demonstration and Analysis. Source: APAF Software Requirements Review Minutes. Rationale: To support ASPERA-3 team in meeting mission goals.
APAF-FR-09: The APAF ground data system shall have built-in error handling. Verification: Demonstration. Source: APAF Project Data Management Plan. Rationale: For better data integrity.

systemFeatures.name: Data Archiving and Storage
systemFeatures.description: Local archiving of all telemetry data, IDFS data sets, and APAF-generated intermediate files on a SwRI UNIX-based production computer with archival system. Supports data availability, re-processing, and analysis. Priority: Essential.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: IDFS data sets generated from daily telemetry processing. Response: Raw telemetry, IDFS data sets, and any intermediate cleaned-up files stored on local SwRI archive.
Stimulus: Reprocessing required due to updated calibration data. Response: Archived raw telemetry retrieved, reprocessed with updated algorithms, new IDFS data sets generated and archived.
functionalRequirements: APAF-FR-05: The ASPERA-3 and MEX OA telemetry data shall be stored on a local SwRI archive. Verification: Demonstration. Source: APAF PDMP. Rationale: For data availability and re-processing.
APAF-FR-06: The ASPERA-3 and MEX OA IDFS data sets shall be stored on a local SwRI archive. Verification: Demonstration. Source: APAF PDMP. Rationale: For data availability and analysis.

APAF-FR-06a: Any APAF-generated intermediate files of ASPERA-3 and MEX OA cleaned-up telemetry shall be stored on a local SwRI archive. Verification: Demonstration. Source: APAF SRR Minutes. Rationale: For data availability, re-processing, and to support the ASPERA-3 team.

systemFeatures.name: Web-Based Data Displays
systemFeatures.description: Public and password-protected web-based displays at www.aspera-3.org serving Education & Public Outreach (E/PO), instrument performance monitoring, and ASPERA-3 team science analysis. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: New ASPERA-3 data processed. Response: Web-based public displays updated with most current data at www.aspera-3.org.
Stimulus: ASPERA-3 team member accesses science displays. Response: Password authentication verified; team member views/analyses any available ASPERA-3 data (not limited to most current).
Stimulus: ASPERA-3 data made public. Response: Password protection removed from science analysis displays for that data.
functionalRequirements: APAF-FR-07: Web-based displays of the most current ASPERA-3 data shall be provided for public view. Verification: Demonstration. Source: APAF PDMP. Rationale: E/PO and monitor instrument performance.

APAF-FR-08: Web-based displays defined by the ASPERA-3 team shall be provided where any available ASPERA-3 data can be used for science analysis. Verification: Demonstration. Source: APAF SRR Minutes. Rationale: To support ASPERA-3 team in meeting mission goals and objectives.

APAF-FR-08a: The web-based displays for science analysis shall be password protected until the ASPERA-3 data is made public. Verification: Demonstration. Source: APAF SRR Minutes. Rationale: To support ASPERA-3 team intellectual property.

systemFeatures.name: Data Distribution and PDS Archival
systemFeatures.description: Distribution of IDFS data sets and software to ASPERA-3 Co-I's within 24 hours (if error-free), and submission of calibrated/validated ASPERA-3 data to NASA PDS within 6 months of acquisition in PDS-compliant form. Provision of processing algorithms, data access software, and analysis software to IRF and Co-I's. Priority: Essential.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: IDFS data sets processed from daily telemetry. Response: Electronically distributed to all ASPERA-3 Co-I's within 24 hours if transmission and processing are error-free.
Stimulus: 6-month archival deadline approaches. Response: ASPERA-3 data calibrated, validated, formatted in PDS-compliant form, and submitted to NASA PDS.
Stimulus: New analysis software developed by SwRI. Response: Software integrated into NASA approved data repository and made available to Co-I's.
functionalRequirements: APAF-DR-01: ASPERA-3 and MEX OA IDFS data and any APAF-generated intermediate files shall be provided to all ASPERA-3 Co-I's. Verification: Demonstration. Source: NASA Discovery Office Program-Level Requirements Section 4.1.1; APAF SRR Minutes. Rationale: Baseline Science Requirements; support ASPERA-3 team.

APAF-DR-01a: ASPERA-3 IDFS data electronically distributed shall be provided to Co-I's within 24 hours of acquiring ASPERA-3 telemetry as long as transmission and processing are error-free. Verification: Demonstration. Source: APAF SRR Minutes.

APAF-DR-01b: MEX OA IDFS data electronically distributed shall be provided to Co-I's within 24 hours of acquiring MEX OA telemetry as long as transmission and processing are error-free. Verification: Demonstration. Source: APAF SRR Minutes.

APAF-DR-01c: Any APAF-generated intermediate files electronically distributed shall be provided to Co-I's within 24 hours of acquiring telemetry as long as transmission and processing are error-free. Verification: Demonstration. Source: APAF SRR Minutes.
APAF-DR-02: ASPERA-3 IDFS data and MEX OA IDFS data shall be provided to NASA PDS. Verification: Demonstration and Analysis. Source: NASA Discovery Office Program-Level Requirements Section 4.1.1.
APAF-DR-02a: ASPERA-3 data shall be provided to NASA PDS in PDS-compliant form. Verification: Analysis. Source: NASA Discovery Office Program-Level Requirements Section 4.5.1 Science Data Management.
APAF-DR-02b: ASPERA-3 data shall be calibrated and validated prior to depositing in the NASA PDS. Verification: Analysis. Source: NASA Discovery Office Program-Level Requirements Section 4.5.1.

APAF-DR-02c: ASPERA-3 data shall be provided to NASA PDS no later than 6 months after acquisition. Verification: Demonstration. Source: NASA Discovery Office Program-Level Requirements Section 4.5.1; Section 4.4 Ground System Requirements.
APAF-DR-03: SwRI shall provide IDFS data processing algorithms to IRF. Verification: Demonstration. Source: Program-Level Requirements Section 4.4 Ground System Requirements.
APAF-DR-04: Science analysis software shall be integrated into the NASA approved data repository. Verification: Demonstration. Source: Program-Level Requirements Section 4.5.2 Analysis Software.
APAF-DR-05: IDFS data access software developed by SwRI shall be made available to the ASPERA-3 Co-I's. Verification: Demonstration. Source: APAF SRR Minutes. Rationale: Support ASPERA-3 team.

APAF-DR-06: Science analysis software developed by SwRI to analyze IDFS-formatted data shall be made available to the ASPERA-3 Co-I's. Verification: Demonstration. Source: APAF SRR Minutes. Rationale: Support ASPERA-3 team.

APAF-DR-07: How the ASPERA-3 and MEX OA data and exactly what data sets are to be distributed to each Co-I shall be determined by needs and resources available six months prior to MEX launch. Distribution mechanisms shall be defined/described in the APAF Operations Procedures Document. Verification: Demonstration. Source: APAF SRR Minutes.

performanceRequirements: APAF-DR-01a/01b/01c: Electronic distribution of IDFS data to Co-I's within 24 hours of telemetry acquisition (if error-free)
APAF-FR-01: Automated daily processing — telemetry acquired and processed on a daily basis
APAF-DR-02c: ASPERA-3 data provided to NASA PDS no later than 6 months after acquisition
safetyRequirements: The APAF data system will not impose hazards to personnel, property, or the environment. Therefore, there are no safety requirements of the APAF ground data system. [Section 3.9.3]
securityRequirements: APAF-PR-01: The APAF system web server shall be password protected where appropriate to allow only pertinent ASPERA-3 team members access. Verification: Demonstration. Source: APAF Software Development Plan Section 10.0 Security Plan.
APAF-FR-08a: Science analysis web displays password protected until data made public.
No other security requirements associated with the APAF ground data system [Section 3.5]
softwareQualityAttributes: Software quality factors considered (but not formally required): reliability, maintainability, availability, flexibility, portability, testability, and usability [Section 3.9.4]
Built-in error handling for data integrity [APAF-FR-09]
The APAF data system and associated software will be developed with the highest quality possible [Section 3.9.4]
businessRules: The ASPERA-3 MO shall provide the complete data set of the integrated ASPERA-3 investigation to all Co-I's and the NASA PDS [Program-Level Requirements Section 4.1.1]
The time required to complete calibration/validation shall be the minimum necessary to provide accurate scientific data, but shall not exceed six months after acquisition [Section 4.5.1]
SwRI shall develop the ASPERA-3 data reduction system and software for the full ASPERA-3 experiment including interfacing with all sensor teams [Section 4.4]
Data processing software shall meet IRF delivery requirements to support ESA Mars Express launch date and operations [Section 4.3]
SwRI shall provide system maintenance and software support [APAF-LR-01, APAF-LR-02]

otherRequirements: APAF-LR-01: SwRI shall provide APAF system maintenance. Verification: Demonstration. Source: NASA Discovery Office Program-Level Requirements Section 4.4. Rationale: To satisfy mission needs.

APAF-LR-02: SwRI shall provide software support for the APAF system. Verification: Demonstration. Source: NASA Discovery Office Program-Level Requirements Section 4.4. Rationale: To satisfy mission needs.
The APAF data system is not required to operate in more than one state or mode [Section 3.9.1]
There are no installation-dependent requirements; any identified shall be documented in APAF Operations Procedures [Section 3.9.2]
There are no requirements of precedence and criticality at this time [Section 3.9.8]
There are no personnel-related requirements; APAF Operations Procedures Document will aid personnel [Section 3.9.6]
There are no training-related requirements; APAF Operations Procedures shall provide sufficient detail [Section 3.9.7]

glossary.definition: ASPERA-3 Processing and Archiving Facility — the ground data system responsible for processing all ASPERA-3 telemetry, producing IDFS data sets, archiving data, and distributing to the science team and NASA PDS.

Analyzer of Space Plasma and Energetic Atoms (3rd version) — an instrument package on the Mars Express mission measuring particles, neutral atoms, and fields in the near Martian environment. Contains DPU, ELS, IMA, NPD, NPI, and SU sensors plus SU.

Instrument Data File Set — the standard data format developed by SwRI for structuring ASPERA-3 science data. Each physical component is divided into virtual instruments, each formatted as an IDFS data set.
Southwest Data Display and Analysis System — SwRI software tool for analysing IDFS-formatted data output from the APAF system.
European Science Operations Center — ESA facility in Darmstadt, Germany providing Mars Express telemetry data to SwRI via NISN.
NASA Integrated Services Network — the network connection between SwRI and ESOC used for automated telemetry data acquisition.
NASA Planetary Data System — the long-term archival system where calibrated and validated ASPERA-3 data is deposited within 6 months of acquisition in PDS-compliant form.
Mars Express — the European Space Agency mission carrying the ASPERA-3 instrument, scheduled for launch June 2003.
Data Processing Unit — the central processing component of the ASPERA-3 instrument package.
Electron Spectrometer — ASPERA-3 sensor measuring electron spectra in 0.001-20 keV range with 4π steradian coverage.
Ion Mass Analyzer — ASPERA-3 sensor measuring ion energy/mass/angle resolved spectra in 0.001-40 keV, 1-1e6 amu/q, with 4π steradian coverage.
Neutral Particle Detector — ASPERA-3 sensor measuring mass/energy resolved neutral hydrogen and oxygen atom spectra in 0.1-100 keV range.
Neutral Particle Imager — ASPERA-3 sensor measuring integral ENA fluxes in 0.1-60 keV range.
Scanning Unit — mechanical scanning component of the ASPERA-3 instrument package.
Orbit/Attitude — spacecraft orbit and attitude information from Mars Express used as ancillary data for ASPERA-3 science analysis.
Virtual Instrument Description File — file defining the structure and parameters of each virtual instrument (logical group) within a physical ASPERA-3 component.
Plot Interface Definition File — file defining parameters for graphical display of ASPERA-3 data.
Co-Investigator — member of the ASPERA-3 science team who receives IDFS data sets and software for science analysis.
Swedish Institute of Space Physics (Institutet för Rymdfysik) — located in Kiruna, Sweden; the Principal Investigator institution for ASPERA-3.
Finnish Meteorological Institute — located in Helsinki, Finland; provides ASPERA-3 packet definitions.
Education and Public Outreach — the public engagement mission served by web-based displays of ASPERA-3 data.
Southwest Research Institute — located in San Antonio, Texas USA; develops and operates the APAF ground data system.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This flowchart shows the end-to-end APAF data processing pipeline from telemetry acquisition to archival and distribution.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Acquisition["Data Acquisition"]
        ESOC["ESOC Darmstadt\n(Mars Express S/C)"]
        NISN["NISN Network"]
    end

    subgraph APAF["APAF Ground Data System (SwRI)"]
        ACQ["Telemetry\nAcquisition"]
        CLEAN["Intermediate\nCleaned-Up Files\n(if needed)"]
        PROC["IDFS\nProcessing"]
        CAL["Calibration &\nValidation"]
        ARCH["Local SwRI\nArchive"]
        WEB["Web Server\nwww.aspera-3.org"]
        ERR["Error\nHandling"]
    end

    subgraph Components["ASPERA-3 Components"]
        DPU["DPU Data"]
        ELS["ELS Data"]
        IMA["IMA Data"]
        NPD["NPD Data"]
        NPI["NPI Data"]
        SU["SU Data"]
        OA["OA Data"]
    end

    subgraph Distribution["Data Distribution"]
        COI["Co-I Teams\n(within 24 hrs)"]
        PDS["NASA PDS\n(within 6 months)"]
        PUB["Public\n(E/PO Displays)"]
        SCI["Science Team\n(Password Protected)"]
    end

    ESOC -->|Telemetry| NISN --> ACQ
    ACQ --> CLEAN
    ACQ --> PROC
    CLEAN --> PROC
    PROC --> DPU & ELS & IMA & NPD & NPI & SU & OA
    DPU & ELS & IMA & NPD & NPI & SU & OA --> CAL
    PROC --> ARCH
    CLEAN --> ARCH
    ACQ --> ARCH
    CAL --> COI
    CAL --> PDS
    PROC --> WEB
    WEB --> PUB
    WEB --> SCI
    ERR -.->|monitors| PROC
flowchartDiagram.caption: APAF End-to-End Data Processing Pipeline

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This sequence diagram shows the daily automated telemetry processing and distribution flow.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant ESOC as ESOC (Darmstadt)
    participant NISN as NISN Network
    participant APAF as APAF System (SwRI)
    participant Archive as Local SwRI Archive
    participant Web as www.aspera-3.org
    participant CoI as Co-I Teams
    participant PDS as NASA PDS

    Note over ESOC,PDS: Daily Automated Processing Cycle
    ESOC->>NISN: Mars Express telemetry
    NISN->>APAF: ASPERA-3 + MEX OA telemetry
    APAF->>APAF: Check if cleaned-up telemetry provided
    alt Cleaned telemetry not provided by ESOC
        APAF->>APAF: Generate intermediate cleaned-up files
        APAF->>Archive: Store intermediate files
    end
    APAF->>Archive: Store raw telemetry
    APAF->>APAF: Process science data into IDFS
    APAF->>APAF: Process engineering/calibration data
    APAF->>Archive: Store IDFS data sets
    APAF->>Web: Update public displays (most current)
    APAF->>Web: Update science displays (all data)
    APAF->>CoI: Distribute IDFS within 24 hours
    Note over APAF,PDS: Within 6 months of acquisition
    APAF->>APAF: Calibrate and validate data
    APAF->>PDS: Submit PDS-compliant ASPERA-3 data
sequenceDiagram.caption: Daily Automated Telemetry Processing and Distribution

dataDictionary.definition: Integral ENA fluxes (0.1-60 keV via NPI); Mass/energy resolved neutral H and O atom spectra (0.1-100 keV via NPD); Ion energy/mass/angle resolved spectra (0.001-40 keV, 1-1e6 amu/q, 4π steradian via IMA); Electron spectra (0.001-20 keV, 4π steradian via ELS); Ions and electrons (0.001-40 keV, 1-1e6 amu/q via combined sensors).

A logical grouping within a physical ASPERA-3 component, formatted as an IDFS data set. Each component (DPU, ELS, IMA, NPD, NPI, SU, OA) may contain multiple virtual instruments. Definitions maintained at www.ASPERA-3.org website.
System Parameters — configuration files for the APAF system on phobos.space.swri.edu, documented in Programmers Guide to the ASPERA-3.org Web Server.
ASPERA-3 and Mars Express Orbit/Attitude Telemetry Data — raw telemetry acquired via ESOC DDID, Mars Express SGICD (ME-ESC-IF-5001), and ASPERA-3 Packet Definitions (FMI/UMU).

Virtual Instrument Descriptions — structure definitions from APAF SRS, Program-Level Requirements, and individual Software Design Documents. Includes instrument parameters, spacecraft parameters, and science information for IDFS formatting.
Operator Input — operational commands and configuration per APAF Operations Procedures Document (APAF-OPD-15-03561).
Distribution of ASPERA-3 and MEX OA IDFS Data Sets — electronically distributed to Co-I's per APAF PDMP and Operations Procedures. Target: within 24 hours of telemetry acquisition.
System Logs — operational logs generated per APAF Operations Procedures for system monitoring and troubleshooting.
Local Archive of ASPERA-3 and MEX OA Telemetry & IDFS Data — stored on SwRI production computer archival system per Operations Procedures and local archive configuration files.
Web-based Displays — public and password-protected science displays hosted at www.ASPERA-3.org.
