introduction.purpose: This document defines the functional requirements specification (FRS) for the Number Portability Administration Center Service Management System (NPAC SMS), enabling Local Number Portability (LNP) for Service Providers. It establishes the baseline end-user functional requirements including provisioning, disconnect, repair, conflict resolution, disaster recovery, order cancellation, audit, report, and data management capabilities. The NPAC SMS serves as the central clearinghouse for all ported telephone number information, managing the database of routing data required to effect the porting of telephone numbers between Service Providers across North American regions.
introduction.documentConventions: Requirements use a structured naming convention: the first character denotes type (A=assumption, C=constraint, R=requirement). The second character indicates origin: N=narrative portion of the RFP, X=added upon award, R=identified during post-award requirements analysis. The keyword "shall" indicates mandatory requirements that must be demonstrated during design review and acceptance testing; "is", "will", and "should" indicate guidance or preference. All time references in SOA/LSMS messages and reports are in GMT; NPAC Administrative Interface uses local time; system tunables use Central Time.
introduction.intendedAudience: This document is intended for NPAC operations personnel, LNP Service Providers (both wireline and wireless), NPAC SMS system developers and vendors, regulatory bodies including the North American Numbering Council (NANC) and the LNPA Working Group, and telecommunications engineers implementing Local Number Portability. The detailed requirements serve architects designing SOA and LSMS interfaces, operations teams managing porting workflows, and compliance officers verifying adherence to FCC mandates.
introduction.productScope: The NPAC SMS is a hardware and software platform containing the database of information required to effect the porting of telephone numbers. It receives customer information from old and new Service Providers including the Location Routing Number (LRN), validates received information, downloads new routing information upon activation, maintains records of all ported numbers and transaction history, provides audit functionality, and transmits LNP routing information to Service Providers to maintain synchronization. The system supports National Number Pooling via NPA-NXX-X (1K Block) management with Efficient Data Representation (EDR) for compatible LSMSs. Scope excludes real-time call processing, SP-to-SP interactions, customer authorization verification, and SP internal network operations.
references: NPAC SMS Interoperable Interface Specification (IIS), Version 3.0.2, December 4, 2000
Illinois Commerce Commission NPAC/SMS Request for Proposal (ICC NPAC/SMS RFP), February 6, 1996
Generic Requirements for SCP Application and GTT Function for Number Portability, ICC LNP Workshop
Generic Switching and Signaling Requirements for Number Portability, v1.03, September 4, 1996
FCC 96-286 First Report And Order, CC Docket No. 95-116, July 2, 1996
CTIA Report on Wireless Portability Version 2, July 7, 1998
Report on Local Number Portability, Industry Numbering Committee (INC)

overallDescription.productPerspective: The NPAC SMS operates as a centralized clearinghouse within the North American telecommunications infrastructure for Local Number Portability. It interfaces with Service Provider systems through two primary electronic interfaces: the SOA (Service Order Administration) to NPAC SMS Interface for order management and notifications, and the NPAC SMS to Local SMS Interface for routing data distribution to Service Provider network elements. Additional interfaces include the NPAC Administrative GUI (OpGUI) for NPAC operations personnel, the NPAC SOA Low-tech Interface for manual Service Provider operations, a Web bulletin board for network data publication, and FTP sites for bulk data downloads. The system supports OSI protocol stack communications with CMIP-based messaging, NTP clock synchronization to Stratum 1 hosts, and both primary and backup/disaster recovery configurations.
productFunctions: Provisioning Service: Manages the end-to-end lifecycle of porting a telephone number including subscription version creation, concurrence validation between old and new Service Providers, activation with real-time routing data broadcast to all Local SMSs, and failure/retry handling.

Disconnect Service: Processes disconnection of ported numbers with support for immediate and deferred (effective release date) disconnection, broadcasting delete actions to all Local SMSs with tunable retry parameters.
Repair Service: Supports audit-based troubleshooting and subscription data resynchronization via broadcast of correct routing data to affected Service Provider networks.

Conflict Resolution: Manages disputes between Service Providers with configurable conflict expiration windows (default 30 calendar days), conflict restriction windows, and automated cancellation upon timer expiration.

Disaster Recovery and Backup: Provides planned and unplanned failover to backup NPAC with Service Provider notification, cutover quiet periods, and bidirectional reconnection procedures maintaining identical database state.
Order Cancellation: Handles cancel-pending workflows with concurrence requirements from both Service Providers, tunable acknowledgment windows, and automatic status transitions upon timer expiration.

Audit Administration: Provides on-demand audits of LSMS data against NPAC SMS data, random database integrity sampling, audit discrepancy reporting, and automatic LSMS correction for detected discrepancies.
Report Generation: Supports pre-defined and ad-hoc report generation with distribution to electronic files, local/remote printers, e-mail, and FAX machines.

National Number Pooling: Manages NPA-NXX-X (1K Block) allocation and lifecycle including NPA-NXX-X Holder Information, Block Holder Information with EDR, automated block completeness verification, and cascading delete operations for de-pooling.
NPA Split Processing: Handles NPA splits with permissive dialing periods, automatic NPA-NXX-X creation, subscription version migration to new NPA, and coordinated block data conversion.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: Authorized staff operating the NPAC Administrative Interface (OpGUI). Full access to create/modify/delete NPA-NXX, LRN, NPA-NXX-X, and Service Provider data. Can initiate mass updates, manage NPA splits, schedule block creation events, resend failed broadcasts, run reports, and perform data administration.

Telecommunications carrier gaining a ported number. Initiates subscription version creation, provides routing data (LRN, DPC/SSN values), sends activation requests, and receives conflict/status notifications via SOA to NPAC SMS Interface.

Telecommunications carrier losing a ported number. Can concur or deny porting authorization, initiate conflict status (one time only), and receive all status change notifications. Cannot activate subscription versions.
The Service Provider currently serving the ported number. Can initiate disconnects, modify active subscription version routing data, and request audits.
Service Provider assigned a 1K number pool block. Can create blocks via SOA interface (SOA Origination=TRUE), modify active block routing data, and receive block status notifications.

Service Provider network element receiving routing data downloads. Supports either EDR mode (receives Block objects) or non-EDR mode (receives individual Subscription Versions). Responds to audit queries and receives bulk data downloads.
System administrator responsible for managing tunable parameters, user access control, security administration, and system configuration.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The NPAC SMS operates as a high-availability telecommunications platform with primary and backup/disaster recovery configurations. Uses OSI 7-layer protocol stack with CMIP for machine-to-machine interface communications. Supports NTP clock synchronization to Stratum 1 hosts, GMT-based timestamps, and Central Time for system tunables. FTP sites provide bulk data download capabilities. Must maintain 99.5% minimum availability with no more than 8 hours downtime per month.
designAndImplementationConstraints: C1-1: The NPAC SMS is not involved in real time call processing.
C1-2: The NPAC SMS is not involved in facilitating or tracking Service Provider-to-Service Provider activities.
CN2-1: Service Provider interactions, customer authorization processes, and mechanisms for obtaining porting consent are beyond NPAC SMS scope.
RR3-3: Pipe characters (|) are prohibited in all text string inputs.
OSI 7-layer protocol stack required for SOA and LSMS electronic interfaces.
All timestamps must use Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) for SOA/LSMS messages and reports.
Service Provider IDs must be 4-character alphanumeric codes from a proper source.
NTP synchronization to Stratum 1 host is mandatory for system clock accuracy.
CMIP request retry mechanism with configurable x-by-y parameters.
userDocumentation: NPAC SMS Functional Requirements Specification (FRS) Version 3.0.23 - This document.
NPAC SMS Interoperable Interface Specification (IIS) Version 3.0.2.
System Tunables Reference (Appendix C) - Lists all configurable parameters with default values.
assumptionsAndDependencies: A1-1: Service Providers will be billed proportionally to their usage of NPAC SMS services.
AR1-1: All NPAC Customers will obtain a unique Service Provider ID from a proper source.
AR3-1: Time references are in GMT for SOA/LSMS messages and reports.
AN3-4.1: The service provider responsible for an NPA split communicates split information to the NPAC.
AR6-1: A range activate will contain an average of 20 TNs.
AR6-2: 20% of all downloads will be processed via range activations.
A8-1: NPAC SMS will process audit requests from Service Providers immediately.
AR10-1: NPAC-initiated downtime does not include downtime needed for software release updates.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: Three user-facing interfaces: (1) NPAC Administrative Interface (OpGUI) - GUI for NPAC operations personnel managing all system aspects. (2) NPAC SOA Low-tech Interface - manual access for Service Provider personnel. (3) Web Bulletin Board - publishes NPA-NXX, LRN, NPA-NXX-X, and Service Provider information.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: Primary and backup/disaster recovery hardware configurations. 4mm DAT tape drives for off-line batch downloads. FTP server infrastructure with SP-specific subdirectories. NTP Stratum 1 host for system clock synchronization.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: SOA to NPAC SMS Interface - bidirectional machine-to-machine using OSI/CMIP for subscription management, network data, block management, audit, and notification recovery. NPAC SMS to Local SMS Interface - distributing routing data downloads, SV broadcasts, block broadcasts, network data updates, and audit queries.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: OSI 7-layer protocol stack with NSAP (12 bytes), TSAP (4 bytes), SSAP (4 bytes), and PSAP (4 bytes) addressing. Internet addresses for Web interface. Configurable retry mechanisms (x-by-y pattern) for failed broadcasts. Message queuing during Service Provider downtime periods.

systemFeatures.name: Subscription Version Lifecycle Management
systemFeatures.description: Complete lifecycle management of ported telephone numbers through 10 distinct status states (Pending, Conflict, Active, Sending, Failed, Partial Failure, Disconnect Pending, Old, Canceled, Cancel Pending) with automated state transitions driven by tunable timers, Service Provider actions, and broadcast results.
stimulusResponseSequences: New SP creates SV -> NPAC validates data -> Creates pending version -> Notifies old SP for concurrence.
Both SPs concur, New SP activates -> NPAC sets to Sending, broadcasts to all LSMSs -> Sets to Active/Partial Failure/Failed based on responses.
Current SP disconnects -> Immediate or deferred (effective release date) broadcast of delete -> Sets to Old.
functionalRequirements: R5-1.1: SV instances have one status at any time from the set of 10 defined statuses.
RN5-1: Only one pending/cancel-pending/conflict/disconnect-pending/failed/partial-failure SV per subscription.
RN5-2: Only one active Subscription Version per subscription.
R5-18.1 through R5-18.10: Field-level validation for all subscription data upon creation.
R5-21.1: Long and short Initial Concurrence Window tunables (default 9 and 1 business hours).
R5-23.1: Long and short Final Concurrence Window tunables (default 9 and 1 business hours).
R5-45.1: Conflict Expiration Window tunable (default 30 calendar days).
R5-2.1: Old Subscription Retention tunable (default 18 calendar months).
R5-5: Individual SVs created for TN range requests.
R5-6: All subscription administration transactions logged with comprehensive event details.

systemFeatures.name: National Number Pooling (NPA-NXX-X Block Management)
systemFeatures.description: Manages 1000-number (1K) block allocation using NPA-NXX-X Holder Information and Block Holder Information. Supports EDR for compatible LSMSs receiving Block objects, and individual SV broadcasts for non-EDR LSMSs. Includes automated block completeness verification, cascading delete for de-pooling, and synchronized status management.
stimulusResponseSequences: NPAC creates NPA-NXX-X -> Validates NPA-NXX, effective date, duplicates, pending SVs -> Stores and broadcasts.
Block creation triggered -> Validates effective date, pending SVs, routing data -> Creates Block as Sending -> Broadcasts to EDR and non-EDR LSMSs -> Status per response matrix.
NPA-NXX-X deletion -> Cascading delete: SV deletes to non-EDR, Block deletes to EDR -> Updates all to Old -> Deletes NPA-NXX-X.
functionalRequirements: RR3-52 through RR3-60: NPA-NXX-X and EDR indicator management per SP.
RR3-61 through RR3-74: NPA-NXX-X Holder Information CRUD with validation.
RR3-119 through RR3-143: Block Holder operations, status/FSL synchronization.
RR3-137.1 through RR3-138.2: Status synchronization matrices for Block/SV coordination.
RR3-144 through RR3-153: Block creation with SOA origination, broadcast, status updates.
RR3-169 through RR3-179: Block deletion as part of cascading de-pool.
RR3-183 through RR3-184: Default routing restoration for disconnected pooled numbers.

systemFeatures.name: NPA Split Processing
systemFeatures.description: Manages NPA splits with permissive dialing periods. Automatically updates SVs, NPA-NXX-X holder information, and block data during the split lifecycle.
stimulusResponseSequences: NPAC enters NPA Split data -> Validates NPA-NXX existence, effective dates, ownership -> Stores split.
PDP begins -> Updates all SVs to new NPA, creates old SV copies, creates new NPA-NXX-X, converts blocks.
PDP ends -> Removes old NPA-NXX mapping, deletes old NPA-NXX-X, removes split info.
functionalRequirements: RN3-1 through RN3-2: Support permissive dialing accepting both old and new NPAs.
RN3-4.1 through RN3-4.37: Comprehensive NPA Split validation and exception processing.
RR3-31 through RR3-40: NPA-NXX-X Holder NPA Split operations.
RR3-41 through RR3-51.2: Block Holder NPA Split operations.
RR3-218: SV broadcasts use new NPA-NXX during permissive dialing.
RR3-219: Automatic deletion of old NPA-NXX at end of permissive dialing.

systemFeatures.name: Service Provider Data Administration
systemFeatures.description: Manages SP records lifecycle: creation with unique SPID validation, modification by authorized parties, deletion with dependency checks, and query capabilities. Supports associated SP relationships.
stimulusResponseSequences: NPAC creates SP -> Checks duplicate SPID -> Collects data -> Validates -> Creates record.
SP or NPAC modifies data -> Validates permissions -> Revalidates -> Updates -> Broadcasts name changes.
NPAC deletes SP -> Checks SV/LRN/NPA-NXX/NPA-NXX-X dependencies -> Archives to history -> Notifies.
functionalRequirements: R4-1 through R4-5.2: CRUD operations for Service Providers.
R4-6 through R4-11: Creation with duplicate detection and validation.
R4-13 through R4-17: Modification with restrictions by user type.
R4-20 through R4-22.3: Deletion with dependency checking and archival.
RR4-1 through RR4-8: Removal dependencies and duplicate validation.
RR3-16 through RR3-29: Multiple SPID per SOA association support.

systemFeatures.name: Audit Administration
systemFeatures.description: Three audit mechanisms: on-demand SP-initiated audits, database extract comparisons, and random database integrity sampling. Supports automatic LSMS discrepancy correction.
stimulusResponseSequences: SP requests audit -> NPAC queries targeted LSMSs -> Compares data -> Corrects discrepancies -> Reports results.
Scheduled integrity sampling -> Selects random active SVs -> Compares -> Reports and corrects discrepancies.
functionalRequirements: R8-1 through R8-8: Audit request processing and capabilities.
Audit discrepancy reporting to requesting SOA.
Database integrity sampling with random SV selection.
Number Pool environment audit accommodation.

systemFeatures.name: Security and Access Control
systemFeatures.description: Comprehensive security: identification, authentication with password requirements, role-based access control, data integrity, audit trail with intrusion detection, continuity of service, OSI security compliance, and encryption key exchange.
stimulusResponseSequences: User access -> Identify -> Authenticate -> Check permissions -> Grant/deny with audit logging.
Security event -> Generate audit entry -> Intrusion detection analysis -> Security monitoring reports.
functionalRequirements: R7-1 through R7-3.1: Identification and authentication.
R7-4.1 through R7-4.2: System and resource-level access control.
R7-5: Data and system integrity protection.
R7-6.1 through R7-6.2: Audit log generation with intrusion detection.
R7-7: Continuity of service requirements.
R7-9: OSI Security Environment including encryption, authentication, integrity, non-repudiation, access control, and key exchange.

systemFeatures.name: Report Generation and Distribution
systemFeatures.description: Pre-defined and ad-hoc reports with multi-destination distribution. Includes specialized reports for NPA splits, pending-like SVs, and block operations.
stimulusResponseSequences: User requests report -> System generates per format -> Distributes to destinations (file, printer, email, fax).
System generates NPA Split Current/Pending and History reports.
functionalRequirements: R9-1 through R9-3: Report request, generation, and delivery.
R9-3.1: National Number Pooling specific reports.
RR9-11 through RR9-18: Pending-like SV reports for number pooling.
RN3-4.31 and RN3-4.32: NPA Split reports.

systemFeatures.name: Interface Recovery and Resynchronization
systemFeatures.description: Recovery mechanisms for missed messages during SP downtime. Supports network data, subscription data (LSMS only), and notification recovery with tunable time range validation.
stimulusResponseSequences: SP re-associates in recovery mode -> Requests recovery for time range -> NPAC validates and sends data -> New messages queued -> SP signals recovery complete -> Queued messages delivered.
CMIP request fails -> Retry x times at y-minute intervals -> If failed, notify NPAC personnel.
functionalRequirements: R6-4.1 through R6-4.4: Protocol requirements and interface performance.
R6-6: CMIP request retry with configurable x-by-y mechanism.
R6-7: Notification recovery with criteria size validation.
R6-8: Network data recovery with time range validation.
R6-9: Subscription data recovery for LSMS.
Recovery mode suspends retry counter during SP recovery.

systemFeatures.name: Bulk Data Download and Database Extracts
systemFeatures.description: Complete dataset recovery via file downloads. Supports subscription, network, NPA-NXX-X, and block data with configurable selection criteria and automated FTP distribution.
stimulusResponseSequences: NPAC requests bulk download -> Selects data type and SP filter -> System generates files per Appendix E -> Places in SP FTP subdirectory.
NPAC requests Block download -> Selects Active/PF or Latest View -> Specifies time/block range -> Generates sorted file -> FTP distribution.
functionalRequirements: R3-8: Off-line batch download via 4mm DAT tape and FTP.
R3-14 through R3-17: Periodic database extracts to ASCII files on FTP site.
RR3-220 through RR3-227: Bulk data download creation with naming conventions and criteria.
RR3-198 through RR3-207: Block bulk data download with Active/Latest-View selection.

systemFeatures.name: Performance, Reliability, and Billing
systemFeatures.description: System availability, capacity, performance requirements, and usage recording for proportional SP billing.
stimulusResponseSequences: System monitors availability -> Alerts for downtime exceedances -> Planned downtime requires 24-hour advance notification.
System records per-SP usage -> Generates billing data for proportional cost allocation.
functionalRequirements: R10-1 through R10-4: Availability (99.5% min), reliability, capacity, and performance.
R10-5: Planned downtime with notification requirements.
R11-1 through R11-2: Usage recording for proportional billing.
A1-2: Resource accounting must not degrade basic system performance.

performanceRequirements: System must maintain 99.5% minimum availability with no more than 8 hours downtime per month.
Planned downtime requires 24-hour advance notification to all Service Providers.
CMIP request retry mechanism with configurable retry count and interval parameters.
Range activations contain an average of 20 TNs with 20% of downloads via range activations.
Resource accounting must not degrade basic system performance.
NTP synchronization to Stratum 1 host for accurate system clock.
Must support simultaneous associations with multiple SP SOA and LSMS systems.
safetyRequirements: Disaster recovery must maintain identical application version and database state between primary and backup.
Cutover quiet periods enforced during primary-to-backup and backup-to-primary transitions.
Off-line batch downloads via 4mm DAT tape for Local SMS disaster recovery.
Automated daily Block completeness verification for 1K number pooling blocks.
securityRequirements: Role-based access control with system-level and resource-level permissions.
Password authentication with configurable requirements.
OSI security mechanisms: encryption, authentication, data origin authentication, integrity, non-repudiation, access control, audit trail, and key exchange.
Audit log generation for all security-relevant events.
Intrusion detection and reporting capabilities.
Encryption key exchange protocol (Appendix D).
Transaction logging of all subscription administration activities.
softwareQualityAttributes: Reliability: Primary and backup configurations with automated failover.
Availability: Minimum 99.5% uptime with planned downtime windows.
Recoverability: Network, subscription, and notification recovery mechanisms.
Configurability: Over 50 system tunables (Appendix C).
Auditability: Comprehensive audit trail and database integrity sampling.
Backward Compatibility: Support for both EDR and non-EDR LSMSs and release migration (Appendix H).
businessRules: Only one pending/cancel-pending/conflict/disconnect-pending/failed/partial-failure SV per TN.
Old SP can place SV into conflict only one time.
Conflict Restriction Window prevents Old SP conflict after tunable time when Final Concurrence Timer expired.
Timer type selection uses longer timer when SP types mismatch.
Business hours selection uses shorter hours when SP settings mismatch.
SPs can only modify their own Service Provider data.
NPA-NXX and LRN deletions prohibited if active SVs or NPA-NXX-X info exists.
Pipe characters (|) prohibited in all text string inputs.

otherRequirements: GNU General Public License (GPL) on all documentation and derivatives.
Release migration from NPAC SMS Release 2.0 to 3.0 requires conversion of pre-EDR pooled data to EDR format (Appendix H).
System tunables encompass six categories: Subscriptions, Communications, Audits, Logs, Keys, and Blocks.

glossary.definition: Number Portability Administration Center Service Management System - centralized platform managing ported telephone number routing information for LNP.
Local Number Portability - ability of subscribers to retain telephone numbers when switching between service providers.
Location Routing Number - 10-digit identifier for the switch on which portable NPA-NXXs or Number Pool Blocks reside.
Service Order Administration - Service Provider system interfacing with NPAC SMS for subscription version operations and notifications.
Local Service Management System - SP network element receiving routing data downloads from NPAC SMS.
Numbering Plan Area (3-digit area code) and Central Office Code (3-digit exchange) - first 6 digits of a North American telephone number.
7-digit code representing a 1K Block (1000 telephone numbers) for number pooling allocation.
Efficient Data Representation - capability allowing LSMS to receive Block objects instead of individual SVs for pooled numbers.
Global Title Translation - SS7 signaling mechanism for routing calls to ported numbers via DPC and SSN values.
Destination Point Code - 9-digit SS7 signaling point identifier used in GTT routing for CLASS, LIDB, CNAM, ISVM, and WSMSC.
Common Management Information Protocol - OSI protocol for machine-to-machine communications between NPAC SMS and SP systems.
Permissive Dialing Period - time window during NPA split when both old and new area codes are accepted.
Service Provider Identifier - 4-character alphanumeric code uniquely identifying an NPAC Customer.
Service Provider assigned a 1K Number Pool Block by the Pooling Administrator, responsible for default routing of pooled numbers.
Service Provider owning the NPA-NXX code, responsible for vacant number treatment and default routing.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: High-level NPAC SMS provisioning process from SV creation through activation and broadcast.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    A["Service Providers Exchange Notifications"] --> B["NPAC SMS Receives Create SV Request"]
    B --> C{"Validate Data & Check NPA-NXX/LRN/SPID"}
    C -->|Valid| D["Create Pending Subscription Version"]
    C -->|Invalid| E["Return Error to Originator"]
    D --> F{"Concurrence from Both SPs?"}
    F -->|Yes| G["Await Due Date & Activation Request"]
    F -->|Timeout| H["Send Missing Notification Request"]
    H --> I{"Response Received?"}
    I -->|Old SP Missing| J["Allow New SP to Activate After T2"]
    I -->|New SP Missing| K["Cancel SV, Notify Both SPs"]
    G --> L["New SP Sends Activation"]
    L --> M{"Due Date Reached?"}
    M -->|Yes| N["Set Status to Sending"]
    M -->|No| O["Reject Activation"]
    N --> P["Broadcast to All Local SMSs"]
    P --> Q{"All LSMSs Acknowledge?"}
    Q -->|All Success| R["Set Status to Active"]
    Q -->|Some Fail| S["Set Status to Partial Failure"]
    Q -->|All Fail| T["Set Status to Failed"]
flowchartDiagram.caption: NPAC SMS Provisioning Service Process Flow

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: Inter-service provider port activation flow between New SP SOA, NPAC SMS, Old SP SOA, and LSMSs.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant NewSP as New Service Provider SOA
    participant NPAC as NPAC SMS
    participant OldSP as Old Service Provider SOA
    participant LSMS as Local SMS Systems
    NewSP->>NPAC: Create SV (TN, LRN, DPC/SSN, Due Date)
    NPAC->>NPAC: Validate Data, Create Pending SV
    NPAC->>OldSP: Notification - SV Pending
    Note over NPAC: Initial Concurrence Window Starts
    OldSP->>NPAC: Create SV (Authorization=True)
    NPAC->>NewSP: SV Pending (Concurred)
    NewSP->>NPAC: Activate SV
    NPAC->>NPAC: Set Status to Sending
    par Broadcast to all LSMSs
        NPAC->>LSMS: Create/Activate SV (Routing Data)
        LSMS-->>NPAC: Acknowledge Success/Failure
    end
    alt All LSMSs Success
        NPAC->>NPAC: Set Status to Active
    else Some LSMSs Fail
        NPAC->>NPAC: Set Status to Partial Failure
    end
    NPAC->>NewSP: Status Change Notification
    NPAC->>OldSP: Status Change Notification
sequenceDiagram.caption: NPAC SMS Inter-Service Provider Port Activation Sequence

erDiagram

introduction.purpose: This SRS describes the purpose and functionality of the Space Fractions software product requested by Ms. Andrea Brooks of Pecan Springs Elementary School. The document includes the details of the project's requirements, interface, design issues, and components. Space Fractions is a learning tool created to help improve fraction-solving skills for sixth-grade students through an interactive, web-based game built in Macromedia Flash.
introduction.documentConventions: The SRS follows a three-section structure: Section 1 provides a brief overview with definitions and references; Section 2 gives a general description from the user's perspective including functional and data requirements, constraints, and assumptions; Section 3 contains detailed requirements specifications targeted toward technical designers. Functional requirements use a consistent template with Purpose, Inputs, Processing, and Outputs sub-sections.
introduction.intendedAudience: This document is intended for both the client (Ms. Andrea Brooks, Pecan Springs Elementary School teacher) and the technical designers of the software. The definitions section assists technical designers and the client in clarifying terms used throughout. Section 2 serves the client's understanding of the product, while Section 3 is aimed at technical implementers. The project is part of the CS373 Software Engineering course at the University of Texas at Austin under Dr. Vicki Almstrum.
introduction.productScope: The Space Fractions project is a web-based, interactive game that helps sixth-grade students improve their fraction-solving skills through a storyline-driven experience. The product consists of Flash movies linked together to form a complete game with an introductory movie, main menu, a series of fraction questions forming a storyline, and an ending scene with score feedback. An additional Math Umbrella component provides a unified interface linking to past S2S (Student to Student) math-related projects. A Question Updater component allows administrators to customize game questions via a web interface. The product is not dependent on any other software and is not a component of another program.
references: Dr. Vicki L. Almstrum, professor at the University of Texas at Austin
Ms. Andrea Brooks, client and teacher at Pecan Springs Elementary
Class home page: http://www.cs.utexas.edu/users/almstrum/cs373/fa01/
Mr. Keith Henning, team mentor
Macromedia home page: http://www.macromedia.com
S2S projects done in previous semesters: http://www.cs.utexas.edu/users/s2s/admin/s2s-by-semester.html
Schach, Stephen R. Object-Oriented and Classical Software Engineering, Fifth Edition. McGraw-Hill, 2002

overallDescription.productPerspective: Space Fractions is a standalone web-based Flash application that requires only a web browser capable of running the Flash plug-in. It is not dependent on any other software and is not a component of another program. Since the product requires a Flash-supporting browser, the external interface depends on the browser configuration; various environments may yield different interfaces, but program behavior remains the same. The program does not require any new hardware. It resides on the S2S website and is accessible over the Internet.
productFunctions: Introductory movie providing background story and information to help users complete fraction questions, with option to skip.
Main menu with help section, game start option, and link to the team's web page.
Series of multiple-choice fraction questions testing arithmetic, equivalence, graphical interpretation, and improper vs. proper fraction skills, sequentially forming a storyline.
Dynamic and adaptive gameplay providing different storyline branches based on user performance at critical decision points.
Ending scene with score calculation, ranking, customized feedback message, and option to try again or quit.
Question Updater: web-accessible administrative tool for customizing game questions via pulldown menus and text fields.
Math Umbrella: unified web interface linking to mature S2S projects dealing with mathematics for sixth graders, organized by topic.

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: Sixth-grade female student learning fractions who does not like using computers. Has used computers for email and games but finds them boring. Prefers reading or talking with friends. Really interested in learning fractions and enjoys working with other students. Needs intuitive navigation and clear help sections.

Sixth-grade male student learning fractions who is very competitive. Enjoys competitive sports and using computers, especially games. Has used computers since age five. Likes games where he can excel. Only somewhat interested in fractions. Needs score feedback, fast gameplay, and competitive elements.

Sixth-grade teacher with computer skills. Enjoys teaching and finding innovative ways to teach students. Has been teaching sixth grade for six years. Finds students have particularly hard time learning fraction concepts. Uses the Question Updater to customize game content. Needs confidence students can navigate the interface independently.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The product runs on any Internet-accessible computer with a web browser that supports JavaScript and Macromedia Flash 5. The Flash plug-in is available for multiple platforms. No modifications to the host operating system or hardware are required. The game is hosted on a web server accessible via the Internet through the S2S website.
designAndImplementationConstraints: Must run in any web browser supporting JavaScript and Macromedia Flash 5.
All user input must be accomplished via mouse clicks only — no keyboard input required.
The product must be web-based with no permanent files on the user's computer.
Questions must be stored in a text file on the web server, editable through the administrative interface.
Score data is maintained as local data within the game session only.
userDocumentation: In-game help section accessible from the main menu providing basic instructions on gameplay.
Team website with additional project information.
assumptionsAndDependencies: Target users (sixth-grade students and their teacher) are assumed to have basic computer and Internet skills.
Users have access to a computer with Internet connectivity and a web browser supporting Flash.
The Macromedia Flash plug-in is available and installed on the user's browser.
The web server hosting the game remains accessible and operational.
Students understand basic fraction concepts that are being reinforced through the game.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The interface is graphically oriented and appealing, designed for sixth-grade students. No portion of the interface requires the keyboard; all input is accomplished via mouse clicks. The interface renders within the Flash player embedded in the user's web browser.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The software is developed in Macromedia Flash and HTML. These technologies require a minimum hardware configuration but no other hardware requirements are needed. There is no explicit interaction with the client's hardware.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The product requires a web browser capable of running the Flash plug-in with the system requirements specified by Macromedia under the 'Playback' section. Otherwise, the product has no software interaction dependencies.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The product communicates over standard HTTP protocol for web content delivery. The Question Updater component writes data files to the web server. No other network communication protocols are required.

systemFeatures.name: Introductory Movie
systemFeatures.description: A short animated movie that sets up the storyline of the game and provides background information to help the user complete the fraction questions.
stimulusResponseSequences: Upon entrance, the introductory movie begins playing automatically.
If the user clicks a mouse button during the movie, it terminates and forwards the user to the main menu.
If the movie plays to completion without interruption, the user is automatically moved to the main menu.
functionalRequirements: The system shall play the introductory movie upon user entrance to the game.
The system shall allow the user to skip the introductory movie via a mouse click.
The system shall forward the user to the main menu upon completion or skipping of the movie.
No input other than mouse clicks shall be recognized during the introductory movie.

systemFeatures.name: Main Menu
systemFeatures.description: A menu screen displaying a brief help section on playing the game, providing a link to start the main game and a link to the team's web page.
stimulusResponseSequences: The main menu is displayed after the introductory movie completes or is skipped.
The component waits until the user selects a button.
The user is forwarded to the game sequence or the team's web page depending on the button selected.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display a main menu with options to start the game, view help, and visit the team's website.
The system shall provide a general help screen accessible from the main menu with basic instructions on gameplay.
The system shall provide a short summary of the team and a link to the team's web page.
The system shall forward the user to the game sequence when the start button is clicked.

systemFeatures.name: Game Sequence
systemFeatures.description: A series of multiple-choice fraction questions that sequentially form a storyline related to the introduction. Questions test arithmetic, equivalence, graphical interpretation, and improper vs. proper fraction skills.
stimulusResponseSequences: The component displays a question and waits for the user to choose an answer via mouse click.
If the correct answer is selected, a success message is displayed and the component moves to the next question.
If an incorrect answer is selected, the user is informed and given another chance, but the question is not counted as correctly answered.
At critical points, different plot directions are chosen based on whether the critical question was answered correctly.
After proceeding through a set number of questions, the user is directed to the ending scene.
functionalRequirements: The system shall present fraction questions as multiple-choice selections with mouse-click input.
The system shall provide immediate feedback on whether the answer is correct or incorrect.
The system shall allow a second attempt on incorrectly answered questions without counting the question as correctly answered.
The system shall branch the storyline at designated critical points based on user performance.
The system shall track the user's score throughout the game sequence.
A friendly robotic sidekick shall assist with usability issues and give hints toward the correct response.
Questions shall be customizable by the game administrator via dynamically loaded question data files.

systemFeatures.name: Ending Scene
systemFeatures.description: A conclusion screen offering a storyline ending based on performance at critical game points, displaying the user's score with a customized message, and providing options to exit or return to the main menu.
stimulusResponseSequences: The ending scene displays after the final question in the game sequence.
The plot conclusion is determined by the user's responses to critical questions.
The user's overall score is displayed with a customized ranking message.
The component waits for the user to select either returning to the main menu or exiting the game.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display an ending scene that concludes the game's storyline based on critical question performance.
The system shall calculate and display the user's overall score with a customized feedback message.
The system shall provide an option to return to the main menu to play again.
The system shall provide an option to exit the game.

systemFeatures.name: Question Updater
systemFeatures.description: A web-accessible administrative tool that allows the game administrator to update the series of fraction questions used in the game sequence.
stimulusResponseSequences: The administrator navigates to the updater page and is prompted for a password.
Upon correct password submission, the administrator uses a web forms interface with pulldown menus and text fields to update questions.
Each question is updated on a separate page; the administrator clicks a button to progress between pages.
After submission, the component validates that inputted data is complete and contextually valid.
The component generates a text file on the game server used by the game sequence to load questions dynamically.
functionalRequirements: The system shall require password authentication before allowing access to the question updater.
The system shall provide a web forms interface with pulldown menus and text fields for updating questions.
The system shall validate that inputted data is complete and makes sense in the context of the question.
The system shall generate a text file on the game server containing the updated question data.
The game sequence shall dynamically load question data from the generated text file.

systemFeatures.name: Math Umbrella
systemFeatures.description: A web-based menu system providing links to mature S2S projects dealing with mathematics for sixth graders, organized by topic (e.g., fractions, decimals, percents).
stimulusResponseSequences: The component displays links to S2S math projects organized by topic.
The component waits for the user to click a link.
Upon click, the component opens the specified S2S product in a separate browser window.
functionalRequirements: The system shall display links to S2S mathematics projects organized by topic area.
The system shall open selected S2S products in a separate browser window.
The system shall provide a format accessible over the World Wide Web.

performanceRequirements: Only one person can use a single instance of the product at a time.
Multiple users can concurrently access the product over the Internet and download content for use on their computers.
The introductory movie (~200KB) and main menu movie (~100KB) can be downloaded in approximately one minute with a modem connection.
The main game movie (1-2MB) can begin playing within a few minutes on a regular modem connection since Flash movies do not need to be fully downloaded to play (streaming playback).
safetyRequirements: The product is designed for sixth-grade students and must contain only age-appropriate content.
No personal student data is collected or stored beyond the in-session game score.
securityRequirements: The product will be as secure as the web browser that runs it.
The Question Updater component requires password authentication to prevent unauthorized modification of game content.
softwareQualityAttributes: Availability: The product will be available over the Internet via the S2S website.
Reliability: Ensured by extensive testing by team members and mentors.
Maintainability: Primary goal for the project. Appropriate use of sub-scenes in the main Flash game to split up code allows for easy alteration at a later date.
Usability: Interface is graphically oriented and appealing for sixth-grade users with mouse-only input.
businessRules: All user interaction must be via mouse clicks only — no keyboard input.
The game must be web-based with no permanent files stored on the user's computer.
Question data must be customizable by the game administrator without modifying the game code.
The storyline must branch at critical points based on user performance to provide varied experiences.

otherRequirements: There are no additional requirements at this time.

glossary.definition: Hypertext Markup Language: the formatting language used to describe web pages.
A type of computer made by Apple Computers.
Kilobytes: a measure of the amount of space available on a computer.
An application for creating dynamic graphics and sound, generally intended to produce artifacts for display over the World Wide Web.
Megabytes: a measure of space. One MB equals 1,024KB.
A downloadable component for a web browser that provides additional functionality.
Student to Student: the designation representing the collective efforts of several hundred University of Texas Computer Science students taking Software Engineering with Dr. Vicki Almstrum.
Software Requirements Specification: this document.
A software product that consists of several independent programs unified under a single interface.
Able to be run over the web, without any permanent files on the user's computer.

introduction.purpose: This document defines the software requirements for the Swift X-Ray Telescope (XRT) Control Processor (XCP) Flight Software (FSW). It is a Level 4 specification as defined in GSFC-410.4-SPEC-0004, Swift Missions Requirements Document. The XRT is a sensitive, autonomous X-ray CCD imaging spectrometer designed to measure the flux, spectrum, and light curve of Gamma-Ray Bursts (GRBs) and afterglows over a wide dynamic range covering more than seven orders of magnitude in flux. It refines BAT positions (~1-4 arcmin uncertainty) to 2.5 arcseconds within 5 seconds of target acquisition.
introduction.documentConventions: Where final numerical values or specification references are not available, best estimates are given and noted TBR (To Be Reviewed). Items not yet defined are noted TBD (To Be Determined). Requirements use the keyword 'shall' for mandatory functionality. The document follows NASA GSFC documentation standards with Requirements Flow-Down traceability. Detailed requirements are enumerated in an electronic spreadsheet in Appendix A to facilitate requirements traceability and verification tracking.
introduction.intendedAudience: Software developers at Southwest Research Institute (SwRI) Automation and Data Systems Division and Penn State University (PSU) Department of Astronomy and Astrophysics. Also for use by NASA Goddard Space Flight Center (GSFC) mission managers, SwRI Software Quality Assurance, system integrators, and ground systems operators at the Science Mission Operations Center (SMOC).
introduction.productScope: The XCP Flight Software executes on the RAD6000 microprocessor within the XRT Electronics Package (XEP), controlling the X-ray CCD camera for the Swift Gamma-Ray Burst Explorer satellite. The FSW processes science data from the camera in multiple acquisition modes (Image, Photo-Diode, Windowed Timing, Photon Counting, Null, Raw Data, Bias), relays data to the Spacecraft Control Unit (SCU) as CCSDS Source Packets, receives and processes telecommands, manages housekeeping telemetry collection and transmission, controls telescope tube heaters (36 groups) and mirror baffle heaters (3 groups), reads the Telescope Alignment Monitor, synchronizes the spacecraft clock, and manages software state transitions across six defined states (Off, Boot, Init, Auto, Manual, Red). The software comprises 26 Computer Software Components (CSCs) split between SwRI system/framework software and PSU science software. Scope excludes ground software (GSW) detailed design.
references: 04121-XCPSDP-01, Software Development Plan for the XRT Control Processor, Rev 2 Chg 0, December 2000
GSFC-410.4-SPEC-0004, Swift Mission Requirements Document
GSFC-410.4-SPEC-0005 (GSFC-661-SWIFT-SRD), Swift Science Requirements Document, Version 1.0, August 21, 2000
GSFC-730-SWIFT-IRD, Swift Interface Requirements Document, Version 1.2, April 6, 2000
1143-EI-S19121, Swift 1553 Bus Protocol Interface Control Document, Rev -, August 25, 2000
XRT-PSU-015, XRT Requirements Document, Version 2.4, November 28, 2000
XRT-PSU-021, XRT Software Requirements Document, Version 1.2, February 10, 2001
XRT-PSU-028, XRT Data Formats, Version 1.1, November 21, 2000
LMFS-RSC-WB, RSC VME Engineering Workbook, September 10, 1996
MIL-STD-1553B, September 21, 1978 with Notices 1 & 2
9000-0013, NASA Phase A Study Report in Response to AO-98-OSS-03, June 1999

overallDescription.productPerspective: The Swift observatory is a NASA medium-class explorer (MIDEX) satellite — the first-of-its-kind observatory for multi-wavelength transient astronomy. Swift employs three semi-autonomous science instruments: BAT (wide-angle burst detection), XRT (X-ray CCD imaging spectrometer), and UVOT (ultraviolet/optical telescope). On GRB detection, the spacecraft slews to bring the burst into XRT/UVOT view. The XCP FSW executes on the LMFS RAD6000 (radiation-hardened PowerPC) within a VME enclosure. It interfaces externally with the SCU via dual-redundant MIL-STD-1553B serial bus and 1PPS synchronization signal, and internally with the Camera Head CCD, Telescope Alignment Monitor via RS-422, housekeeping sensors via analog I/O, telescope tube heaters via solid-state relays, and EEPROM for non-volatile storage. The software reuses components from SSFF (Space Station Furnace Facility), IMAGE (CIDP), and CUBIC projects.
productFunctions: Process science data from the CCD camera and relay it to the SCU in CCSDS Source Packets
Receive commands from the SCU that establish the current instrument state and camera mode
Transmit detailed housekeeping telemetry to the SCU in CCSDS Source Packets
Receive time messages from the SCU and synchronize the XCP local copy of the spacecraft clock
Control 36 telescope tube heater groups with on-off hysteresis controllers
Control 3 mirror baffle heater groups with PID control algorithms
Read the Telescope Alignment Monitor (TAM) via RS-422 and compute position corrections using centroid algorithm
Autonomously determine observing mode (Image, Photo-Diode, Windowed Timing, Photon Counting) based on source count rate
Perform source centroiding to refine GRB position to 2.5 arcsec accuracy within 5 seconds
Send XRT Position messages to UVOT and ground via TDRSS for immediate spectroscopic observations
Compress science data products for telemetry
Manage EEPROM file system for dynamic software loading, patching, and configuration management
Perform built-in tests (BIT) at startup and memory error detection/correction (EDAC) continuously

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: Ground system operators at the Science Mission Operations Center who send telecommands and monitor XRT telemetry. Use ITOS (Integrated Test and Operations System) for command and display. Interact via Malindi ground contacts (~7 per day, 7-10 minutes each) and TDRSS real-time link.

Penn State University astronomers who define observation strategies, analyze science data products (X-ray images, spectra, light curves, photon counts), and specify science flight software requirements for the 5 science CSCs.

Southwest Research Institute engineers who develop and maintain the 21 system/framework CSCs, perform integration and testing, manage software updates via EEPROM patches, and support anomaly resolution.
Engineers who use Ground Support Equipment (GSE) applications communicating with XCP over unused RS-232 port to exercise hardware interfaces via flight software drivers.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: The XCP FSW executes on a radiation-hardened RAD6000 microprocessor (PowerPC architecture) within a VME enclosure in orbit aboard the Swift observatory at ~600km LEO altitude with 96-minute orbital period. The operating system is VxWorks 5.3 (WIND 2.4 kernel) — a COTS real-time multi-tasking OS. DRAM with EDAC provides program and data memory. EEPROM stores the bootstrap, flight program configurations (primary and alternate), and an MS-DOS compatible file system. The CCD is cooled by a thermo-electric cooler to operating temperature. The spacecraft provides dual 28VDC power buses (Operational Power Bus and Survival Power Bus). Communication is via dual-redundant MIL-STD-1553B data bus with CCSDS Source Packet formatting.
designAndImplementationConstraints: C1: TDRSS downlink bandwidth limited to 1 kbps for the S/C — limits housekeeping telemetry rate.
C2: Malindi ground contacts limited to ~7 per day, 7-10 minutes each (96-minute orbit) — FSW must avoid time-consuming setup or configuration during nominal operation.
C3: Real-time HK packets limited to 230 bytes per S/C RT HK frame constraint.
C4: S/C does not reassemble segmented packets and ITOS ground system is not currently capable of reassembling segmented packets.
C5: ITOS ground system cannot decompress packets.
The RAD6000 processor board supplies PROM, DRAM with EDAC, and EEPROM.
VxWorks 5.3 RTOS is COTS — supplemented with system utilities for memory management and VME bus access.
CCD operates in frame-transfer mode with 600x600 pixel image section, 600x600 store section, and split readout registers.
MIL-STD-1553B bus interface is dual-redundant with Remote Terminal (RT) protocol.
Watchdog timer provides hardware reset capability; failure to strobe triggers reset.
Primary FSW configuration in EEPROM is software-locked read-only; alternate provides backup.
userDocumentation: NPAC SMS Interoperable Interface Specification — defines hardware interfaces and data protocols.
XRT Data Formats (XRT-PSU-028) — describes science and housekeeping telemetry formats.
Appendix A: Detailed Software Requirements spreadsheet.
Appendix B: EEPROM Memory Maps.
Appendix C: Telecommands reference.
Appendix D: CPU Throughput Calculation.
assumptionsAndDependencies: Software reuses significant components from SSFF (MIL-STD-1553 and Ethernet drivers), IMAGE CIDP, and CUBIC projects.
Bootstrap monitor reused from SSFF/IMAGE with minor adjustments for XCP hardware addresses.
XEP hardware design is a mix of heritage/existing designs from IMAGE, UVOT, LMFS, and new designs from SwRI and PSU.
VxWorks 5.3 RTOS loader is available for dynamic software loading from EEPROM file system.
CCD clock sequencer (AD21020 DSP) supports up to 64 programmable waveform patterns.
Two signal chain boards are dual-redundant for CCD analog video processing.
TEC power supply has variable voltage output controlled by digital potentiometer with 100 wiper tap points.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: Ground operators interact with the XRT via telecommands sent through the ITOS ground system at SMOC. Commands are relayed via TDRSS or Malindi ground station contacts. Engineering Ethernet interface available on Engineering Units (EU) only — not present on Flight Units.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The XCP interfaces with: (1) XRT Communications Module (XCM) containing the MIL-STD-1553B dual-redundant bus interface, 64KW shared memory FIFO buffer, Real-Time Clock, dual UART, CCD data FIFO buffer, and analog MUX/ADC. (2) Camera Head with CCD (600x600 pixel image/store sections) and dual preamplifiers connected by 1-meter cable. (3) Sequencer board with AD21020 DSP generating CCD clock waveforms. (4) Power Distribution Module with solid-state relays for heaters, TAM power, camera door/pressure relief HOP actuators. (5) Housekeeping board reading voltages, temperatures, and pressures (~115 sensor channels). (6) Timer/Sequencer Module (TSM). (7) EEPROM for non-volatile configuration and file storage.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The FSW uses VxWorks 5.3 (WIND 2.4 kernel) RTOS with VME bus access utilities and dynamic module loader. It communicates with the SCU using the Swift 1553 Bus Protocol (document 1143-EI-S19121) with CCSDS Source Packet formatting. The EEPROM File System (EEFS) is MS-DOS media-compatible. Ground Support Equipment interfaces via RS-232 serial port (EU only).
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: Dual-redundant MIL-STD-1553B serial bus at standard rate connecting XRT, BAT, UVOT, two Star Trackers, and the S/C on a common bus. RS-422 serial interface to the Telescope Alignment Monitor (TAM). 1PPS (One Pulse Per Second) hardwired signal from the SCU for clock synchronization, paired with At-The-Tone-The-Time-Will-Be messages over 1553B. Science data downlinked via S/C Solid State Recorder (SSR) through TDRSS (1 kbps real-time) or Malindi ground contacts.

systemFeatures.name: Command and Control (XCP-CCM)
systemFeatures.description: Central state machine managing XRT system states (Off, Boot, Init, Auto, Manual, Red) and command dispatch. Receives telecommands from SCU via XCP-SCUI, validates commands against current state permissions, dispatches to appropriate CSCs, and manages state transitions. The Red state provides direct hardware-level commanding for anomaly recovery.
stimulusResponseSequences: Power Up -> Boot (execute BIT Stage 1, load flight program from EEPROM) -> Init (check CCD voltages, BIT Stage 2) -> Manual or Auto.
Auto mode receives SISCATTITUDE with IS_SETTLED=False -> Begins pre-observation activities (bias rows, bias map, raw image) -> Settles -> Centroid -> Begin acquisition mode selection based on flux.
CCD overtemperature or CCD bias voltage error detected -> Autonomous transition to Red state.
Watchdog timer expiry -> Hardware reset -> Re-enter Boot state.
functionalRequirements: FSW shall maintain six operational states: Off, Boot, Init, Auto, Manual, Red.
FSW shall accept telecommands only in commandable states (Init, Auto, Manual, Red).
FSW shall autonomously transition from Boot to Init upon successful BIT Stage 1 completion.
FSW shall transition to Red on CCD overtemperature or CCD bias voltage error.
Red state shall revert to Manual after execution of any command or Red command timeout.
Commands not valid in Red state shall be rejected with transition to Manual.

systemFeatures.name: Autonomous Observation Sequencing (XCP-DCC)
systemFeatures.description: In Auto mode, the Data Collection Control CSC autonomously selects the optimal CCD acquisition mode based on real-time source count rate across four flux ranges with seven distinct data acquisition modes. Manages the complete observation lifecycle from slew preparation through data collection to target change.
stimulusResponseSequences: Slew starts (IS_SETTLED=FALSE, >10 arcmin) -> Calculate row bias map -> Calculate image bias map -> Collect raw data image (if flag set).
IS_SETTLED=TRUE -> Acquire image frame, detect source -> If source found: centroid -> Send XRT Position to TDRSS & UVOT within 5 seconds.

Count rate > 8500 cps -> Image Mode. Rate 40-8500 cps -> Photo-Diode Mode (fast timing, 0.5 msec). Rate 2-40 cps -> Windowed Timing Mode (5 msec). Rate < 2 cps -> Photon Counting Mode (~2.5 sec update).
TARGET_ID changes or IS_SETTLED=FALSE -> End observation.
functionalRequirements: FSW shall support Automatic, Preplanned, and Target of Opportunity observation types.
FSW shall autonomously select CCD mode based on source flux rate thresholds.
FSW shall perform bias row and bias image map recalculations during pre-observation slew when distance > 10 arcmin.
FSW shall centroid on detected source and transmit XRT Position Message to UVOT and ground via TDRSS.
FSW shall send XRT Centroid Error message if source detection threshold or timeout is exceeded.
FSW shall send Postage Stamp Report to ground via TDRSS within 1200 seconds of burst alert.

systemFeatures.name: CCD Interface and Data Acquisition (XCP-CCD, XCP-CDD, XCP-SEQ)
systemFeatures.description: Manages CCD hardware interface including ring buffer creation in XCP local memory, block transfer of CCD row data, sequencer program loading from EEPROM, and DAC voltage configuration. Supports 64 programmable waveform patterns for varying acquisition modes.
stimulusResponseSequences: DCC sets mode -> SEQ loads appropriate sequencer program from EEFS onto TSM -> Configures DAC voltages via ANIO -> CCD starts clocking.
CCD generates frame -> Row data transferred to CDD FIFO -> CCD CSC block-transfers to ring buffer -> Data available for ERP processing.
functionalRequirements: FSW shall support up to 64 CCD waveform patterns loaded from EEFS.
FSW shall create and manage a ring buffer in XCP local memory for CCD row data.
FSW shall verify DAC setup before beginning each data acquisition mode.
CCD purge function shall be available to clear accumulated charge.

systemFeatures.name: Event Recognition and Science Processing (XCP-ERP)
systemFeatures.description: Processes raw CCD data to generate science data products appropriate to the current observing mode: images, light curves, photon event lists, and spectra. Contains the event recognition algorithm, centroid algorithm, bad pixel/row/column routines, bias algorithms, baseline correction, and mean row correction.
stimulusResponseSequences: Image Mode frames -> Detect pixels above threshold -> Accumulate cosmic ray filtering -> Centroid calculation -> XRT Position + Postage Stamp Image reports.
Photo-Diode Mode -> CCD reports single value per 16 µsec (~60,000 pixels/sec) -> Fast Timing Frame reports.
Windowed Timing Mode -> CCD reports one row per 5 msec -> Normal Timing Frame reports.
Photon Counting Mode -> Full image collected, scanned for lit pixels (~2.5 sec update) -> Event List reports.
functionalRequirements: FSW shall implement event recognition algorithm distinguishing X-ray events from noise and cosmic rays.
FSW shall compute source centroid with accuracy better than CCD pixel size using centroiding algorithm.
FSW shall validate PSF standard deviation and report centroiding errors for source confusion.
FSW shall support at least three selectable bias algorithms, with ground-command selection.
FSW shall generate Postage Stamp, Fast Timing, Normal Timing, Event List, Spectrum, and Raw Frame report types.

systemFeatures.name: Heater Control (XCP-THC, XCP-BHC)
systemFeatures.description: Manages telescope thermal control via 36 tube heater groups (on-off with hysteresis) and 3 baffle heater groups (PID control with solid-state relays for high cycle counts). Heater set points and hysteresis controlled by telecommands.
stimulusResponseSequences: Telecommand sets temperature setpoint and hysteresis for tube heater group -> THC activates solid-state relay when temperature drops below setpoint - hysteresis -> Deactivates when temperature exceeds setpoint + hysteresis.
Telecommand sets baffle heater setpoint -> BHC runs PID algorithm to drive solid-state relay for proportional temperature regulation.
functionalRequirements: FSW shall control 36 telescope tube heater groups with on-off hysteresis control.
FSW shall control 3 baffle heater groups using PID control algorithms via solid-state relays.
Heater temperature setpoints and hysteresis shall be configurable via telecommand.
Tube heaters shall use SPB-powered survival heaters controlled by the spacecraft bus independently.

systemFeatures.name: Thermo-Electric Cooler Control (XCP-TEC)
systemFeatures.description: Closed-loop temperature control of the CCD via thermo-electric cooler using a PID algorithm. Temperature setpoint, ramp rate, and mode (open/closed loop) are controlled by telecommand. Digital potentiometer with 100 tap points sets the TEC voltage.
stimulusResponseSequences: Ground command sets TEC temperature setpoint and ramp rate -> XCP-TEC ramps to setpoint using PID algorithm -> Monitors 4 TEC sensors (voltage, current, temperature) and reports in housekeeping.
functionalRequirements: FSW shall control CCD temperature via PID closed-loop TEC control.
FSW shall support open and closed loop TEC operating modes selectable by telecommand.
TEC temperature setpoint and ramp rate shall be configurable via telecommand.
TEC digital potentiometer shall be controlled via up/down and clock digital signals.

systemFeatures.name: SCU Communications and Telemetry (XCP-SCUI, XCP-1553, XCP-TIS)
systemFeatures.description: Manages all communication with the Spacecraft Control Unit via dual-redundant MIL-STD-1553B bus. Handles command reception, telemetry transmission (CCSDS Source Packets), time synchronization with 1PPS signal, and heartbeat keep-alive messaging.
stimulusResponseSequences: SCU sends At-The-Tone message via 1553B + 1PPS hardwire signal -> XCP-TIS synchronizes local RTC to spacecraft clock, computes UTC time.
XCP-PP collects housekeeping data -> XCP-CCM formats into HK CCSDS packet (<= 230 bytes) -> XCP-SCUI transmits to SCU via 1553B.
XCP-SCUI sends heartbeat to SCU indicating XRT aliveness. If XRT fails to communicate, S/C turns off OPB (hardware reset).
functionalRequirements: FSW shall communicate with SCU via dual-redundant MIL-STD-1553B as Remote Terminal.
FSW shall format all telemetry as CCSDS Source Packets.
Real-time HK packets shall not exceed 230 bytes per S/C RT HK frame.
FSW shall synchronize local clock with SCU using 1PPS and At-The-Tone messages.
FSW shall transmit heartbeat keep-alive messages to SCU.

systemFeatures.name: Error Detection, Correction, and Recovery (XCP-EDAC, XCP-BIT, XCP-BOOT)
systemFeatures.description: Multi-layer error handling: DRAM EDAC detects/corrects single-bit errors and detects multi-bit errors; Memory Scrubber proactively repairs single-bit errors; Built-In Tests verify hardware at startup (Stages 1 & 2); Bootstrap manages primary/alternate FSW loading with automatic fallback; watchdog timer provides ultimate hardware reset.
stimulusResponseSequences: Power up -> Bootstrap loads from EEPROM -> Performs BIT Stage 1 -> Loads primary FSW config -> Starts flight program. If primary fails -> Loads alternate FSW configuration.
Memory read triggers EDAC check -> Single-bit error: log, correct, increment counter. Multi-bit error: discontinue watchdog strobe -> Watchdog timer expires -> Hardware reset.
Memory Scrubber runs at lowest priority -> Steps through DRAM reading each location -> Rewrites locations with single-bit errors to prevent degradation to multi-bit.
functionalRequirements: FSW shall perform BIT Stage 1 during bootstrap and BIT Stage 2 during Init.
FSW shall detect and correct single-bit DRAM errors via EDAC, logging address and incrementing error count.
FSW shall detect multi-bit DRAM errors and initiate watchdog reset.
Memory Scrubber shall run as lowest-priority background task, reading and rewriting all DRAM locations.
Bootstrap shall automatically load alternate FSW configuration if primary fails to boot.
Primary FSW configuration in EEPROM shall be software write-locked prior to flight.
Detectable exceptions causing watchdog reboot shall be recorded to EEPROM.

systemFeatures.name: Housekeeping and Periodic Processing (XCP-PP, XCP-ANIO)
systemFeatures.description: Periodic collection and formatting of ~115 housekeeping sensor channels including CCD bias/clock voltages, board temperatures, mirror temperatures, telescope tube temperatures, contamination sensors, TEC sensors, and miscellaneous sensors. Also monitors running tasks and handles overall error management.
stimulusResponseSequences: Periodic timer fires -> XCP-PP commands XCP-ANIO to read analog MUX channels -> ADC converts -> HK data aggregated from all CSCs -> Formatted into CCSDS HK packets -> Sent to XCP-SCUI for transmission.
functionalRequirements: FSW shall periodically collect housekeeping from all sensor channels: CCD bias voltages (10), CCD clock voltages (16), miscellaneous voltages (7), board temperatures (10), mirror temperatures (10), tube temperatures (40), contamination sensors (4), baffle temperatures (3), TEC sensors (4), miscellaneous temperatures (5), miscellaneous sensors (6).
FSW shall monitor all running tasks for heartbeat and error conditions.
HK data shall be formatted as CCSDS Source Packets and transmitted via 1553B.

systemFeatures.name: Software Maintenance and Configuration (XCP-EEFS, XCP-EEPRM)
systemFeatures.description: EEPROM-based non-volatile storage with MS-DOS compatible file system supporting dynamic software loading, patching, and complete software reload capability for on-orbit corrections. Maintains primary (read-only locked) and alternate FSW configurations.
stimulusResponseSequences: Ground uploads software patch as object file -> Stored in EEFS -> Dynamic loading and linking into active FSW via VxWorks loader.
Complete software rebuild uploaded to alternate FSW location -> XCP commanded to boot alternate configuration.
functionalRequirements: FSW shall provide MS-DOS compatible EEPROM file system for software storage.
FSW shall support dynamic loading and linking of software patches from EEFS.
FSW shall support complete software reload to alternate EEPROM configuration.
Primary FSW configuration shall be software write-locked prior to flight.

performanceRequirements: Photo-Diode Mode: CCD reports one pixel every 16 µsec (~60,000 pixels/sec).
Windowed Timing Mode: CCD reports one row every 5 msec.
Photon Counting Mode: full image scan approximately every 2.5 seconds.
Source centroiding must refine position to 2.5 arcsec within 5 seconds of target acquisition.
XRT Position and Postage Stamp Report must be transmitted via TDRSS within 1200 seconds of burst alert.
Maximum science data rate: ~10MB per 24-hour continuous observation (average 1 kbps), well within allocated 3.9 kbps.
Typical 30-minute observation generates ~2.7MB (average ~12 kbps).
CPU throughput must accommodate all CSC processing (see Appendix D calculations).
safetyRequirements: CCD overtemperature or CCD bias voltage error causes autonomous transition to Red state.
Watchdog timer provides ultimate hardware reset against software hangs.
Multi-bit DRAM errors trigger immediate processor reset via watchdog.
Camera door cannot be closed once opened — software must prevent inadvertent opening.
Sun Shutter has automatic photosensor control with telecommand override.
S/C can turn off OPB in time-critical emergency regardless of XRT state.
Primary FSW configuration write-locked before flight to prevent corruption.
securityRequirements: Primary FSW EEPROM configuration is software write-locked.
Bootstrap EEPROM section includes checksum verified during BIT.
Commands validated against current state permissions before execution.
RED state restricted commands prevent unauthorized hardware-level access during normal operation.
softwareQualityAttributes: Simplicity (G1): Simple, consistent data flow interfaces with external systems.
Reusability (G3, G6): Modular, project-generic designs reusing SSFF, IMAGE, and CUBIC components.
Maintainability (G4): Design provides for on-orbit upgrade and maintenance via EEPROM patching.
Testability (G5): Flexible design supporting ground I&T and off-nominal flight configurations.
Reliability (G7, G9): Error-free code goal with reasonably fault-tolerant design.
Integration efficiency (G8): Minimize re-work at each integration level.
businessRules: SwRI develops system and framework CSCs (21 CSCs); PSU develops science CSCs (5 CSCs).
Engineering Units include Ethernet board for development; Flight Units do not.
ITOS ground system limitations constrain packet segmentation and compression choices.
Software developed following SwRI SED Standard Software Process (SED-SSP).

otherRequirements: Observation buffer memory allocation should exceed 10MB to meet allocated 3.9 kbps average science telemetry rate.
Ground system simulators must use same external interfaces as SMOC for reusability of displays and data processing.
MIL-STD-1553B driver, RS-422 driver, and Ethernet driver are reused from SSFF project.
Bootstrap monitor reused from IMAGE CIDP with minimal modifications.

glossary.definition: X-Ray Telescope — sensitive, autonomous X-ray CCD imaging spectrometer on the Swift observatory.
XRT Control Processor — RAD6000-based flight computer executing the FSW within the XEP.
Flight Software — the complete software package executing on the XCP including RTOS, system framework, and science applications.
Gamma-Ray Burst — intense flashes of gamma radiation from distant astrophysical sources that Swift is designed to detect and study.
Charge-Coupled Device — the X-ray sensitive imaging detector (600x600 pixel image and store sections) in the camera head.
Spacecraft Control Unit — the main spacecraft computer that commands instruments and manages telemetry via 1553B bus.
Burst Alert Telescope — wide-angle X-ray telescope on Swift that initially detects GRBs.
Consultative Committee for Space Data Systems — the standard format for science and housekeeping telemetry packets.
Radiation-hardened microprocessor (PowerPC architecture) from Lockheed Martin Federal Systems used in the XCP.
Error Detection and Correction — hardware memory protection facility that detects/corrects single-bit and detects double-bit errors in DRAM.
Computer Software Component — discrete functional module of the flight software (26 total).
Tracking and Data Relay Satellite System — NASA satellite relay for real-time telemetry downlink at 1 kbps.
MIL-STD-1553B — dual-redundant serial data bus standard used for spacecraft-to-instrument communication.
Thermo-Electric Cooler — solid-state cooling device maintaining CCD at operating temperature via PID control.
Telescope Alignment Monitor — optical device measuring mechanical alignment changes in the XRT telescope tube via reflected point source imaging.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This flowchart depicts the autonomous observation mode selection based on source flux count rate after spacecraft settling.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    A["Spacecraft Settled on Target"] --> B["Acquire Image Frame"]
    B --> C{"Source Detected?"}
    C -->|No| D["Accumulate Frames, Filter Cosmic Rays"]
    D --> E{"Accumulation > 30s?"}
    E -->|No| B
    E -->|Yes| F["Send Centroid Error\nNo Source Found"]
    C -->|Yes| G["Centroid on Source"]
    G --> H{"PSF Standard Deviation OK?"}
    H -->|No| I["Send Centroid Error\nSource Confusion"]
    H -->|Yes| J["Send XRT Position\nto TDRSS & UVOT"]
    J --> K{"Determine Count Rate"}
    K -->|"> 8500 cps"| L["Image Mode"]
    K -->|"40-8500 cps"| M["Photo-Diode Mode\n0.5 msec timing"]
    K -->|"2-40 cps"| N["Windowed Timing Mode\n5 msec timing"]
    K -->|"< 2 cps"| O["Photon Counting Mode\n~2.5 sec update"]
flowchartDiagram.caption: Swift XRT Autonomous Observation Mode Selection — showing the flux-based decision tree from source detection through centroiding to data acquisition mode determination.

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This sequence diagram shows the observation lifecycle from GRB detection to position report transmission.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant BAT as BAT Telescope
    participant SC as Spacecraft/SCU
    participant XCP as XRT Control Processor
    participant CCD as CCD Camera
    participant GND as Ground/TDRSS
    BAT->>SC: GRB Detected (position ~1-4 arcmin)
    SC->>SC: Begin Slew to GRB
    SC->>XCP: SISCATTITUDE (IS_SETTLED=FALSE)
    XCP->>CCD: Calculate Row Bias Map
    XCP->>CCD: Calculate Image Bias Map
    SC->>XCP: SISCATTITUDE (IS_SETTLED=TRUE)
    XCP->>CCD: Acquire Image Frame (low gain, 0.1s)
    CCD-->>XCP: Frame Data
    XCP->>XCP: Detect Source, Count Pixels
    XCP->>XCP: Centroid Calculation
    XCP->>GND: XRT Position Message (2.5 arcsec)
    XCP->>SC: XRT Position to UVOT
    Note over XCP: Within 5 seconds of settling
    XCP->>XCP: Determine Count Rate
    alt High flux > 8500 cps
        XCP->>CCD: Image Mode
    else Medium 40-8500 cps
        XCP->>CCD: Photo-Diode Mode
    else Low < 2 cps
        XCP->>CCD: Photon Counting Mode
    end
    XCP->>SC: Science Reports (CCSDS Packets)
    SC->>GND: Downlink via SSR
sequenceDiagram.caption: Swift XRT Observation Lifecycle — from BAT burst detection through spacecraft slew, source centroiding, and autonomous mode selection to science data downlink.

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This diagram shows the major software components (CSCs) and their data flow relationships.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    SCU_INTERFACE ||--|{ COMMAND_CONTROL : commands
    COMMAND_CONTROL ||--|{ DATA_COLLECTION : configures
    COMMAND_CONTROL ||--|{ TUBE_HEATER : commands
    COMMAND_CONTROL ||--|{ BAFFLE_HEATER : commands
    COMMAND_CONTROL ||--|{ TEC_CONTROL : commands
    COMMAND_CONTROL ||--|{ TAM_MONITOR : commands
    DATA_COLLECTION ||--|{ SEQUENCER : programs
    DATA_COLLECTION ||--|{ CCD_INTERFACE : controls
    CCD_INTERFACE ||--|{ EVENT_RECOGNITION : provides_data
    EVENT_RECOGNITION ||--|{ DATA_COMPRESSION : reports
    DATA_COMPRESSION ||--|{ SCU_INTERFACE : transmits
    PERIODIC_PROCESSING ||--|{ SCU_INTERFACE : housekeeping
    BOOTSTRAP ||--|{ EEPROM_FILESYSTEM : loads
    EDAC ||--|{ PERIODIC_PROCESSING : errors
entityRelationshipDiagram.caption: XRT Flight Software CSC Data Flow Architecture — showing command, telemetry, and control relationships among major software components.
