introduction.purpose: This document provides a complete and accurate list of requirements for the EVLA Correlator Backend System. Upon completion, the document acts as a binding contract between developers and users and provides a common point of reference for system expectations. The Correlator Backend System lies between the Correlator and the End-to-End (e2e) archiving System, serving as the primary component of the real-time astronomical data processing pipeline of the Expanded Very Large Array (EVLA).
introduction.documentConventions: Requirements are numbered based on the section/subsection in which they appear. The format follows IEEE STD 830, Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications. Text between '<' and '>' indicates questions or comments. The phrase 'The user' can generally be replaced with 'An authorized user'. Requirements use 'shall' for mandatory items.
introduction.intendedAudience: Project leaders, designers and developers of the system, and end users. Also of interest to EVLA project scientists and engineers or as a reference for individuals involved in similar projects with similar requirements.
introduction.productScope: The Correlator Backend System's primary responsibility is to perform basic data assembly, formatting, and processing services and to support real-time inspection of the astronomical data stream. Major functions: receive lag data from the Correlator in real-time; assemble time-series from correlator lag output; perform Fourier Transforms; perform additional user-selected processing; deliver formatted results to the End-to-End System as AIPS++ Measurement Sets.
references: ANSI/IEEE Std 830-1998, IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications
ANSI/IEEE Std 1233-1996, IEEE Guide for Developing System Requirements Specifications
EVLA Memo No. 15, Scientific Requirements for the EVLA Real-Time System
EVLA Project Book
EVLA System Requirements (SyRS)
EVLA Architecture and Design
The Very Large Array Observing Log, J. Nieri, February 1994
NRC-EVLA Memo# 014, Refined EVLA WIDAR Correlator Architecture, Brent Carlson, Oct. 2, 2001
NRC-EVLA Memo# 015, EVLA Correlator Monitor and Control System, Backend Software Requirements and Design Concepts, Brent Carlson, Jan. 23, 2002

overallDescription.productPerspective: The EVLA Correlator Backend System is a real-time data processing system implemented on a distributed memory cluster of identical connected processors. It sits between the WIDAR Correlator (input) and the End-to-End archiving System (output). Data input from the Correlator and output to the e2e are over very high-speed networks. Internal processors communicate with one another and the Monitor and Control (M&C) System over a network. Backend Management functions run on one cluster processor with shadow processors on standby; remaining processors run Data Processing functions.
productFunctions: Receive lag frame data from the Correlator Baseline Boards via high-speed network in real-time
Assemble lag frames into complete, properly ordered time series (lag sets) up to 262,144 values
Apply normalization based on data invalid counts
Apply coarse quantization correction (VanVleck correction) based on state count and quantizer power data
Perform time stamp adjustments as required by observational mode and recirculation
Apply pre-FFT windowing operations for narrow band RFI mitigation
Perform power-of-two complex-to-complex Fast Fourier Transform on time series
Apply post-FFT windowing as convolution
Apply user-selected chainable time-domain and frequency-domain processes
Perform spectral integration (summation) controlled by observational mode parameters
Format results as AIPS++ Measurement Sets with all required metadata
Deliver formatted results to the e2e System with verified successful transfer
Self-monitoring of processes, processors, networks, I/O performance, and compute performance
Automatic error detection, reporting, and recovery including processor/process/network failures
Redistribute workload among processors (offloading) including Correlator destination address changes

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: Primary contact with array operations via status and error messages channeled through the Monitor and Control System. All interaction is indirect via M&C.

Responsible for corrective and preventive maintenance, periodic performance tests, and upgrades. Vitally important for maintaining real-time processing. Need tools to inspect individual devices from remote locations.
Primarily interested in the science output. Main interaction is selecting and providing parameters for additional data processing beyond the Fourier transforms.
Develops the software and interacts with the system for proper functioning. Requires remote access for troubleshooting away from the EVLA and during non-working hours.
A few authorized individuals allowed access to parts of the system usually considered restricted.
Individual with unrestricted access to all aspects of the system. Has authority to grant and revoke privileges on a per-user basis.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: Distributed memory cluster of identical processors running standard, widely-used operating systems (source code availability very important). Communication via high-speed networks for Correlator data input (minimum 1.6 GB/sec aggregate), e2e output (minimum 25 MB/sec), internal processor interconnects, and M&C. Backend System includes the networks connecting internal processors, the Correlator, and the e2e.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The system is a critical component in the astronomical data path — if unavailable, incoming data will be lost.
Throughput constrained by computational performance of available hardware and practical ability to configure/maintain large numbers of processors.
Throughput constrained by efficiency of supporting software, data processing code, and ability to tune for maximum performance.
Real-time operations critically dependent on network performance levels.
All operations shall be reversible — raw uncorrected input data must be recoverable from output.
Input data stream assumes lag frames arrive from Correlator with lag set sizes always a power of two.
Lag frames for the same baseline are directed to the same Backend processor.
BE processes shall not violate archive data requirements.
userDocumentation: Complete and comprehensible hardware systems specifications and configuration information.
Well-documented software code in familiar languages (preferably not more than two).
assumptionsAndDependencies: Correlator delivers suitably formatted UDP/IP network data packets to the Backend input network.
Lag frames will not necessarily arrive in lag set order.
Number of lags per lag set will always be a power of two.
All auxiliary data needed for processing and formatting will be provided by the Correlator directly or via M&C in a timely manner (much originating from Station Board CMIBs).
The e2e System will be capable of accepting output data rates and volumes generated by the Backend.
Final assembly of all visibility data from different baselines is performed by the e2e system.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The BE system will not directly produce any user interface screens. All use is indirect via the Monitor and Control System.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: Distributed memory cluster of identical processors. High-speed network interfaces for: Correlator input (minimum 1.6 GB/sec aggregate, UDP/IP packets containing lag frames), e2e output (minimum 25 MB/sec), internal processor interconnects, and M&C communication. Sufficient memory with sufficient access speeds (amounts TBD). Excess storage (memory/disk) for Correlator bursting demands and outage recovery.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: Interface with Monitor and Control (M&C) System for receiving auxiliary data, state counts, observational mode parameters, filter/windowing parameters, meta-data, operational status/control, and error/warning reporting. Output formatted as AIPS++ Measurement Sets for the e2e System. Standard, widely-used OS with source code availability preferred.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: Correlator to Backend: high-speed network carrying LTA or Speed Dump Lag Frames in UDP/IP packets. Backend to e2e: high-speed network carrying formatted AIPS++ Measurement Sets. Backend to/from M&C: network for control commands, auxiliary data, status reports, error/warning messages. All internal processor-to-processor communication over cluster network.

systemFeatures.name: Data Reception and Assembly
systemFeatures.description: Receives correlator lag frame data via high-speed network, verifies successful receipt, manages input buffering, and assembles lag frames into complete, properly ordered time series (lag sets) up to 262,144 values.
functionalRequirements: 3.2.2.1: BE shall receive incoming data packets from the Correlator to Backend network interface.

3.2.2.2: BE shall verify successful receipt of incoming data including checking for receive errors, determining all expected data was received, accumulating error statistics, comparing against tolerances, and reporting out-of-tolerance conditions.
3.2.2.3: BE shall store input data records in a memory buffer, track buffer locations until processing complete, and report buffer overflow conditions.
3.2.2.5: BE shall assemble received input data into continuous time series (lag sets).
3.2.2.6: BE shall ensure time series data is correctly ordered and contains valid values, compare against tolerances and report out-of-tolerance conditions.
3.2.1.5: BE shall handle lag frames of less than 128 values.
3.2.1.6: BE shall handle lag sets up to a maximum size of 262,144 values.

systemFeatures.name: Signal Processing Pipeline
systemFeatures.description: Core data processing pipeline performing normalization, quantization correction, windowing, Fourier Transform, and user-selectable time/frequency domain processing. All processes are chainable and repeatable.
functionalRequirements: 3.2.2.7: BE shall replace all invalid data with zero values.
3.2.2.8: BE shall keep track of data invalids.
3.2.2.9: BE shall apply normalizations based on reported data invalid counts.
3.2.2.10: BE shall apply coarse quantization correction (VanVleck correction) based on state count and/or quantizer power measurement data.
3.2.2.11: BE shall make time stamp adjustments as required by observational mode and correlator output parameters (recirculation).
3.2.2.12: BE shall perform windowing operations prior and subsequent to Fourier Transform for narrow band RFI mitigation; post-FT windowing applied as convolution.
3.2.2.13: BE shall apply user-selected time domain processes; chainable and repeatable.
3.2.2.14: BE shall perform power-of-two complex-to-complex FFT retaining all positive and negative frequencies; must accept output of any time domain process.
3.2.2.15: BE shall apply user-selected frequency domain processes; chainable and repeatable.
3.2.2.16: BE shall sum frequency domain spectral results; integration duration controlled by observational mode parameter from M&C; track number of samples/dumps integrated per channel.

systemFeatures.name: Data Output and Formatting
systemFeatures.description: Combines finished spectra with metadata and auxiliary data into AIPS++ Measurement Set format, manages output buffering with disk backup, and delivers to the e2e System with verified transfer.
functionalRequirements: 3.2.2.17: BE shall combine finished spectra with meta- and auxiliary data to form AIPS++ Measurement Set output.
3.2.2.18: BE shall store formatted output in memory buffer with backup disk buffering; store data until successful transfer to e2e; report errors and buffer overflow conditions.
3.2.2.19: BE shall send output data to the e2e System.
3.2.2.20: BE shall verify all sent data was successfully received; report all errors.
3.1.3.1: BE shall deliver formatted final results to the e2e System producing all data needed for archiving and further processing.
3.2.2.33: All processes shall be reversible; raw unconverted input always recoverable from output.

systemFeatures.name: System Monitoring and Recovery
systemFeatures.description: Comprehensive self-monitoring of I/O performance, compute performance, computation errors, processes, processors, and networks with automatic detection, reporting, and corrective action capabilities.
functionalRequirements: 3.2.2.21: BE shall monitor data transfer rates from Correlator and to e2e; accumulate statistics and compare against tolerances.
3.2.2.22: BE shall monitor overall data processing rate; compare against tolerances and report out-of-tolerance conditions.
3.2.2.23: BE shall trap, flag, and repair infs, NaNs, underflows, overflows, and other computation errors; accumulate statistics and compare against tolerances.
3.2.2.24: BE shall periodically or upon request check PIDs and assure all started tasks are alive and running; report missing, stopped, defunct processes.
3.2.2.25: BE shall periodically or upon request check Backend processors and assure all needed processors are alive and responding.
3.2.2.26: BE shall periodically or upon request check all Backend networks and assure all connections are intact and functioning.

3.4.1: System shall detect, report on, and automatically remedy processor hardware failure, OS hangs/crashes, performance below spec, error rates above spec, internal/external communications failures.

systemFeatures.name: Cluster Management and Control
systemFeatures.description: Process and processor lifecycle management including starting/stopping processes, priority adjustment, processor rebooting, network rebooting, and workload redistribution across the cluster.
functionalRequirements: 3.2.2.27: BE shall initiate processing tasks on any Backend processor.
3.2.2.28: BE shall signal a kill for any Backend process.
3.2.2.29: BE shall alter priority of any BE task.
3.2.2.30: BE shall initiate reboot of any Backend physical processor.
3.2.2.31: BE shall initiate reboot of any internal network.
3.2.2.32: BE shall redistribute internal workload among processors (offloading), possibly involving change of Correlator destination IP addresses.
3.2.2.4: BE shall respond to incoming correlator mode changes, user optional processing changes, and other external inputs affecting the pipeline.

performanceRequirements: 3.3.2.1: BE shall accept aggregate data input from Correlator of minimum 1.6 GB/sec (initial deployment, increased over time).
3.3.2.2: BE shall deliver output to e2e of minimum 25 MB/sec including resends and recovery transfers.
3.3.2.3: Total processor capability sufficient to accomplish all processing without loss or delay on I/O streams.
3.3.2.4: Sufficient memory with sufficient access speeds (amount TBD).
3.3.2.5: Sufficient excess storage for short-duration Correlator bursting demands plus standby reserve for reliability and outage recovery.
3.3.1.1: Backend shall maintain input data fidelity and dynamic range across all processing, manipulation, and I/O functions.
3.7.4: System ultimately scalable to handle up to 2 GB/sec per Correlator output channel in real-time.
safetyRequirements: 3.3.1.2: System shall flag and mark corrupted data segments and proceed without interruption or effect on other data (partial data, zero data, underflows, overflows, infinities, NaNs).
securityRequirements: 3.8.1: All users shall login using unique identification (e.g., username and password).
3.8.2: All login attempts shall be done securely (e.g., encrypted passwords).
3.8.3: Administrator shall have unrestricted access to all aspects of the system.
3.8.4: Each user shall have a set of system access properties defining privileges.
3.8.5-3.8.7: Administrator can create/add, remove, and edit user access properties.
3.8.8: Administrator can block all or selective user access (active sessions auto-logged off).
softwareQualityAttributes: 3.4.2: Software shall perform without total system restart between array maintenance windows.
3.4.3: Hardware shall perform indefinitely without complete loss of service except total power failure.
3.4.4: System shall respond loss-lessly to I/O and processing changes from Correlator mode changes.
3.4.5: System shall continue loss-less operation during temporary e2e unavailability (duration TBD).
3.4.6: System shall complete processing of onboard data and deliver results during Correlator loss, maintaining availability for immediate resumption.
3.4.7: System shall continue operating during M&C absence until first encounter of unavailable critical auxiliary data, then cache predetermined amount (TBD) of correlator data.
3.4.8: System shall sit at idle in standby mode and resume with minimal delay (amount TBD).

3.5.1-3.5.4: All hardware accessible for maintenance; all source code available on systems; all modules debuggable; all processes killable, restartable, debuggable, testable without affecting operations.
3.6.1-3.6.2: Tools without source code require complete diagnostics and support; OS requires source code or sufficient diagnostics.
3.7.1-3.7.3: Hardware scalable, transparent to software, and seamless at interfaces.
businessRules: 3.9.1: System shall continue operations (not necessarily full capacity) on unaffected resources during partial shutdowns for maintenance/repair/upgrade.
3.9.2: System shall handle non-real-time operations transparently as if real-time (test mode).
3.9.3: Modular design principles employed to maximum extent; maximal use of hot-swappable devices.
3.10.1-3.10.2: Complete hardware specs available; software well-documented, easily readable, in familiar languages (preferably ≤ 2).
3.3.3.1-3.3.3.5: All software (applications, management, I/O) shall optimize use of language, compiler, and system resources.
3.3.3.4: All data processing functions shall be chainable and repeatable where computationally sensible.
3.3.3.5: OS, middleware, and languages shall follow industry standards, be commonly available and widely used.

otherRequirements: The fundamental unit of output is the minimum sub-band cross-power spectrum produced by the Correlator — no stitching operations combining spectra from different sub-bands will be performed.
Visibility data from different baselines may be processed by different BE processors; final assembly is expected to be performed by the e2e system.
All users' access attempts shall be logged for monitoring past system access.

glossary.definition: Expanded Very Large Array — the upgrade project for the VLA radio telescope array in Socorro, New Mexico.
Wide-band Interferometric Digital Architecture Receiver — the EVLA correlator.
A complete, properly ordered series of lag values (up to 262,144) ready for Fourier Transform.
A data packet from the Correlator containing up to 128 lag values; multiple frames may be needed to form a complete lag set.
End-to-End System — the archive system that receives, stores, and further processes the astronomical data.
Monitor and Control System — external system providing operational control, auxiliary data, and status monitoring.
Astronomical Information Processing System — the standard data format (Measurement Sets) used for radio astronomy data output.
Correlator Monitor Interface Board — hardware on the Correlator Station Boards that provides auxiliary data.
Radio Frequency Interference — unwanted radio signals that must be mitigated during processing.
Coarse quantization correction applied based on state count and/or quantizer power measurement data.
Not a Number — a floating point bit string that does not translate into a valid floating point number.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This flowchart depicts the main data processing pipeline from Correlator input through signal processing to e2e output.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
    A["Correlator Lag Frames\nUDP/IP @ 1.6 GB/s"] --> B["Data Receive & Verify"]
    B --> C["Input Data Management\nMemory Buffer"]
    C --> D["Time Series Assembly\nLag Set Construction"]
    D --> E["Data Integrity Verification"]
    E --> F["Normalization &\nVanVleck Correction"]
    F --> G["Time Stamp Adjustment"]
    G --> H["Pre-FFT Windowing\nRFI Mitigation"]
    H --> I["Optional Time Domain\nProcessing (chainable)"]
    I --> J["FFT: Complex-to-Complex\nPower-of-2"]
    J --> K["Post-FFT Windowing\n(convolution)"]
    K --> L["Optional Frequency Domain\nProcessing (chainable)"]
    L --> M["Spectral Integration\n(Summation)"]
    M --> N["Output Formatting\nAIPS++ Measurement Sets"]
    N --> O["Output Data Management\nMemory + Disk Buffer"]
    O --> P["Data Send & Verify\nto e2e @ 25 MB/s"]
flowchartDiagram.caption: EVLA Correlator Backend Data Processing Pipeline — from lag frame reception through FFT processing to formatted archive output.

tbdList: Memory amount and access speed requirements (3.3.2.4).
Excess storage level for Correlator bursting demands (3.3.2.5).
Standby reserve amount for reliability and outage recovery (3.3.2.5).
Duration of temporary e2e loss that system must tolerate (3.4.5).
Amount of correlator data to cache after M&C loss (3.4.7).
Delay tolerance for standby mode resume (3.4.8).
No optional time domain processes have yet been proposed (3.2.2.13).
No frequency domain processes have yet been proposed (3.2.2.15).
